title: Global Mafia Alliance Network
summary: This community is a sprawling global network of Italian mafias (including 'Ndrangheta, Sicilian Mafia, Camorra, Sacra Corona Unita, and others) tightly interconnected with Balkan, Eastern European, and other international organized crime groups. The cooperation is primarily driven by drug trafficking—especially cocaine and heroin—along with arms smuggling, money laundering, and human trafficking. Italian organizations serve as central hubs, linking to Serbian, Albanian, Russian, and Turkish groups, while also maintaining ties with Corsican, Lebanese, and Nigerian criminal networks. The community exhibits a high degree of cross-border collaboration, with alliances often formalized through shared illicit markets and joint ventures in Europe and beyond.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrangheta
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian criminal organization of mafia type originating in Calabria, recognized as a unitary criminal organization with a collegial summit. It is the only mafia present on all five continents, with a global turnover of 150 billion euros according to a 2022 DIA investigation. Active in 42 nations with 400 clans, mainly in Calabria. Its main activity is drug trafficking, followed by public contracts, vote buying, extortion, usury, arms trafficking, gambling, and toxic waste disposal.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sicilian Mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: Cosa Nostra is a Sicilian mafia-type criminal organization based in Italy, primarily in Sicily, with international branches. It originated in the 19th century and became known for its violent activities and influence. The Italian state intensified countermeasures in the 1980s, leading to the deaths of prominent anti-mafia magistrates. It remains active today.

top_orgs_detail.name: Camorra
top_orgs_detail.description: The Camorra is an Italian Mafia-type criminal organization originating in Campania, dating to the 18th century. It is structured into individual clans, each led by a boss, and is involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, counterfeiting, and money laundering. The organization has a strong presence in other European countries, particularly Spain, and maintains close ties with South American drug cartels. Currently, the most powerful groups are the Mazzarella clan and the Secondigliano Alliance.

top_orgs_detail.name: American Mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The American Mafia is a highly organized Italian American criminal society that emerged in the late 19th century in impoverished Italian immigrant neighborhoods in New York City and other major U.S. cities. It is structured into families and engages in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. The organization cooperates with Italian organized crime groups such as the Sicilian Mafia, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta. It remains active primarily in the Northeastern and Midwestern United States, with some operations in Canada.

top_orgs_detail.name: Serbian mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: Various criminal organizations based in Serbia or composed of ethnic Serbs, involved in smuggling, arms trafficking, drug trafficking, human trafficking, assassinations, heists, and money laundering. Emerged in the 1970s with support from Yugoslav security services. Became powerful in Europe by the 1980s, and expanded during the Yugoslav Wars. Includes major groups like 'Group America' and the 'Pink Panthers'.

top_orgs_detail.name: Albanian mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Albanian mafia is a term for various criminal organizations based in Albania, Kosovo, and other Albanian-majority territories, or composed of ethnic Albanians. Known for ferocity and violence, they are active in the United States and European Union, involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and human trafficking. Their roots lie in traditional family clans (Fis) and the Kanun code, and they expanded internationally after the fall of communism and the Kosovo War.

top_orgs_detail.name: Russian mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: A collective of various organized crime groups originating and operating in Russia, also known as bratva. It emerged and expanded significantly after the collapse of the Soviet Union in the 1990s, becoming a highly organized and globalized criminal network. The Russian mafia engages in a wide range of criminal activities including human trafficking, drug trafficking, and money laundering. It operates internationally, with presence in post-Soviet states, the European Union, Israel, the United States, and Australia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sacra corona unita
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian mafia-type criminal organization based in Apulia, primarily active in the Salento region. Founded in the early 1980s as a response to Camorra expansion, with ties to 'Ndrangheta. Reached its peak in the late 1980s and early 1990s, then weakened by state intervention. In the 2010s it adopted a strategy of infiltration into the business sector.

top_orgs_detail.name: Banda della Magliana
top_orgs_detail.description: The Banda della Magliana is a mafia-type criminal organization based in Rome, Italy, active from the late 1970s until 1999. It unified local criminal groups under a single structure, engaging in kidnappings, gambling, drug trafficking, and robberies. The group had ties to other Italian mafias, Masonic lodges, far-right terrorism, and intelligence services. It was involved in several high-profile Italian crimes and mysteries.

top_orgs_detail.name: Greek mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Greek mafia is a colloquial term for various large-scale organized crime elements originating in Greece or operated by Greeks. These groups are involved in racketeering, smuggling, money laundering, drug trafficking, kidnapping, and murder. They operate primarily in Greek urban centers like Athens, often as family-based cells, and collaborate with Albanian, Romanian, and Serbian mafia groups. Greek crime bosses are known as 'godfathers of the night' and often run illegal businesses from nightclubs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Società foggiana
top_orgs_detail.description: The Società foggiana is a mafia-type criminal cartel operating in Foggia and its province in Italy, with infiltrations in other regions. It emerged in the early 1980s as a splinter of Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata and gained recognition as an independent mafia in 1994. Known for its brutality, it lacks a single leader but is organized into family-based 'batteries' with a federal structure. It is considered one of the most violent Italian mafias.

top_orgs_detail.name: Corsican mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mafia corsa refers to criminal organizations originating from Corsica, with a tradition of banditry and violence. It operates primarily in Corsica, Marseille, and the Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur region, with extensions to North Africa, the Antilles, and South America. The groups are numerous, often small, and involved in extortion, drug trafficking (notably the French Connection heroin network), and high rates of homicide. The most prominent recent group is the Petit Bar gang of Ajaccio.

top_orgs_detail.name: Solntsevskaya Bratva
top_orgs_detail.description: Russian crime syndicate founded in the late 1980s by Sergei Mikhailov and Viktor Averin in the Solntsevo District of Moscow. It controls transport hubs and engages in racketeering, money laundering, drug trafficking, and other crimes. The group has expanded internationally and maintains alliances with various criminal organizations. It remains active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lebanese mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lebanese mafia is a colloquial term for organized crime groups originating from Lebanon. Active globally due to the Lebanese diaspora, they are involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, extortion, and money laundering. Key regions include Lebanon's Beqaa Valley, Australia, Canada, and the Triple Frontier in South America. The groups are often structured around extended crime families and have been active since the mid-20th century.

top_orgs_detail.name: Armenian mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Armenian mafia is a general term for organized criminal groups composed of ethnic Armenians, structured in clans called yekhpayrutyuns. Based in Armenia, it operates internationally in countries including Germany, France, Spain, Russia, and the United States. The groups are involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other crimes. They have been the subject of investigations by German, French, and Spanish authorities.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mala del Brenta
top_orgs_detail.description: Mala del Brenta was a mafia-type criminal organization founded in Veneto, Italy, around the 1970s, later spreading to northeastern Italy. It was heavily involved in kidnappings, robberies, drug and arms trafficking, money laundering, and murders. The group was severely weakened in the 1990s after the arrest and defection of its main boss, Felice Maniero.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan dei Casamonica
top_orgs_detail.description: A Roma mafia organization of Romani origin, based in Rome and Lazio, Italy. Formed by the Casamonica and Spada families from Abruzzo and Molise who settled in Rome in the late 1930s. The clan expanded through marriages with other Romani families and alliances with other criminal groups, notably the Banda della Magliana. It controls various illegal activities in Rome's suburbs and Ostia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Romanian mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Romanian mafia is a category of organized crime groups composed of Romanian citizens or diaspora members. Active primarily in Romania, Italy, Spain, the United Kingdom, and North America, they engage in human trafficking, drug trafficking, prostitution, cybercrime, and other illicit activities. Known for ATM skimming and major scams, they have expanded across Europe and the Americas, with some groups forming alliances with Mexican cartels. The groups are horizontally organized, with leaders called 'Nașu'.

top_orgs_detail.name: Nuova Camorra Organizzata
top_orgs_detail.description: The Nuova Camorra Organizzata (NCO) was an Italian Camorra criminal organization founded in the late 1970s by Raffaele Cutolo in Campania. It was a structured, hierarchical group that engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, and extortion, becoming one of the most powerful criminal organizations in Italy by 1981. The NCO was hostile to the Sicilian Mafia but allied with 'Ndrangheta clans. It was considered extinct by the late 1980s after many members were killed or imprisoned.

title: American Mafia and Allied Networks
summary: This community is dominated by the American Mafia, including the Five Families of New York, the Chicago Outfit, and numerous smaller families across the United States. These organizations are bound by a shared Italian-American heritage, hierarchical structure, and involvement in traditional rackets such as gambling, loansharking, extortion, and drug trafficking. Cooperation is extensive, with families forming alliances, sharing territories, and collaborating on criminal enterprises like casino skimming and labor racketeering. The community also includes allied motorcycle clubs (e.g., Pagan's, Warlocks), Irish-American gangs (e.g., Winter Hill Gang, Westies), and African-American syndicates (e.g., The Council), reflecting a multi-ethnic criminal network centered on La Cosa Nostra.
top_orgs: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction

top_orgs_detail.name: Gambino crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Gambino family is one of the Five Families of New York City and one of the most powerful mafia organizations in the United States. Originating from the Black Hand gang led by Ignazio Lupo and Giuseppe Morello in East Harlem, it evolved under bosses such as Salvatore D'Aquila, Al Mineo, and Vincent Mangano. The family was formally established as one of the Five Families by Salvatore Maranzano in 1931. It remains active in organized crime activities including illegal gambling and racketeering.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bonanno crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bonanno crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia in New York City, founded by Salvatore Maranzano in the 1890s. It has historically been the smallest and most brutal of the Five Families, with operations extending into New Jersey, Florida, California, Arizona, and Montreal. The family was involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and labor union corruption, and was known for internal conflicts such as the Banana War in the 1960s. It remains active as of the 2020s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Chicago Outfit
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Chicago, Illinois, originating in the early 1910s. Rose to power under Johnny Torrio and Al Capone during Prohibition, engaging in bootlegging, gambling, loansharking, extortion, and murder. Considered one of the most violent Mafia families in the US, with over 1,000 murders since the 1920s. Still active but declined due to law enforcement and attrition.

top_orgs_detail.name: Genovese crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Genovese crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families of New York City. Founded by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano in the 1930s, it is the oldest and largest of the Five Families, with strong influence in New Jersey and other regions. Known for its secrecy and white-collar rackets, it is considered the most powerful Mafia family in the U.S. by the FBI.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lucchese crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lucchese crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia, based in New York City and New Jersey. Founded in the early 1920s, it was originally led by Gaetano Reina and later Tommy Gagliano. Under Tommy Lucchese, the family became one of the most powerful on the Mafia Commission. It is currently active, with involvement in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and drug trafficking.

top_orgs_detail.name: Philadelphia crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, founded around 1911 by Salvatore Sabella. Operates throughout the Greater Philadelphia metropolitan area and South Jersey. Known for violence and multiple mob wars, including the reigns of bosses Angelo Bruno and Nicodemo Scarfo. Currently active with an estimated 50 made members and 100 associates as of 2004.

top_orgs_detail.name: Detroit Partnership
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Detroit, Michigan, founded around 1908 by Pietro Mirabile. It operates primarily in the Greater Detroit area, with additional territories in Ohio, Ontario, and Las Vegas. The family engaged in bootlegging, labor racketeering, heroin trafficking, and casino skimming. Membership peaked at about 100 made men in the 1960s, declining to 40-50 by 2011, but remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Colombo crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Colombo crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia in New York City. Founded in 1928 by Joe Profaci, it is the youngest of the Five Families and has a history of instability and internal wars. The family engages in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, and other criminal activities. It is currently active, primarily in the New York metropolitan area.

top_orgs_detail.name: Buffalo crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Buffalo, New York, operating throughout Western New York, Erie, Pennsylvania, and Hamilton, Ontario. Founded around 1910 by Angelo Palmeri, later led by Stefano Magaddino, a member of The Commission. Currently led by Joseph A. Todaro Jr., the family is involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and drug trafficking.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cleveland crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Cleveland, Ohio, formed around 1920 by Joseph Lonardo. It peaked with 60 made members in the 1950s and exerted influence over the Teamsters and Las Vegas casinos. After a gang war in the late 1970s and subsequent convictions, the family declined but remains active in illegal gambling and loansharking.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Angeles crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Los Angeles, California, founded around 1900 by Joseph Ardizzone. It gained power through bootlegging during Prohibition and peaked under Jack Dragna in the 1940s-1950s. The family has since declined due to RICO prosecutions and competition from other gangs, but remains active with an estimated 15-20 made members as of 2003.

top_orgs_detail.name: Patriarca crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian-American Mafia crime family operating in New England, with factions in Providence, Rhode Island and Boston, Massachusetts. Founded around 1916 by Gaspare Messina, it was led by Raymond Patriarca from 1954 to 1984, engaging in illegal gambling, loansharking, and other rackets. The family has experienced internal conflicts and leadership changes, with an estimated 30 made members as of 2024.

top_orgs_detail.name: New Orleans crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in New Orleans, Louisiana, founded around 1860 by Silvestro Carollo. Engaged in racketeering, extortion, gambling, narcotics, and murder. Reached peak influence under Carlos Marcello, but declined after his imprisonment and death in 1993. Some elements may remain active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Pittsburgh crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, formed in the late 1880s under Salvatore Catanzaro. It rose to prominence during Prohibition and was led for decades by Sebastian LaRocca. The family engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, and labor union corruption. It became defunct after the death of its last known made member in 2021.

top_orgs_detail.name: DeCavalcante crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The DeCavalcante crime family is an Italian American Mafia family operating primarily in North Jersey, with ties to New York's Five Families. Founded around the 1900s by Phil Amari, it engages in racketeering, gambling, drug trafficking, and other illicit activities. The family is sometimes called the 'Sixth Family' due to its influence and connections.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bufalino crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Northeastern Pennsylvania, primarily in Scranton, Wilkes-Barre, and Pittston. Originated in the 1880s with Stefano La Torre, involved in labor racketeering in the coal and garment industries. Longest-serving boss Russell Bufalino led until his death in 1994. The family was active from around 1900 until 2008.

top_orgs_detail.name: Trafficante crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Trafficante crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Tampa, Florida, founded around 1920 by Ignacio Antinori. It was notably led by Santo Trafficante Jr., who was involved with the CIA in plots to assassinate Fidel Castro. After his death in 1987, Vincent LoScalzo took control. The family is still active, primarily in the Tampa Bay area and throughout Florida.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kansas City crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Kansas City, Missouri, formed in the early 1910s by the DiGiovanni brothers. It was led for decades by Nicholas Civella, who expanded its influence into Las Vegas casinos. The family is involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and other illegal activities, and remains active with an estimated 12 made members as of 2015.

top_orgs_detail.name: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
top_orgs_detail.description: The New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters controlling organized crime in New Jersey. It is divided into crews led by caporegimes involved in labor racketeering, illegal gambling, loansharking, and extortion. The faction has maintained a strong presence in Northern Jersey since the Prohibition era, with notable members including Guarino Moretti, Gerardo Catena, and Louis Manna. It remains active as of the 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
top_orgs_detail.description: A crew of Italian-American mobsters operating in Northern New Jersey, part of the Lucchese crime family. Active since the 1920s, it was led by Anthony Accetturo and Michael Taccetta in the 1970s-1980s. A war with New York members in the late 1980s led to defections and convictions. Currently controlled by Joseph R. Perna.
