title: Mexican Cartel Alliance Network
summary: This community is dominated by Mexican drug cartels and their allied gangs, forming a complex web of shifting alliances and rivalries. Central organizations like the Sinaloa Cartel, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gulf Cartel, and Los Zetas cooperate with numerous smaller groups, including prison gangs like Barrio Azteca and Mara Salvatrucha, as well as Colombian and Central American trafficking organizations. Cooperation often involves drug trafficking, logistical support, and armed enforcement, with alliances frequently formed to counter common enemies. The community reflects the fragmented and fluid nature of Mexican organized crime, where temporary partnerships are common in the pursuit of territorial control and drug routes.
top_orgs: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima

top_orgs_detail.name: Sinaloa Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican transnational criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and other illicit activities. Based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, it was founded in 1989 by Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán, Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, and others. Considered the most important cartel in Mexico and one of the most influential globally, it controls major drug routes into the United States and operates in multiple continents. Currently divided into factions led by Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar (Los Chapitos) and Ismael Zambada Sicairos (La Mayiza).

top_orgs_detail.name: Jalisco New Generation Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and arms smuggling, designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. and Argentina. Founded in 2007 as an armed wing called Los Mata Zetas, it became independent in 2010 and emerged publicly in 2011. Led by Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ("El Mencho") from 2009 until his death in 2026, it is considered one of Mexico's most dangerous cartels and a rival of the Sinaloa Cartel.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Zetas
top_orgs_detail.description: Los Zetas were a Mexican drug cartel founded in 1997 by Osiel Cárdenas Guillén, originally as an armed wing of the Gulf Cartel. They engaged in drug trafficking, murder, money laundering, and cybercrime, operating primarily in Tamaulipas and expanding across Mexico and internationally. The cartel was known for extreme violence and was considered a major rival to the Sinaloa Cartel. By 2018, the organization was dismantled, with factions such as the Cartel del Noreste continuing operations.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gulf Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican drug cartel based in Matamoros, Tamaulipas, founded in the 1930s by Juan Nepomuceno Guerra and Juan García Ábrego. Originally involved in liquor smuggling during Prohibition, it later became one of Mexico's oldest drug trafficking organizations. The cartel operates across multiple Mexican states and has international connections in Europe, Africa, and the Americas. It spawned splinter groups including Los Zetas and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: La Familia Michoacana
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and other illegal activities, designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department. Founded in the 1980s as a vigilante group in Michoacán, it later allied with the Gulf Cartel. In 2011, it split into two factions, with one retaining the name La Familia Michoacana and the other becoming Los Caballeros Templarios. The group was largely expelled from Michoacán and retreated to the State of Mexico and Guerrero. Its main leader, Nazario Moreno González, was killed in 2014.

top_orgs_detail.name: Juárez Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican drug cartel based in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua, founded around 1970 by Rafael Aguilar Guajardo, Pablo Acosta Villarreal, Amado Carrillo Fuentes, and Vicente Carrillo Fuentes. It controls cocaine trafficking routes into the United States and is known for extreme violence, including decapitations. Its armed wing is La Línea, and it uses Barrio Azteca gang. Currently led by Juan Pablo Ledezma.

top_orgs_detail.name: Beltrán-Leyva Organization
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican drug cartel and organized crime syndicate founded in 1996 by the five Beltrán Leyva brothers. Originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel, it became independent and controlled drug trafficking corridors, engaging in cocaine, heroin, and marijuana trafficking, as well as human smuggling, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. The cartel infiltrated Mexican government agencies and Interpol. It was declared disbanded in 2011 after the arrest of its last significant leader, though remnants continued until 2017.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tijuana Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Tijuana, Baja California, founded in 1989 by the Arellano Félix brothers after the fragmentation of the Guadalajara Cartel. It was one of the largest and most violent cartels in Mexico during the 1980s and 1990s, but has been weakened by arrests and deaths of its leaders. Since 2014, it has allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), effectively merging into the Cártel de Tijuana Nueva Generación (CTNG).

top_orgs_detail.name: Guerreros Unidos
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Morelos and Guerrero, founded in 2010 by Cleotilde Toribio Rentería after the fragmentation of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel. Engaged in drug trafficking, homicide, kidnapping, extortion, and human trafficking. Notorious for involvement in the 2014 disappearance of 43 students. Currently active and in conflict with multiple rival cartels.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Viagras
top_orgs_detail.description: Los Viagras is a Mexican criminal group based in Michoacán, founded around 2014 as a self-defense group and later evolving into a drug trafficking organization. Led by Nicolás Sierra Santana, the group is involved in methamphetamine production and trafficking, and has been in conflict with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel. They have been known for using minors as human shields and are a major source of violence in the Tierra Caliente region. In 2024, they allied with the CJNG against Cárteles Unidos.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cárteles Unidos
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization formed in 2010 in Michoacán, originally as La Resistencia, to expel Los Zetas from Michoacán and Jalisco. Composed of members from various cartels including Sinaloa, Gulf, and La Familia Michoacana. Engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and armed attacks, primarily in the Tierra Caliente region. Designated a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department in February 2025. Currently active and in conflict with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cartel del Norte del Valle
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian drug trafficking organization based in the northern Valle del Cauca region, active from the 1980s to 2008. It emerged after the fragmentation of the Cali and Medellín cartels in the mid-1990s, becoming one of the most powerful cocaine trafficking groups. Known for extreme violence and internal betrayals, it was led by Orlando Henao Montoya until his death in 1998, after which it split into factions. The organization was dismantled by 2008, though small dissident groups remain active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Knights Templar Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization formed in 2010 from remnants of La Familia Michoacana, based in Michoacán. Engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, kidnapping, extortion, and other crimes. It fought rival cartels and vigilante groups until its collapse around 2017 after the arrest of its leader and deaths of key members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Mexicles
top_orgs_detail.description: Los Mexicles is a Mexican and Mexican-American gang founded in 1987 in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua. It operates as an armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel along the US-Mexico border, engaging in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, kidnapping, and vehicle theft. The gang is composed largely of deportees and Mexican-American citizens, and is known for using Nahuatl language in US prisons to communicate secretly. It remains active, primarily in northern Mexican states and parts of Texas.

top_orgs_detail.name: La Línea
top_orgs_detail.description: La Línea is the main faction of the Juárez Cartel, originally formed as an execution unit composed of former police and military personnel trained in urban combat. Based in Ciudad Juárez, Mexico, it was founded in 2008 by Juan Pablo Ledezma. The group is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and kidnappings, and has been linked to several high-profile massacres. It has formed alliances with Barrio Azteca and the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG) to fight against the Sinaloa Cartel.

top_orgs_detail.name: Milenio Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Michoacán, founded in the 1970s by Emilio Valencia Barragán. Dedicated to drug trafficking, particularly cocaine, and involved in homicide, kidnapping, extortion, money laundering, and arms trafficking. It was considered one of the most violent and powerful cartels in Mexico, responsible for a third of cocaine consumed in the United States. The cartel was dismantled in 2009.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Guanajuato, founded in 2014 by José Antonio Yépez Ortiz. Primarily involved in oil theft, as well as drug trafficking, extortion, and other crimes. It lost much of its territory after conflicts with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and government raids, but maintains a presence in several Guanajuato towns. Its leader was captured in 2020.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mara Salvatrucha
top_orgs_detail.description: International criminal gang designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department. Originated in Los Angeles, California, and expanded to the United States, Canada, Mexico, Central America, and Europe. Known for extensive tattoos, hand signs, and extreme violence. Engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and other crimes. Declined sharply in El Salvador after 2022 under President Nayib Bukele's crackdown.

top_orgs_detail.name: Barrio Azteca
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican-American street and prison gang originally based in El Paso, Texas and Ciudad Juarez, Mexico, founded in the early 1980s by Jose 'Raulio' Rivera. It is a transnational criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, human trafficking, murder, and other crimes, with an estimated 8,000 members. The gang has an alliance with La Línea and the Juárez Cartel, and is considered one of the most violent gangs in the United States.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cártel de Tláhuac
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal group founded in 2012 by Felipe de Jesús Pérez Luna, alias El Ojos, operating primarily in Tláhuac, Iztapalapa, Milpa Alta, Xochimilco, and the State of Mexico. It emerged from splinter groups of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and engages in drug trafficking, homicide, extortion, kidnapping, vehicle theft, fuel theft, and human trafficking. The group is currently led by relatives of El Ojos, including his wife and children.

all_orgs: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima

title: Afghan-Pakistani Islamist Militant Network
summary: This community is dominated by Islamist militant organizations primarily based in Afghanistan and Pakistan, with the Taliban and Al-Qaeda as central hubs. The network is characterized by ideological alignment on establishing Islamic states and global jihad, with extensive cooperation ties including training, logistical support, and joint operations. Many groups are designated as terrorist entities and have overlapping memberships, such as the Haqqani network and Lashkar-e-Taiba. Despite occasional rivalries, such as between the Taliban and Islamic State – Khorasan Province, the community exhibits a dense web of alliances rooted in shared objectives and historical ties.

top_orgs_detail.name: Taliban
top_orgs_detail.description: The Taliban is a political and militant organization in Afghanistan, founded in 1994 by Mullah Omar. It ruled most of Afghanistan from 1996 to 2001, enforcing a strict interpretation of Sharia law, before being overthrown by a US-led invasion. After a 20-year insurgency, it recaptured Kabul in 2021 and now controls all of Afghanistan. The group is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries and is widely condemned for human rights abuses.

top_orgs_detail.name: Al-Qaeda
top_orgs_detail.description: Al-Qaeda is a pan-Islamist militant organization founded in 1988 by Osama bin Laden and other veterans of the Soviet-Afghan War. It aims to establish a global Islamic caliphate through jihad, and has conducted major attacks including the 1998 U.S. embassy bombings and the September 11 attacks. The group operates primarily in the Middle East, South Asia, and Africa, and has been designated a terrorist organization by the UN and many countries. Despite the death of bin Laden in 2011, it remains active under new leadership.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lashkar-e-Taiba
top_orgs_detail.description: Lashkar-e-Taiba is a Pakistani Islamist jihadist militant organization founded in 1985-1986 by Hafiz Saeed and others. It seeks to merge Kashmir with Pakistan and destroy India, Hinduism, and Judaism through jihad. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it is responsible for major attacks including the 2008 Mumbai attacks. The group is supported by Pakistan's ISI and operates primarily in Kashmir and globally.

top_orgs_detail.name: Islamic State – Khorasan Province
top_orgs_detail.description: Regional branch of the Islamic State active in Central and South Asia, primarily Afghanistan and Pakistan. Founded on 26 January 2015 by former Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan militants. Seeks to establish a caliphate in the historic Khorasan region. Responsible for numerous high-casualty attacks, including the 2021 Kabul airport attack and the 2024 Crocus City Hall attack. Currently led by Shahab al-Muhajir.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan
top_orgs_detail.description: Deobandi jihadist militant organization primarily operating along the Afghanistan-Pakistan border. Founded by Baitullah Mehsud in 2007, it seeks to overthrow the Pakistani government and establish an Islamic state. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and Pakistan, it has carried out numerous suicide bombings and attacks. Led by Noor Wali Mehsud since 2018, the group underwent reorganization in 2020, merging several factions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Jemaah Islamiyah
top_orgs_detail.description: Southeast Asian Islamist militant group based in Indonesia, founded in 1993 by Abu Bakar Bashir and Abdullah Sungkar. Dedicated to establishing an Islamic state in Southeast Asia, it perpetrated the 2002 Bali bombings and other attacks. Designated a terrorist group by the UN and multiple countries. The group was active until 2024.

all_orgs: High Council of the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan
Islamic Jihad Renaissance Party of Tajikistan
Muslim United Liberation Tigers of Assam

title: Canadian Biker-Gang Alliance Network
summary: This community is dominated by the Hells Angels Motorcycle Club and its extensive network of support clubs, Indigenous street gangs, and Italian-Canadian mafia families across Canada. The Hells Angels act as a central hub, forming alliances with numerous outlaw motorcycle clubs such as Bacchus, Red Devils, and Gate Keepers, as well as street gangs like the Independent Soldiers, Red Scorpions, and Wolfpack Alliance. Cooperation primarily involves drug trafficking, with the Hells Angels supplying cocaine and other narcotics to Indigenous gangs like the Manitoba Warriors and Redd Alert, who distribute on their behalf. The community also includes Italian-Canadian crime families like the Rizzuto and Musitano, who partner with the Hells Angels and the West End Gang in the Consortium, a joint drug trafficking operation. Overall, this network is characterized by a hierarchical structure with the Hells Angels at the top, leveraging support clubs and Indigenous gangs for street-level operations and violence.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hells Angels Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Hells Angels Motorcycle Club (HAMC) is an international outlaw motorcycle club whose members are alleged by law enforcement to engage in organized crime including drug trafficking, gunrunning, extortion, and human trafficking. Founded in 1948 in California, the club has chapters worldwide and claims to be a group of motorcycle enthusiasts. Despite ongoing criminal allegations and convictions, the club remains active and denies collective criminal involvement.

top_orgs_detail.name: Independent Soldiers
top_orgs_detail.description: The Independent Soldiers are a Canadian organized crime group based in British Columbia, formed in the early 1990s by Indo-Canadians originally known as the Sunset Boys. They engage in drug trafficking, murder, and other criminal activities across Canada and in prisons. The gang operates in cells and is known for its red and black colors. They are considered a mid-level gang in the Lower Mainland, with fluid membership and frequent leadership changes.

top_orgs_detail.name: Red Scorpions
top_orgs_detail.description: The Red Scorpions is a Canadian gang based in British Columbia, formed in 2000 by Quang Vinh Thang Le and others. It is involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other crimes, operating primarily in the Vancouver area. The gang is known for its rivalry with the United Nations gang and has been linked to high-profile gang violence. Members are identified by 'RS' tattoos.

top_orgs_detail.name: West End Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The West End Gang is a powerful criminal organization based in Montreal, Quebec, active since the early 1900s. It includes both Irish and French-Canadian members and is recognized as part of the Irish mafia, with close ties to the Winter Hill Gang in Boston. The gang is involved in small crimes, arms trafficking, and drug dealing, and was a key member of the Consortium, a drug cartel with Italian-Canadian families and the Hells Angels. Current membership is estimated at 100-150, and it remains active under the leadership of Gerald Matticks.

top_orgs_detail.name: Musitano crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Musitano 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization originating from Platì, Italy. It is active in northern Italy (Buccinasco and Bareggio), Canada (Hamilton, Ontario), and Brazil (Rio de Janeiro). The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, illegal waste disposal, and murder, including the 1997 killings of Johnny Papalia and Carmen Barillaro. Several members have been convicted, and leaders Angelo and Pat Musitano were killed in 2017 and 2020 respectively.

top_orgs_detail.name: Wolfpack Alliance
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian organized crime group founded in 2010 in British Columbia by Hells Angels member Larry Amero and others. It is a loosely allied consortium of mostly millennial-aged gangsters involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. The group distributes cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel and operates across Canada, including British Columbia, Ontario, and Quebec.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rizzuto crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian Canadian crime family based in Montreal, Quebec, founded in the 1970s by Nicolo Rizzuto. It operates primarily in southern Quebec and Ontario, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, illegal gambling, loan sharking, extortion, weapons trafficking, and murder. The FBI considers it a faction of the Bonanno crime family, while Canadian authorities view it as independent. After internal power struggles and deaths of key members, the family is currently led by Leonardo Rizzuto.

top_orgs_detail.name: Redd Alert
top_orgs_detail.description: Indigenous Canadian criminal gang operating as both a prison gang and a street gang, founded in 1999 by Robert Lee Wagner and other First Nations inmates in Alberta. Based in Western Canada, it is involved in drug trafficking, assault, murder, and other crimes. The gang maintains alliances with the Hells Angels and other organized crime groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Brothers Keepers
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian organized crime group based in British Columbia, founded in 2016 by Gavinder Singh Grewal. Engaged in drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes across Canada and internationally. Considered one of the most prominent and violent gangs in B.C. by the RCMP. Still active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Manitoba Warriors
top_orgs_detail.description: The Manitoba Warriors are an indigenous street gang based in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, founded in 1993 at Stony Mountain Institution. The gang is exclusively Indigenous Canadian and was established to rival the Indian Posse. They engage in drug trafficking, auto theft, illegal firearms activities, gambling, and support Hells Angels and Asian-based networks. Criminal Intelligence Service Canada designates them as a faction of indigenous-based organized crime.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bacchus Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1972 in Albert County, New Brunswick. It is the third largest Canadian one-percenter motorcycle club with seventeen chapters across five provinces. The club has been involved in drug trafficking and has expanded its influence through alliances and patch-overs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Indian Posse
top_orgs_detail.description: Indigenous street gang based in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, founded in 1988 by the Wolfe brothers. One of the largest street gangs in Canada, involved in drug trafficking, auto theft, firearms activities, gambling, and other crimes. Designated by Criminal Intelligence Service Canada as indigenous-based organized crime. Supports Hells Angels and Asian-based networks.

top_orgs_detail.name: Original Red Devils Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club based in Southern Ontario, founded in Hamilton in 1948. It operated three chapters in Ontario and was known for staying small and maintaining respect among other clubs. The club patched over to Bacchus Motorcycle Club in 2014. It was listed as an Outlaw Motorcycle Gang by Criminal Intelligence Service Canada.

top_orgs_detail.name: Native Syndicate
top_orgs_detail.description: The Native Syndicate is a Canadian indigenous street gang, part of the broader phenomenon of Indigenous-based organized crime (IBOC). It is primarily active in the prairie provinces of Canada, where high concentrations of indigenous people reside. The gang is involved in various criminal activities and has been noted as one of the largest groups in federal prisons.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan was a Sicilian Mafia clan based in Siculiana, Sicily, founded in the 1950s. It played a central role in international drug trafficking and money laundering for Cosa Nostra during the 1980s and 1990s, with operations extending to Canada and Venezuela. The clan was described by Italian prosecutors as an 'international holding' and was known for its tight family bonds.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bacon Brothers
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bacon brothers are a trio of gangsters from Abbotsford, British Columbia, involved in firearms and drug trafficking, and implicated in multiple homicides in the Fraser Valley and Greater Vancouver area. The group consists of Jonathan, Jarrod, and Jamie Bacon. Jonathan was murdered in 2011. They are associated with the United Nations gang.

top_orgs_detail.name: Iron Horsemen Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: American outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1965 in Cincinnati, Ohio. Involved in drug trafficking, assault, and other crimes. Considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement. Active in multiple states including Maryland, Kentucky, Maine, and Ohio.

all_orgs: Blood In, Blood Out gang (Kang Crime Family)
Hells Angels MC South Africa chapters

title: Outlaw Motorcycle Club Alliances
summary: This community is dominated by outlaw motorcycle clubs (OMCs) from North America, Europe, and Australia, centered around the Outlaws and Bandidos as key hubs. These clubs form a dense network of support clubs, alliances, and joint criminal operations, primarily in drug trafficking, weapons trafficking, and violent conflicts against rivals like the Hells Angels. Many smaller clubs act as support or recruitment pipelines for larger ones, such as the Black Pistons for the Outlaws and the Chicanos for the Bandidos. The community also includes white supremacist groups like the Aryan Nations, which provide weapons and ideological ties, and Australian clubs like the Rebels and Gypsy Jokers, which cooperate across continents. Overall, the community is bound by mutual defense, shared criminal enterprises, and a common opposition to the Hells Angels.

top_orgs_detail.name: Outlaws Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Outlaws Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw motorcycle gang founded in 1935 in McCook, Illinois, United States. It is involved in organized crime activities such as drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and violent acts against rival clubs. With over 5,000 members across 441 chapters in 43 countries, it is considered a criminal organization by law enforcement agencies worldwide.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bandidos Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bandidos Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle gang designated by the U.S. Department of Justice, involved in drug trafficking, weapons trafficking, murder, and other serious crimes. Active primarily in the Northwestern, Southeastern, Southwestern, and West Central regions of the United States, with an estimated 800 to 1,000 members across 16 states. The club has been supplied with drugs by Los Zetas and has associated with the Outlaws, while rivaling groups such as the Hells Angels and Mongols.

top_orgs_detail.name: Loners Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada in 1979 by Frank Lenti and Gennaro Raso. Has chapters in Canada, Italy, the United States, and other countries. Known for its Italian-Canadian membership and involvement in organized crime. Still active as of 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: Satan's Choice Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1965 in Oshawa, Ontario. At its peak in 1970, it had over 400 members, making it the second largest outlaw motorcycle club in the world. Involved in drug trafficking, prostitution, theft, assault, and murder. The club disbanded in 2000, was briefly reactivated in 2017, and reactivated again in 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rock Machine Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Montreal, Canada in 1986 by Salvatore Cazzetta and his brother Giovanni. It competed with the Hells Angels for control of the Quebec drug trade, leading to the Quebec Biker War (1994-2002) with over 160 deaths. The club briefly became a chapter of the Bandidos from 2000-2001, but was refounded in 2008. As of 2022, it has over 120 chapters worldwide.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rebels Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Rebels Motorcycle Club is an Australian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1969 by Clint Jacks in Brisbane, Queensland. At its peak, it was the largest motorcycle club in Australia with over 1,000 members. The club is considered a criminal organization by Australian authorities, involved in drug trafficking, arms dealing, and other crimes, though it claims to be a group of motorcycle enthusiasts.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club is a one-percenter motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California on April 1, 1956. It has expanded internationally, with significant presence in Australia, the United States, Germany, and Norway. Known for criminal activities including drug trafficking, homicide, and arson, the club is considered one of Australia's most violent bikie gangs. It gained notoriety for the 2001 car bomb murder of former detective Don Hancock in Western Australia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Brother Speed Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: American outlaw motorcycle club formed in Boise, Idaho in 1969 by a group of high school friends. Active in Idaho, Washington, and Oregon, it has been involved in drug trafficking, weapons offenses, and alliances with other outlaw clubs. The club's colors are black and gold, and its center patch features a winged skull.

top_orgs_detail.name: Aryan Nations
top_orgs_detail.description: Aryan Nations was a North American neo-Nazi organization founded by Richard Girnt Butler in the 1970s, based in Idaho. It adhered to Christian Identity doctrine, a white supremacist interpretation of Christianity. The group organized yearly gatherings called the 'Aryan Nations World Congress' and was classified as a terrorist threat by the FBI. After Butler's death in 2004, the organization splintered into several groups, some still using the name.

top_orgs_detail.name: Coffin Cheaters
top_orgs_detail.description: International outlaw motorcycle club formed in Perth, Australia in 1970. Expanded internationally by patching over Norwegian gangs. Known for involvement in violent conflicts, including the 1989 expulsion of the Mongrel Mob from Perth. Still active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Diablos Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California in 1961 by John J. 'Cadillac Jack' Baltas. The club has chapters across the United States, with a significant presence in New England. Membership is by invitation only and restricted to white males. Law enforcement considers it a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang.

top_orgs_detail.name: Grim Reapers Motorcycle Club (Canada)
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1967 in Calgary, Alberta. Active from the 1960s to 1997, it became a dominant club in Alberta before being patched over by the Hells Angels in 1997. Involved in drug trafficking, murder, assault, and extortion.

top_orgs_detail.name: Chosen Few Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Chosen Few Motorcycle Club is a mixed-race 1%er outlaw motorcycle club founded in Los Angeles around 1959 by Lionel Ricks and others. It has chapters across the United States and internationally in the Philippines. The club is known for being the first mixed-race outlaw motorcycle club.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kings Crew Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Western Canada, founded in Calgary, Alberta in 1977. It became one of the dominant clubs in Alberta, participating in the Alberta Biker Conflict. The club engaged in drug trafficking, murder, assault, and extortion. It eventually joined the Hells Angels in the late 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Brothers for Life
top_orgs_detail.description: Middle Eastern criminal organisation based in South-Western Sydney, Australia, founded by Bassam Hamzy in 2007. Involved in drug trafficking and violent gang conflicts, including internal disputes and wars with the Alameddine crime network. Active in Sydney and Melbourne.

top_orgs_detail.name: Annihilators Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian outlaw biker club and organized crime group active from 1988 to 1999, based in Chatham, Ontario. Founded by Wayne Kellestine, the club engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, armed robbery, murder, assault, and kidnapping. It was allied with the Outlaws MC and rivaled the Hells Angels.

top_orgs_detail.name: X-Team
top_orgs_detail.description: X-Team is a gang associated with the Bandidos Motorcycle Club, founded in Denmark in 1997 following the Nordic Biker War. Members are not required to own a motorcycle and act as a street gang recruiting young criminals to provide reinforcements for the Bandidos. They carry out crimes such as debt collection and drug dealing on behalf of the club. The gang had approximately 350 members at its peak in 2002 and operated in multiple countries, but the Danish branch was eventually disbanded.

title: Southern California Gang Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered on the Mexican Mafia and its Sureño coalition, which unites dozens of predominantly Mexican-American street gangs across Southern California. The Mexican Mafia exerts control over Sureño gangs like Florencia 13, 38th Street Gang, and Venice 13, directing their drug trafficking, extortion, and violent activities. White supremacist prison gangs such as the Aryan Brotherhood and Nazi Lowriders are allied with the Mexican Mafia, forming a cross-racial partnership for mutual benefit. Outlaw motorcycle clubs like the Mongols and Vagos also cooperate with these gangs, while smaller groups like Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family connect through drug supply and money laundering. Overall, the community operates as a loose but powerful criminal network bound by shared territory, prison affiliations, and economic interests.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sureños
top_orgs_detail.description: Sureños are loosely affiliated gangs that pay tribute to the Mexican Mafia while in U.S. correctional facilities. Founded in 1967 in Southern California, they are primarily Mexican American and engage in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other crimes. They have a long-standing rivalry with Norteños and Nuestra Familia. The gang operates in 35 U.S. states and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mexican Mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mexican Mafia, also known as La eMe, is a powerful criminal organization based in the United States, primarily composed of individuals of Mexican origin. It was founded on November 15, 1957, by Luis "Huero Buff" Flores and 13 other Chicano gang members at the Deuel Vocational Institution in Tracy, California. The organization is one of the oldest and most powerful prison gangs in the U.S., controlling illegal activities within the prison system and on the streets. It operates mainly in California and other states, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder.

top_orgs_detail.name: Aryan Brotherhood
top_orgs_detail.description: The Aryan Brotherhood is a neo-Nazi prison gang and organized crime syndicate based in the United States, founded in 1964 at San Quentin State Prison. It has an estimated 15,000–20,000 members both inside and outside prisons, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, murder-for-hire, and other criminal activities. The gang is whites-only and operates under a hierarchical structure with a twelve-man council and a three-man commission. It is considered one of the oldest and most notorious racist prison gangs in the U.S.

top_orgs_detail.name: Nazi Lowriders
top_orgs_detail.description: Neo-Nazi, white supremacist organized crime syndicate and prison/street gang based in Southern California. Originated in the California Youth Authority in the 1970s, created by Aryan Brotherhood members. Known for extreme violence and involvement in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. Allied with Aryan Brotherhood, Mexican Mafia, and other gangs. Active both in and outside prison.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mongols Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: International outlaw motorcycle club formed in Montebello, California in 1969 by a group of Hispanic bikers, many of whom were Vietnam veterans. Designated an organized crime group by the U.S. Department of Justice, Australian Federal Police, and Europol. The club has approximately 5,000 full-patched members across 19 U.S. states and 29 countries, making it the third-largest outlaw biker club globally.

top_orgs_detail.name: Venice 13
top_orgs_detail.description: Venice 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in the Oakwood neighborhood of Venice, Los Angeles, California. Founded in the 1920s or 1930s, it is one of the earliest gangs in the city and is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia. The gang is known for its long-running rivalry with the Culver City Boys 13 and has been involved in drug trafficking and violent conflicts. It remains active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Florencia 13
top_orgs_detail.description: American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles, California, composed mainly of Mexican Americans. Named after the Florence area of Los Angeles County and controlled by the Mexican Mafia. Involved in drug trafficking, murder, assault, robbery, and extortion. Active since the 1930s with an estimated 2,000-3,000+ members.

top_orgs_detail.name: OVS (gang)
top_orgs_detail.description: OVS is a Mexican American (Chicano) gang based in Ontario, California, founded in the early 1950s. It consists of sub-cliques including the Black Angels and Jr Black Angels, and has deep ties with the Mexican Mafia. The gang is involved in murder, drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other criminal activities, with an estimated 450 members as of 2010.

top_orgs_detail.name: 38th Street Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The 38th Street Gang is an American criminal street gang in Los Angeles, California, composed mainly of Mexican-Americans. Founded in the 1920s, it is one of the oldest street gangs in Los Angeles and is known for its involvement in the Sleepy Lagoon murder trial and Zoot Suit Riots. The gang engages in drug trafficking, assault, robbery, and other criminal activities, and is controlled by the Mexican Mafia. It remains active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ku Klux Klan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ku Klux Klan is an American white supremacist and far-right hate group that has operated in three major iterations since the Reconstruction era. It uses violence and intimidation to target African Americans, Jews, Catholics, and immigrants, and is considered one of the earliest terrorist groups in the United States. The first Klan was founded in 1865 by former Confederate soldiers, the second in 1915, and a third wave emerged in the mid-20th century. Despite declines, affiliated groups remain active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Temple Street gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Street gang based in downtown Los Angeles, founded in 1923 by Filipino and Mexican youths. Involved in murders, assaults, burglaries, drug trafficking, and gun trafficking. Colors are blue and black. Active in Central Los Angeles and the Philippines.

top_orgs_detail.name: 18th Street Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Multi-ethnic street gang based in Los Angeles, California, founded in the 1960s by Mexican-American and immigrant youths rejected by other gangs. One of the largest street gangs in Los Angeles with 30,000–50,000 members across the US, Mexico, and Central America. Engages in drug trafficking, prostitution, extortion, kidnapping, robbery, assault, and murder. Designated as a terrorist group by the US, El Salvador, and Guatemala.

top_orgs_detail.name: Vineland Boys
top_orgs_detail.description: Hispanic American street gang based in the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles, founded in January 1988 by former 18th Street gang members. Named after Vineland Avenue, the gang has about 300 members and is part of the Sureño coalition under the Mexican Mafia. It has alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family, and ties to the Sinaloa Cartel. The gang was involved in the 2003 murder of Burbank Police Officer Matthew Pavelka, leading to a large-scale crackdown.

top_orgs_detail.name: Straight Satans Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: A one-percenter outlaw motorcycle gang based in Southern California, founded in July 1959 in Santa Monica by three bikers known as Father Bill, Little Jerry, and Glick. The club remained localized without chapters beyond Los Angeles and gained notoriety for its association with the Manson Family during the late 1960s. It was implicated in criminal incidents including a sexual assault in 1972.

top_orgs_detail.name: White Fence
top_orgs_detail.description: White Fence is a predominantly Mexican American street gang based in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles. It claims origins as early as 1900 but emerged in the 1910s as a sports team associated with La Purissima Church. One of the oldest gangs in Los Angeles, it is known for its long-standing rivalry with El Hoyo Maravilla and its early use of firearms. The gang remains active and has expanded its territory to multiple U.S. cities and Central America.

top_orgs_detail.name: Avenues
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican/Mexican American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles County, California. Originally a social club founded by the Flores brothers in the 1940s, it evolved into a violent drug-trafficking organization. The gang is known for hate crimes against African-Americans and is under the control of the Mexican Mafia in prison. It remains active with an estimated 800 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Armenian Power
top_orgs_detail.description: Armenian-American criminal organization and street gang based in Los Angeles County, California, founded in 1989 by Armen Petrosyan. Involved in drug trafficking, murder, fraud, identity theft, illegal gambling, kidnapping, racketeering, robbery, and extortion. Has around 200-250 members and hundreds of associates, with ties to the Mexican Mafia and Russian organized crime.

top_orgs_detail.name: Forming Kaos
top_orgs_detail.description: Criminal street gang based in Costa Mesa, California, founded in the early 1990s as a party/tagger crew before evolving into a street gang. Composed primarily of Mexican-American members, it is affiliated with the Sureños and has ties to the Mexican Mafia. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, firearms trafficking, and violent crime. It remains active as of the present.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clans Alliance
summary: This community is dominated by powerful 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, primarily based in the provinces of Vibo Valentia and Reggio Calabria. The core members—Mancuso, Piromalli, Pesce, and Bellocco—are among the most influential mafia families in Europe, controlling international cocaine trafficking, public contracts, and money laundering. Cooperation ties reveal a dense network of alliances, feuds, and joint criminal operations, with clans supporting each other in drug trafficking, contract rigging, and local power struggles. The community also includes smaller allied clans like Albanese, Alvaro, and Molè, as well as connections to Camorra groups and Albanian criminal networks, reflecting a highly interconnected and hierarchical structure centered on the Gioia Tauro plain.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Mancuso
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mancuso are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Limbadi and Nicotera, in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria. Considered the most influential clan in the province, they are involved in international cocaine trafficking, extortion, usury, public works contracts, and money laundering. They have alliances with other 'Ndrangheta clans, Cosa Nostra, Colombian FARC, and AUC, and operate in northern Italy, central Italy, and abroad, notably in Togo. Described as the financially most powerful clan in Europe.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Piromalli
top_orgs_detail.description: The Piromalli are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Gioia Tauro, Calabria. According to the DIA, they are the largest and most influential crime syndicate in Western Europe, with over 400 allied families and thousands of affiliates. They have been present for over a century and are among the founders of the 'Ndrangheta. They operate in various Italian regions and have international connections in the United States, Romania, and South America.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Pesce
top_orgs_detail.description: The Pesce 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rosarno, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1950s, it engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and control of commercial businesses in the Gioia Tauro plain. The clan has been involved in port activities and public contracts, and its historical boss Giuseppe Pesce transformed it into an entrepreneurial organization. Currently led by Francesco Pesce after the arrests of Antonino and Salvatore Pesce, the clan remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Albanese
top_orgs_detail.description: The Albanese are a powerful 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cittanova and Laureana di Borrello, Calabria, Italy. They have branches in Lombardy, Tuscany, Lazio, and Liguria, and are allied with the Raso-Gullace clan. Their activities include drug trafficking, grape market control, and past kidnappings. In 2016, the Alchemia operation led to 40 arrests for mafia association, corruption, and bid-rigging.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Alvaro
top_orgs_detail.description: The Alvaro 'ndrina is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan from Sinopoli, Calabria, with about 2,000 affiliates. It operates in various Italian provinces and has international branches in Australia. The clan has been involved in feuds, kidnappings, drug trafficking, and has ties to other 'Ndrangheta families. It remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Bellocco
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bellocco 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rosarno, Calabria, Italy, founded in the 1960s by Umberto Bellocco. It is heavily involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, extortion, and control of commercial activities, particularly in the Gioia Tauro plain. The clan has a strong presence in northern Italy, especially in Varese, and has been involved in setting up the Sacra Corona Unita in Apulia. Despite arrests of its leaders, the clan remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Molè
top_orgs_detail.description: The Molè are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in the Gioia Tauro plain, historically allied with the Piromalli clan. Between the 1980s and 1990s, they managed almost all drug trafficking in the Gioia Tauro area, including locally grown cannabis and cocaine from Colombian cartels. They have been involved in numerous operations and arrests, and experienced a split from the Piromalli in the 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Crea
top_orgs_detail.description: The Crea are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rizziconi, Calabria, Italy. They have been involved in organized crime activities including drug trafficking and extortion. Key members include Teodoro Crea, arrested in 2006, and Giuseppe Crea, arrested in 2016. The clan has been targeted in multiple police operations and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Lanzino
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Lanzino is a 'Ndrangheta crime family based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Alvaro clan. The organization has been involved in extortion, drug trafficking, rigged slot machines, and public contract corruption. They have been targeted in multiple police operations, including 'Tela di ragno' and 'Acheruntia', and have infiltrated local politics.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Accorinti
top_orgs_detail.description: The Accorinti are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Briatico, Tropea, and Zungri in the province of Vibo Valentia, Italy. Their existence was legally established in August 2020 following the 'Costa pulita' operation of 2016. They have ties with the Giampà 'ndrina of Lamezia Terme and the Mancuso clan. The clan is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and other criminal activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Fiarè
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Fiarè is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Gregorio d'Ippona, province of Vibo Valentia, Italy. It was judicially recognized only in 2013. The clan is allied with the powerful Mancuso family and has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, usury, money laundering, and political infiltration. Its leader Saverio Razionale moved to Rome in 2005, replicating the criminal network there.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Pititto
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) based in Mileto, Vibo Valentia, Italy, involved in extortion and drug trafficking. It was established in the 1980s and led by Giuseppe Prostamo until his death. The clan has been involved in several operations and trials, including the Stammer operation in 2017 which seized 8 tons of cocaine. It remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Avignone
top_orgs_detail.description: The Avignone are a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Taurianova, Reggio Calabria, Italy. They are involved in drug trafficking and have operated in Genoa in the 1990s. The DIA describes them as one of the most traditional and high-profile criminal organizations in the Taurianova and Cittanova area. They are allied with the Piromalli, Zappia, and Gullace-Raso-Albanese clans.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Violi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Violi are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Sinopoli, in the plain of Gioia Tauro, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Alvaro clan. The clan has been involved in international drug trafficking and other criminal activities, with members active in Canada and connections to other 'Ndrangheta clans. Recent operations have led to arrests of its affiliates.

title: US Street and Prison Gang Alliances
summary: This community is dominated by major US street and prison gangs, primarily from Chicago and Los Angeles, forming a dense network of alliances centered around the People Nation and Bloods. The People Nation alliance, including the Almighty Vice Lord Nation, Latin Kings, and Almighty Black P. Stone Nation, provides a multiethnic coalition for drug trafficking and other crimes. The Bloods and their subsets like the Pirus are heavily interconnected with other groups, including the Crips in occasional truces. Many ties involve mutual protection, drug supply, and prison cooperation, with some links to international groups like the Mongrel Mob and Dominican criminal groups. The community reflects the fragmented but cooperative nature of US gang culture, where rivalries are set aside for strategic partnerships.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bloods
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bloods are a predominantly African-American street gang founded in Los Angeles, California in 1972. They are known for their red color, hand signs spelling 'Blood', and rivalry with the Crips. The gang consists of numerous subsets called 'sets' and is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, robbery, and pimping. They have spread across the United States and influenced similar groups in Europe.

top_orgs_detail.name: Latin Kings
top_orgs_detail.description: Hispanic street gang founded in Chicago in 1954 by Puerto Ricans and other Latin American immigrants. Initially a mutual aid group, it evolved into a criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. The gang has two main factions: King Motherland Chicago and Bloodline, with an estimated 20,000-35,000 members in the KMC faction. It operates across the United States and has international presence in Canada, Ecuador, Italy, and Spain.

top_orgs_detail.name: Crips
top_orgs_detail.description: The Crips are a primarily African-American alliance of street gangs based in Los Angeles, California, founded in 1969 by Raymond Washington and Stanley Williams. They are one of the largest and most violent street gang associations in the United States, with an estimated 30,000 to 35,000 members in 2008. Members are involved in drug trafficking, murder, robbery, and other crimes, and have a long-standing rivalry with the Bloods.

top_orgs_detail.name: Almighty Black P. Stone Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: American street gang founded in Chicago in the late 1950s by Eugene Hairston and Jeff Fort. Originally the Blackstone Rangers, it later developed an Islamic faction called El Rukn. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, murder, and robbery, and is part of the People Nation alliance. It is considered Chicago's most powerful street gang and has been linked to terrorism conspiracies.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Guerrilla Family
top_orgs_detail.description: African American black power prison gang, street gang, and political organization founded in 1966 by George Jackson, George 'Big Jake' Lewis, and W.L. Nolen at San Quentin State Prison in California. It is ideologically Marxist-Leninist and promotes black power and overthrowing the U.S. government. The group is involved in drug trafficking, burglary, and homicide, and remains active primarily in California and Maryland.

top_orgs_detail.name: Almighty Vice Lord Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: The Almighty Vice Lord Nation is the second-largest street and prison gang in Chicago, founded in 1957 by African American youths at the Illinois State Training School for Boys. It is a founding member of the People Nation alliance and engages in drug sales, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang attempted to soften its image in the 1960s as Conservative Vice Lords Inc., but continued criminal activities. It remains active with an estimated 30,000-35,000 members across 28 states.

top_orgs_detail.name: Juggalo gangs
top_orgs_detail.description: Juggalo gangs are criminal groups composed of fans of the hip hop group Insane Clown Posse, using Juggalo culture imagery. They engage in various crimes including drug trafficking, murder, and arson. Classified as a criminal street gang by the FBI and other agencies, they operate across at least 21 U.S. states. The subculture originated in Detroit in the mid-2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: People Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: The People Nation is an American alliance of street gangs based in Chicago, formed in 1978 by the Vice Lords, Black P. Stones, and Latin Kings. It is a multiethnic coalition involved in drug trafficking, robbery, money laundering, and murder. The alliance is known for using a five-pointed star and left-side identifiers, and it is a rival of the Folk Nation.

top_orgs_detail.name: Norteños
top_orgs_detail.description: Norteños are a collection of affiliated gangs in California that pay tribute to Nuestra Familia while in state and federal prisons. Founded in 1968 by Mexican American inmates, they primarily operate in Northern California and are known for their rivalry with Sureños. Their membership consists mainly of Mexican Americans, and they engage in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang uses the number 14 and the color red as symbols of affiliation.

top_orgs_detail.name: Pirus
top_orgs_detail.description: The Pirus are a subset of the Bloods gang alliance, founded in 1969 in Compton, California by Sylvester Scott and Vincent Owens. They are primarily African American and engage in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. The Pirus formed as a rival to the Crips and later became part of the larger Bloods alliance. They remain active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Almighty Gaylords Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: Chicago street gang founded in 1952, part of the People Nation alliance. Initially composed mostly of Italians and some Mexican Americans, it became known as Chicago's largest white street gang. Active in extortion, assault, and murder, with territory across multiple states. Still active as of the article's last update.

top_orgs_detail.name: Nuestra Familia
top_orgs_detail.description: Nuestra Familia is a Mexican-American prison gang originating in Northern California, founded in 1965 at Soledad Correctional Training Facility. It operates both inside and outside prisons, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and robbery. The gang is a rival of the Mexican Mafia and has a large influence over the Norteños street gang. It remains active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Latin Counts
top_orgs_detail.description: The Latin Counts is a Latino street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, with a significant branch in Detroit, Michigan. Founded in 1955 by Tejano youth in the Pilsen neighborhood, it is a member of the People Nation alliance. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and other criminal activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: Native Mob
top_orgs_detail.description: The Native Mob is a Native American street gang founded in the 1990s in Minneapolis, Minnesota. It is one of the largest and most violent Native American gangs in the U.S., active in Minnesota, Wisconsin, Colorado, and South Dakota. The gang engages in drug trafficking, assault, robbery, and murder, with an estimated 200 members as of 2021. Members have been placed on the FBI most wanted list in 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: OutLaw Gangster Disciples
top_orgs_detail.description: The OutLaw Gangster Disciples are a subset of the Gangster Disciples street gang, formed in the early 1990s in Chicago's Cabrini-Green housing project by Charles 'Big Chuck' Dorsey. They are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder, with an estimated 25,000–50,000 members as of 2009. The gang has expanded to Chicago's South Side and has been documented in the U.S. military.

top_orgs_detail.name: Four Corner Hustlers
top_orgs_detail.description: The Four Corner Hustlers is an African American street gang founded in the West Garfield Park neighborhood of Chicago in the late 1960s by Walter Wheat and Freddy Gauge. The gang is known for being one of the most violent and feared on Chicago's West Side. It is allied with the People Nation and has a long-standing rivalry with the Folk Nation. The gang is involved in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, robbery, and murder.

top_orgs_detail.name: Zoe Pound
top_orgs_detail.description: Criminal street gang based in Miami, Florida, founded by Haitian immigrants in 1990. Initially formed as a vigilante self-defense group against attacks on Haitian Americans, it evolved into a criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, robbery, murder, prostitution, and racketeering. The gang expanded across Florida and other states, with territory reaching as far as Virginia by 2006. In 2009, six leaders were arrested on racketeering charges.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kumi 415
top_orgs_detail.description: Predominantly African-American prison gang formed in Folsom State Prison in the mid-1980s, with founding members from the San Francisco Bay Area. The name derives from Swahili 'kumi' (ten) and the sum of 415, which is also San Francisco's area code. Members use African symbols and Swahili language in their culture and tattoos. The gang operates in several California prisons and jails.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Mafia Family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Black Mafia Family (BMF) is a drug trafficking and money laundering organization founded in 1985 in Detroit, Michigan by brothers Demetrius and Terry Flenory. It distributed cocaine across the United States with ties to Mexican cartels, and later used a hip-hop record label as a front. The organization was dismantled in 2005 after a federal investigation, with the Flenory brothers sentenced to 30 years in prison.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sex Money Murder
top_orgs_detail.description: A set of the Bloods street gang operating on the East Coast of the United States, founded in the Soundview section of the Bronx, New York City in 1995 by Peter 'Pistol Pete' Rollock. Originally a subset of the United Blood Nation, it later became independent after the 'Wet T-Shirt War'. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, armed robbery, assault, and murder, and has spread to the southern United States.

title: Iran-Led Axis of Resistance Network
summary: This community is dominated by the Iran-led 'Axis of Resistance,' a loose coalition of Shia and Sunni Islamist groups united in opposition to Israel and Western influence. Central nodes include Hezbollah, Hamas, Houthis, and the Popular Mobilization Forces, all of which receive varying degrees of support from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and Quds Force. Cooperation ranges from direct military and financial aid to ideological alignment and joint operations, such as Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad fighting together in Gaza. The network also extends to state actors like Syria and Russia, as well as non-state allies like Al-Shabaab and AQAP, though ties are often opportunistic or transactional.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hezbollah
top_orgs_detail.description: Hezbollah is a Lebanese Shia Islamist political party and paramilitary group founded in 1982 by Lebanese clerics in response to the Israeli invasion of Lebanon. It maintains strong ties with Iran and is part of the Axis of Resistance. The group operates a vast social services network and holds 15 seats in the Lebanese Parliament. Its paramilitary wing is considered equivalent to a medium-sized army.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hamas
top_orgs_detail.description: Hamas is a Sunni Islamist Palestinian nationalist political organization founded in 1987 by Ahmed Yassin. It governs the Gaza Strip since 2007 and has a military wing, the al-Qassam Brigades. The group has fought multiple wars with Israel and is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries.

top_orgs_detail.name: Houthis
top_orgs_detail.description: The Houthis, officially known as Ansar Allah, are a Zaydi revivalist and Shia Islamist political and military organization that emerged from Yemen in the 1990s. Founded by Hussein al-Houthi, the group has been a central player in Yemen's civil war and is backed by Iran. It is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries and is known for human rights abuses including targeting civilians and using child soldiers.

top_orgs_detail.name: Muslim Brotherhood
top_orgs_detail.description: Transnational Sunni Islamist organization founded in Egypt in 1928 by Hassan al-Banna. Combines political activism with Islamic charity work, aiming to establish a state ruled by Sharia under a caliphate. It gained political power after the Arab Spring but was overthrown in 2013 and subsequently banned and designated a terrorist group in Egypt and several other countries.

top_orgs_detail.name: Popular Mobilization Forces
top_orgs_detail.description: Iranian-backed paramilitary umbrella group operating within Iraq, formally part of the Iraqi Armed Forces but acting independently and answering to Iran's supreme leader. Formed in 2014 to fight the Islamic State, it comprises about 67 primarily Shia armed factions. The PMF is designated as a terrorist group by some countries for certain factions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Palestinian Islamic Jihad
top_orgs_detail.description: Islamist Palestinian paramilitary organization formed in 1981 as an offshoot of the Muslim Brotherhood. It seeks the establishment of an Islamic Palestinian state through military means and rejects the Oslo Accords and two-state solution. Its armed wing, Al-Quds Brigades, operates in the West Bank and Gaza Strip, conducting suicide bombings and rocket attacks. Designated a terrorist organization by multiple countries.

top_orgs_detail.name: Al-Shabaab
top_orgs_detail.description: Sunni Islamist political and militant organization based in Somalia, formed in the mid-2000s as a youth militia within the Islamic Courts Union. It controls territory in south and southwestern Somalia, is allied with al-Qaeda, and has been designated a terrorist group by multiple countries. Active in the Somali Civil War, it has carried out high-fatality attacks and operates in neighboring East African countries.

top_orgs_detail.name: Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula
top_orgs_detail.description: Sunni Islamist militant organization based in Yemen, formed in 2009 from the merger of al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia. It seeks to overthrow the Yemeni government and establish an Islamic emirate. AQAP is considered the most active al-Qaeda affiliate and has conducted high-profile attacks in the West, including the 2009 Northwest Airlines Flight 253 bombing attempt and the 2015 Charlie Hebdo shooting. Its strength has waned due to internal struggles, civil war, and US drone strikes.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clans Network
summary: This community consists of numerous 'Ndrangheta clans from the Calabria region of Italy, primarily based in the provinces of Crotone and Catanzaro. The organizations are deeply interconnected through shifting alliances, often formed to control drug trafficking routes, infiltrate public contracts, and engage in feuds with rival clans. Key players like the Grande Aracri, Mannolo, and Arena clans dominate the network, cooperating with smaller families such as the Nicoscia, Dragone, and Trapasso. Their cooperation spans Italy and extends into Germany, Switzerland, and other European countries, focusing on drug and arms trafficking, money laundering, and political corruption. Despite internal conflicts, these clans maintain a cohesive criminal ecosystem through strategic partnerships and shared illicit enterprises.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Grande Aracri
top_orgs_detail.description: The Grande Aracri 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta, a criminal organization based in Cutro, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1980s by Nicolino Grande Aracri, it expanded drug trafficking across Northern Italy and into Germany, Switzerland, and France. The clan has been involved in violent feuds, infiltrated public contracts, and faced major anti-mafia operations. In 2021, its capobastone began cooperating with authorities.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Mannolo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mannolo are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta from Cutro and San Leonardo di Cutro, Calabria. They are involved in drug trafficking, political influence, and weapons offenses. They are allied with the Trapasso-Zoffreo, Pane-Iazzolino, and Grande Aracri clans. The group has been targeted in multiple police operations from the 1990s to the 2020s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Arena
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Arena is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Isola Capo Rizzuto, Calabria, Italy. It has influence in the Catanzaro area through alliances with local clans. The organization is involved in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, counterfeiting, and auto theft, with international operations in Brazil and Germany. Key figures include Nicola Arena and Carmine Arena.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Megna
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Megna is a criminal organization originating from Crotone, Italy, primarily operating in Turin. It is a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta, involved in mafia-type association, extortion, drug trafficking, and arms trafficking. The group has been engaged in conflicts with rival clans and has been targeted in multiple police operations, including 'Old Family' in 2013 and 'Orso' in 2021. Its historical boss is Domenico 'Mico' Megna.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Nicoscia
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Nicoscia is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Isola Capo Rizzuto. It is allied with the Grande Aracri and Russelli clans and operates in several Italian regions, including Emilia-Romagna. The clan was heavily targeted in the 2000 operation Scacco Matto, leading to the arrest of its boss Salvatore Nicoscia. It has been involved in a feud with the Arena clan and is active in drug trafficking, extortion, and other crimes.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Vrenna
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Vrenna is a mafia clan ('ndrina) based in Crotone, Calabria, Italy. Founded by Luigi Vrenna, who served as capobastone until his arrest in 1974 for ordering the murder of two children, sparking the Faida di Crotone. The clan engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, and arms smuggling, with operations extending to Lazio and Emilia-Romagna. It was heavily targeted by law enforcement operations such as Heracles and Old Family, leading to numerous arrests.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Ferrazzo
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Ferrazzo is a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, Italy, influential in Petilia Policastro and Mesoraca, province of Crotone. It is involved in arms and drug trafficking, money laundering in tourism and real estate, and has operations in Sardinia, Lombardy, Abruzzo, Molise, Switzerland, and Spain. The clan has alliances with other 'ndrine in the Catanzaro area.

top_orgs_detail.name: Giglio
top_orgs_detail.description: The Giglio are a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Strongoli, Calabria, Italy. They are active in international drug trafficking, usury, arms trafficking, money laundering, and are considered the heads of the Locale of Strongoli. The clan has been involved in a feud with the Valente clan and was targeted in the 2018 Operation Stige, which led to numerous arrests.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Scumaci
top_orgs_detail.description: The Scumace are a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in the province of Catanzaro. They are allied with the Grande Aracri, Trapasso-Zoffreo, Mannolo of Cutro, and Tropea-Talarico of Cropani. The clan has been active since at least the 1990s, with members arrested for weapons possession.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Pane
top_orgs_detail.description: The Pane-Iazzolino are a 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta based in Belcastro, Italy. They operate in several towns including Belcastro, Albi, Taverna, Magisano, and Sersale. They are involved in international drug trafficking, usury, arms trafficking, and money laundering. They are allied with other 'ndrine such as the Grande Aracri and Mannolo of Cutro.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Trapasso
top_orgs_detail.description: The Trapasso are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cutro and Steccato di Cutro, Calabria. They are allied with the Mannolo and Grande Aracri clans and have branches in Emilia-Romagna, especially Parma. Involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and infiltration of public contracts and politics. Active from at least the 1970s to present.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Dragone
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Dragone is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cutro, Calabria, Italy. Founded by Antonio Dragone in the 1960s, it engaged in extortion, robbery, and murder. The clan was involved in a long-running feud with the Oliverio family and later with the Grande Aracri and Nicoscia clans. It expanded into Emilia-Romagna in the 1980s, establishing criminal interests in Reggio Emilia, Modena, and Piacenza. The clan's power declined after the killing of Antonio Dragone in 2004 and subsequent police operations.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Bubbo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bubbo are a 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta operating in Petronà, Cerva, and Sersale, allied with the Nicoscia, Farao-Marincola, and Trovato clans. They have been involved in arms sales and are part of the 'Locale di Galbiate' in Lecco, Lombardy. In the 1990s, they engaged in a feud with the Carpino clan, resulting in multiple murders and attempted murders. In 2020, members were arrested in Operation Borderland.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Paparo
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Isola Capo Rizzuto, Calabria, with operations in the Milan area. It is allied with the Nicoscia, Arena, and Barbaro families. Members have been involved in mafia association, extortion, arms trafficking, and public works infiltration, including high-speed railway projects. The clan was targeted in the 2009 'Isola' operation, leading to arrests and asset seizures.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Gaglianesi
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Gaglianesi is a 'Ndrangheta crime family based in Catanzaro, Italy. It operates in various neighborhoods of Catanzaro, controlling criminal activities through a hierarchical structure. Key figures include Girolamo Costanzo, Anselmo Di Bona, and Lorenzo Iiritano. The organization has been active since at least the 1990s, with members arrested in operations such as Falco-Ghibli in 1993.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clans Alliance
summary: This community is a dense network of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, primarily based in the Locride and Gioiosa Jonica areas. They are bound by extensive cooperation in international drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and infiltration of legitimate businesses, including port construction. Key clans like Commisso, Ursino, Aquino, and Mazzaferro form the core, with alliances extending to northern Italy and abroad. The community is characterized by shifting alliances and feuds, but overall, they collaborate to control criminal markets in Italy, Canada, and Europe.

top_orgs_detail.name: Commisso 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: A powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Siderno, Calabria, Italy, with a branch in the Greater Toronto Area, Canada. Founded in the 1930s by Antonio Macrì, it is involved in international drug trafficking, money laundering, and extortion. The clan rose to prominence under Cosimo Commisso and has been engaged in feuds with rival 'ndrine, notably the Costa 'ndrina. It remains active in Italy, Canada, and the United States.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Ursino
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ursino are a powerful 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Gioiosa Jonica, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Aquino and Cordì clans and have settlements in Turin, Liguria, France, Spain, and Canada, with connections to Apulian organized crime. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder, including the 1983 assassination of magistrate Bruno Caccia. Their existence was judicially confirmed in 2014 following Operation Mistero.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Lo Presti
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lo Presti 'ndrina is a Mafia clan of the 'Ndrangheta, originally from Marina di Gioiosa Jonica in Calabria, Italy, and based in Bardonecchia, Piedmont since the late 1950s. It is known as the Maneja family in Calabria and is closely related to the Mazzaferro clan. The family became dominant in the Val di Susa area and was involved in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, usury, and infiltration of public administration. In 1995, the President of Italy dissolved the Bardonecchia city council due to mafia infiltration, the first such case in northern Italy.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Ierinò
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Ierinò is a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Gioiosa Jonica, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in drug trafficking and has been active in kidnappings for ransom, including the 1975 kidnapping of Tobia Matarazzi. The clan operates in various Italian regions and has connections with other 'Ndrangheta families. It is currently active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Aquino 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: The Aquino 'ndrina is a 'Ndrangheta clan based in Marina di Gioiosa Ionica, Italy, active alongside the Mazzaferro clan. They are involved in extortion, usury, and international drug trafficking, with alliances with other 'ndrine. The clan has been in conflict with the Mazzaferro-Femia since the 1970s and has members arrested globally.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Cordì
top_orgs_detail.description: The Cordì are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Locri, Calabria, Italy. They have a strong presence in Portigliola, Gerace, and Antonimina, and are historical rivals of the Cataldo clan. Active since at least the 1960s, they are involved in drug trafficking and have operations in Marche, Emilia, and Rome. The clan has been engaged in a long-running feud with the Cataldo family, resulting in over 30 deaths by 2005.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Mazzaferro
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mazzaferro are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Marina di Gioiosa Ionica, Calabria, Italy. They are active in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, and have been involved in major infrastructure projects. The clan has branches in northern Italy and abroad, including Germany, Belgium, and the United Kingdom. They have been in a long-standing feud with the Aquino-Scali clan since the 1970s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Locale di Piscopio
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrangheta criminal organization based in the Piscopio district of Vibo Valentia, Italy, founded between 2009 and 2010. It is involved in drug trafficking and extortion, operating in the Vibo Valentia area, particularly in Piscopio and Stefanaconi. The group has connections with other 'ndrangheta clans in Calabria and Turin. It was involved in the Stefanaconi feud (2011-2012) and has been targeted by multiple police operations.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Belfiore
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Belfiore is a Calabrian criminal organization based in Gioiosa Jonica, with settlements in Turin, France, and Spain. It was involved in the 1983 murder of magistrate Bruno Caccia in Turin, for which Domenico Belfiore was sentenced to life imprisonment. The group has been engaged in drug trafficking and has formed alliances with other 'Ndrangheta clans. Recent operations against the clan include 'Gioco duro' in 2008 and 'Viceré' in 2018.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Bruzzise
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Bruzzise is a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Palmi. It is allied with the Parrello clan. The group was involved in a long-running feud with the Gallico-Morgante-Sgrò-Sciglitano faction from the 1970s until 1990, and again in the 2000s. In 2010, the 'Cosa mia' operation led to arrests of its members for extortion, mafia association, and infiltration in public contracts.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan dei catanesi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan dei catanesi was a criminal organization based in Turin, Italy, composed mainly of members from Catania, Sicily. It was a branch of the Cursoti clan and was active until the mid-1980s, when it was dismantled by arrests and defections. The group controlled gambling, prostitution, and heroin trafficking in Turin, and was involved in numerous murders and kidnappings.

title: Reggio Calabria 'Ndrangheta Clans Alliance
summary: This community consists of 'Ndrangheta clans based primarily in Reggio Calabria, Italy, that formed shifting alliances during the Second 'Ndrangheta war and subsequent criminal operations. Key players include the Serraino, Condello, Libri, and De Stefano families, who cooperate in drug and arms trafficking, money laundering, and construction rackets. The network is characterized by both historical feuds and strategic partnerships, with ties extending to Lombardy and abroad. Despite internal conflicts, these clans maintain a cohesive criminal ecosystem through shared illicit enterprises and mutual support in police operations.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Serraino
top_orgs_detail.description: The Serraino are a powerful 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cardeto, Santo Stefano in Aspromonte, and Reggio Calabria, Italy.
