Originating in the 19th century as part of the 'picciotteria' association, they have monopolized criminal activities in their home territory and expanded across Italy and internationally. Their illicit activities include drug and arms trafficking, with connections in Belgium, Canada, Bolivia, France, United States, Germany, Switzerland, Morocco, Libya, and Syria. They were considered the richest and most powerful family in Lombardy until the early 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Condello
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Condello is a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Villa San Giovanni, and Fiumara, Italy. It is involved in drug and arms trafficking, contract rigging, and extortion rackets. The clan was led by Pasquale Condello until his arrest in 2008, and later by Domenico Condello until his arrest in 2012. It has been engaged in violent feuds, including the Second 'Ndrangheta war.

top_orgs_detail.name: Latella 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: The Latella 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta, a criminal organization based in Reggio Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1980s by Pasquale Latella, it controls the Ravagnese and Croce Valanidi quarters. The clan has been involved in feuds and alliances, notably during the Second 'Ndrangheta war, and has expanded into construction and financial sectors. It remains active as of the 2010s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Barreca
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Barreca is a Calabrian 'Ndrangheta clan based in Reggio Calabria, active in the southern area of the city since the 1980s. It split from the Ambrogio clan after the Pellaro feud in the late 1980s. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and violent conflicts, with several members becoming collaborators of justice. It has been targeted by multiple police operations, including Valanidi 2 and Barracuda.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Tegano
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina De Stefano is a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, historically linked to the Tegano and Tripodo families. Originating in the 1950s under Domenico Tripodo, it became dominant after winning the first 'Ndrangheta war in the 1970s. The clan is based in the Archi district and has been involved in drug trafficking, economic infiltration, and alliances with Sicilian mafia and Camorra.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Libri
top_orgs_detail.description: The Libri 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, founded in the 1950s by brothers Domenico and Pasquale Libri. It reached its peak in the 1960s-1970s and is considered one of the most aggressive and entrenched clans in the city. Its activities include drug and arms trafficking, extortion, and infiltration into public institutions. The clan suffered losses during the Second 'Ndrangheta war and its founder died in prison in 2006.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Rosmini
top_orgs_detail.description: Powerful 'ndrina (mafia clan) based in Reggio Calabria, controlling the neighborhoods of Ciccarello, Modena, and San Giorgio. Allied with the Condello-Imerti-Serraino group during the second 'ndrangheta war and closely linked to the De Stefano family. Has significant interests in Lombardy and controls public contracts in central and southern Reggio Calabria. Currently led by Diego Rosmini junior.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Imerti
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Imerti is a powerful 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta based in Villa San Giovanni. It was the main 'ndrina that triggered the second 'Ndrangheta war. Founded by Antonio Imerti, it has been involved in drug trafficking and extortion. The group has been targeted by multiple police operations, with many members arrested.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Iamonte
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Iamonte is a historical criminal clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Melito Porto Salvo, Calabria, Italy. It engages in drug trafficking, money laundering, public contracts, arms and explosives trafficking. The clan has expanded its operations to other Italian regions and abroad, with allies including the Moscato and Malacrinò clans.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Zindato
top_orgs_detail.description: The Zindato-Caracciolo are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, allied with the more powerful Libri clan. Key figures include Francesco Zindato, arrested in 2010 for murder, and Antonino Caracciolo, arrested in 1999. The group has been involved in extortion and mafia association, with recent operations in 2010, 2014, and 2020 targeting its members.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Paviglianiti
top_orgs_detail.description: The Paviglianiti are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Lorenzo, Bagaladi, and Condofuri, Calabria. Historically led by Domenico Paviglianiti, dubbed the 'boss of bosses' by the press. They were allied with the De Stefano clan during the Second 'Ndrangheta war. Recent operations have targeted them for drug trafficking, extortion, corruption, and infiltration of public contracts.

top_orgs_detail.name: De Stefano 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: The De Stefano 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta, a mafia-type organization based in Calabria, Italy. Originating from the Archi neighborhood of Reggio Calabria, it rose to prominence in the 1970s after the first 'Ndrangheta war. The clan has strong ramifications throughout Italy, particularly in Lombardy, Tuscany, Emilia-Romagna, and Liguria. Several of its members have been listed among Italy's most wanted fugitives.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Lo Giudice
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lo Giudice are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta, active in the Santa Caterina district of Reggio Calabria, Italy. During the second 'Ndrangheta war, they sided with the Imerti-Condello-Serraino-Rosmini clans. Key figures include Giuseppe Lo Giudice, killed in 1990, and Antonino Lo Giudice, a pentito who later retracted. The group is involved in organized crime activities typical of the 'Ndrangheta.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Di Giovine
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, with branches in Milan and the United States. Involved in drug and arms trafficking. Notable for the prominent role of women, particularly Maria Serraino, who led the clan's heroin trafficking operations in Milan in the 1970s-80s.

title: Brazilian PCC-Led Criminal Alliance Network
summary: This community is dominated by the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC), Brazil's largest drug gang, and its extensive network of allied Brazilian factions such as Bonde do Maluco, Bonde dos 40, Terceiro Comando Puro, and Comando Classe A. These groups cooperate primarily in drug trafficking, sharing routes and resources across Brazil and into Europe, Africa, and neighboring countries. The PCC serves as a central hub, linking smaller regional factions like Guardiões do Estado and Família Terror de Amapá, as well as international partners including the Turkish mafia, Azerbaijani mafia, and Pakistani mafia. Many ties are formal alliances documented in infoboxes, often formed to counter common rivals like Comando Vermelho. The community reflects a structured, hierarchical network where the PCC coordinates a broad coalition for mutual benefit in the drug trade.
top_orgs: Sindicato do Crime do Rio Grande do Norte

top_orgs_detail.name: Primeiro Comando da Capital
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian organized crime syndicate founded on August 31, 1993, by eight prisoners at Taubaté Penitentiary in São Paulo. It is Latin America's biggest drug gang with 40,000 lifetime members and 60,000 contractors as of 2023. The group is based in São Paulo and operates throughout Brazil, South America, West Africa, and Europe, with a focus on cocaine trafficking. It has partnered with the Italian 'Ndrangheta and controls over 50% of Brazil's drug exports to Europe.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bonde do Maluco
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal organization based in Bahia, founded in 2015 in the Mata Escura penitentiary complex in Salvador. Specializes in drug trafficking, robberies, kidnappings, and murders. It is the largest criminal organization in Bahia, with an estimated 15,000 members, and has expanded to other states. Allied with the PCC and rivals with the Comando Vermelho and Katiara.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bonde dos 40
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal organization founded in 2007 at the Pedrinhas Penitentiary Complex in São Luís, Maranhão. It operates in Maranhão, Piauí, and Mato Grosso, engaging in drug trafficking, murders, robberies, and prison rebellions. Originally allied with Primeiro Comando da Capital, it later broke with them and entered conflicts with Comando Vermelho. Considered one of Brazil's largest criminal gangs, it exerts influence in the Northeast region.

top_orgs_detail.name: Terceiro Comando Puro
top_orgs_detail.description: Terceiro Comando Puro (TCP) is a Brazilian criminal organization based in Rio de Janeiro, founded on February 25, 2002, as a dissident faction of the Terceiro Comando. It is the second largest criminal faction in Rio de Janeiro and the third in Brazil, after PCC and Comando Vermelho. The group is involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and murders, and operates in multiple Brazilian states. It is currently active and rivals the Comando Vermelho and Amigos dos Amigos.

top_orgs_detail.name: Comando Classe A
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal faction originating in the state of Pará, founded around 2018 in Altamira. It emerged from prison disputes, breaking with Comando Vermelho and allying with PCC. The group controls drug trafficking routes in the Amazon region and has ties with Colombian cartels and former FARC members. It remains active as of 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: Guardiões do Estado
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal faction operating in the state of Ceará, founded around 2017 in the Conjunto Palmeiras community. It is the 4th largest faction in Brazil, composed mostly of poorly prepared teenagers and young adults. Known for bus burnings, massacres, and homicides, including the Cajazeiras massacre. Its internal structure is decentralized with no single leader.

top_orgs_detail.name: Turkish mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Turkish mafia is a general term for criminal organizations based in Turkey or composed of Turkish citizens. Active since the 1970s, they are involved in drug trafficking, human smuggling, and other crimes, with a strong presence in Western Europe. They have ties to the Turkish deep state and far-right groups like the Grey Wolves. The organization remains active today.

top_orgs_detail.name: Família Terror de Amapá
top_orgs_detail.description: A highly dangerous criminal organization based in Amapá, Brazil, founded in 2015 within the Amapá Penitentiary Administration Institute. It primarily engages in drug and arms trafficking, homicides, and robberies, with territorial influence extending to other Brazilian states and French Guiana. The group is allied with national factions like PCC and rivals the Comando Vermelho.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bonde dos 13
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal faction based in Acre, founded around 2013 in the Rio Branco penitentiary complex. It is heavily involved in drug trafficking, especially along the Bolivia border, and is allied with the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC). The group is engaged in a violent conflict with the Comando Vermelho (CV) for control of Amazonian drug routes. As of 2024, it operates in all municipalities of Acre.

top_orgs_detail.name: Organised crime in Pakistan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Pakistani mafia is a network of ethnically based criminal organizations involved in drug trafficking, assassination, land grabbing, arms smuggling, and other illegal activities. It operates primarily in Pakistan but has international reach, including in the United Kingdom and Scandinavia. The group has alleged ties to the Indian underworld figure Dawood Ibrahim and his D-Company, as well as to Pakistan's Inter-Services Intelligence. Notable gangs include the Chotu gang, which was dismantled in a military operation in 2023.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sindicato do Crime do Rio Grande do Norte
Azerbaijani mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: General term for organized criminal gangs consisting of ethnic Azerbaijanis, primarily active in Russia and former Soviet states. Originating in the early 1980s, they rose to power in the 1990s by taking over heroin trafficking after conflicts weakened Chechen groups. Their activities include drug and arms trafficking, fraud, money laundering, and contract killing. They have expanded into Europe, with notable presence in the Netherlands, Germany, and France.

top_orgs_detail.name: Os Manos
top_orgs_detail.description: One of the two largest criminal organizations in Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil, alongside Bala na Cara (BNC). Founded in 1990 inside the Central Prison of Porto Alegre by Dilonei Melara, it originated from the Falange Gaúcha. The group is heavily involved in drug trafficking, robberies, money laundering, and other violent crimes, and has expanded to neighboring states and countries like Uruguay and Paraguay. It is considered the largest criminal faction in the state.

all_orgs: Sindicato do Crime do Rio Grande do Norte

title: Kurdish-led militant and political coalition
summary: This community is centered around the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) and the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF), forming a broad coalition of Kurdish, leftist, and allied militant groups. The PKK acts as a hub, cooperating with numerous Turkish and international Marxist-Leninist organizations through the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement, while the SDF leads a network of Syrian internal security and border forces. Cooperation ties include formal alliances, joint membership in umbrella movements, and logistical support, such as the Tottenham Boys funneling profits to the PKK. The community also includes Palestinian factions and the Armenian Secret Army, reflecting a shared anti-imperialist and revolutionary ideology.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kurdistan Workers' Party
top_orgs_detail.description: Kurdish militant political organization and armed guerrilla group primarily based in southeastern Turkey, northern Iraq, and northeastern Syria. Founded on 27 November 1978 by Abdullah Öcalan, initially seeking an independent Kurdish state but later shifting to autonomy and cultural rights within Turkey. Designated as a terrorist organization by Turkey, the US, EU, and others. In March 2025, declared a ceasefire and announced plans for dissolution.

top_orgs_detail.name: Syrian Democratic Forces
top_orgs_detail.description: The Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) is a Kurdish-led coalition of ethnic militias and rebel groups founded on 10 October 2015. It serves as the official military wing of the Democratic Autonomous Administration of North and East Syria (Rojava). The SDF aims to create a secular, democratic, and federalized Syria, and has been a key U.S. ally in the fight against the Islamic State. Since 2025, it has been integrating into Syrian state institutions.

all_orgs: Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia
Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine

title: Italian Mafia and Camorra Alliance
summary: This community is a dense network of Italian organized crime groups, primarily Camorra clans from Campania and Cosa Nostra families from Sicily, united by historical alliances and shared criminal enterprises. The Nuova Famiglia confederation, formed to oppose the Nuova Camorra Organizzata, serves as a central hub, linking major Camorra clans like the Casalesi, Alfieri, Giuliano, and Vangone-Limelli. Sicilian Mafia families, including the Catania, Caltagirone, and Ramacca families, are tightly integrated through alliances and cooperation in drug trafficking, extortion, and violence. The community's cooperation is characterized by mutual support in turf wars, joint drug distribution networks (including contacts with Colombian cartels), and shared political and economic infiltration. Despite internal power struggles and state crackdowns, these organizations remain active and interconnected.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Alfieri
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Alfieri was a Camorra organization operating in the northeastern suburbs of Naples, Italy, specifically in the areas of Saviano and Nola, with a stronghold in Piazzolla di Nola. Founded in 1953 by brothers Salvatore and Carmine Alfieri, it became part of the Nuova Famiglia confederation in the late 1970s to oppose Raffaele Cutolo's NCO. The clan was involved in the Camorra wars and was eventually dismantled.

top_orgs_detail.name: Casalesi clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Casalesi clan is a powerful clan within the Camorra, operating from San Cipriano d'Aversa in the Province of Caserta, Italy. Formed by Antonio Bardellino in the 1970s, it is a confederation of clans specializing in construction and maintaining a lower profile than drug-dealing groups. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, toxic waste dumping, and violent internal power struggles. It remains active, with members convicted in the Spartacus trial.

top_orgs_detail.name: Catania Mafia family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Catania Mafia family is a crime family of the Cosa Nostra based in Catania, Sicily, founded around 1925 by Antonino Saitta. It evolved into a powerful and violent faction under leaders Giuseppe Calderone and Benedetto Santapaola, controlling extortion, drug trafficking, and legitimate business infiltration. The organization remains active, currently dominated by the Santapaola-Ercolano clan.

top_orgs_detail.name: Giuliano clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Giuliano is a Camorra organization based in central Naples, founded by Pio Vittorio Giuliano in the 1950s. It was one of the most powerful criminal groups in Campania from the 1970s to the 1990s, involved in cigarette smuggling, drug trafficking, and counterfeiting. The clan operated in neighborhoods such as Forcella, San Gaetano, and Piazza Mercato. Several family members became pentiti (collaborators with justice) in the early 2000s, leading to a decline in its power.

top_orgs_detail.name: Nuova Famiglia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Nuova Famiglia was an Italian Camorra confederation created in the late 1970s to oppose Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata. Headed by powerful Camorra bosses including Carmine Alfieri, the Nuvoletta brothers, Michele Zaza, Luigi Giuliano, and Antonio Bardellino, it was allied with the Sicilian Mafia. The organization was active primarily in Campania and other Italian cities, with a presence in South America, and engaged in various criminal activities. It became defunct in the 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cesarano clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Cesarano is a Camorra organization originating from Ponte Persica, a hamlet northeast of Castellammare di Stabia, Italy, and operating there with strong interests and hegemony also in the neighboring municipality of Pompei. Founded by Ferdinando Cesarano, a charismatic figure who was a ruthless killer and capozona of the Nuova Famiglia in the late 1970s and early 1980s, the clan became independent after the dissolution of the Nuova Famiglia in the mid-1980s. As of 2014, the boss is Luigi Di Martino, known as 'O Profeta.

top_orgs_detail.name: Vollaro clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Vollaro clan is a Neapolitan Camorra clan operating in Portici and San Sebastiano al Vesuvio, founded in the mid-1970s by Luigi Vollaro. It engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, and other crimes, and fought internal and external wars. The clan was weakened by arrests and collaboration of members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Galasso di Poggiomarino
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Galasso di Poggiomarino was a Camorra clan operating in the hinterland east of Naples, based in Poggiomarino. Led by Pasquale Galasso and his brother Martino Galasso, it was considered one of the most powerful and ferocious clans in Camorra history. It controlled neighboring municipalities and most public contracts in the province of Naples, with extensions into the province of Salerno. The clan declined in the 1990s after Pasquale Galasso's arrest in 1992 and subsequent collaboration with justice.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mallardo clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mallardo clan is a Camorra clan based in Giugliano, Campania, Italy, founded by Francesco Mallardo in the late 1970s. It is a key member of the Secondigliano Alliance, considered the most powerful active Camorra group. The clan is involved in racketeering, smuggling, extortion, and drug trafficking, and has been targeted by Italian authorities with asset seizures and arrests.

top_orgs_detail.name: Caltagirone Mafia family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Caltagirone Mafia family is a Cosa Nostra crime family based in Caltagirone, Sicily, founded in 1982 by Francesco 'Ciccio' La Rocca. It is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, and political manipulation. The family rose to prominence through alliances with the Catania Mafia and Corleonesi. Despite La Rocca's imprisonment and death in 2020, the family remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Vangone-Limelli
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan based in Trecase, Boscoreale and Boscotrecase, Italy. Controls drug and arms trafficking in the Vesuvian area and abroad. Founded in the late 1970s by Luigi Limelli, historically affiliated with the Gionta clan and the Nuova Famiglia. Considered one of the richest, most powerful and ferocious Camorra clans, with international cocaine trafficking connections.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ramacca Mafia family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ramacca Mafia family is a crime family of Cosa Nostra based in Ramacca, Sicily, founded in the 1950s by Calogero 'Liddu' Conti. It engages in extortion, infiltration of public contracts, and political influence. The family has maintained control over local economic interests and allied with powerful neighboring families. It remains active, with recent reorganizations under Pasquale Oliva and Rosario Di Dio.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Russo dei Quartieri Spagnoli
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in the Quartieri Spagnoli neighborhood of Naples, Italy. Founded by Domenico Russo, known as 'Mimì dei cani', and later led by his sons. Engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, and a violent feud with the Di Biasi/Faiano clan. The clan emerged victorious and continues to dominate the area.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clans Alliance
summary: This community is a coalition of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, centered around the powerful Morabito 'ndrina and its allies, including Palamara, Bruzzaniti, Vadalà, Rodà, and Talia. They cooperate extensively in drug trafficking, public contract corruption, and money laundering, with ties to Colombian cartels and other Italian mafia groups like Mafia Capitale. The clans are linked through joint operations, such as Bellu Lavuru 2 and Old Family, and share networks for illegal betting and extortion. Their activities extend across Italy and abroad, with connections in northern Italy, Slovakia, and the Americas.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Morabito
top_orgs_detail.description: The Morabito 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Africo, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1950s by Giuseppe Morabito, it is involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, arms trafficking, extortion, corruption, and murder. The clan operates in northern Italy and abroad, with strong ties to other 'ndrine and international criminal networks. Its leader Giuseppe Morabito was arrested in 2004.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Palamara
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Palamara is a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Africo, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Bruzzaniti and Morabito clans. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, particularly cocaine from South America via Senegal, and in the rigging of public contracts in the tourism and hydroelectric sectors. Members have been arrested in multiple operations, including 'Naos' in 2008.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Rodà
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Condofuri, Calabria, Italy, operating along the Ionian coast. It has branches in Liguria, specifically in Lavagna, allied with the Nucera and Casile families. The clan has been involved in public contract corruption, particularly regarding the Strada statale 106 Jonica, and has faced multiple police operations such as 'Bellu lavuru 2' in 2012.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Vadalà
top_orgs_detail.description: The Vadalà are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Bova Marina, Reggio Calabria, Italy. They are involved in international drug trafficking, extortion, and have alleged interests in Slovakia. The clan has been implicated in public works corruption and is linked to the murder of Slovak journalist Ján Kuciak.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Talia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Talia are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Bova, Reggio Calabria, Italy. They have been involved in public works corruption, particularly regarding the SS106 Jonica highway and the Palizzi road variant, controlling concrete supply, hiring, and subcontracting. The clan has been targeted in operations such as Bellu Lavuru 2 in 2012, which led to multiple arrests.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Farao
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Farao is a powerful 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta based in Cirò and Cirò Marina, allied with the Marincola. It operates in the provinces of Crotone and Cosenza, with groups in central and northern Italy and abroad in Germany. Its main activities are extortion and drug trafficking, with connections to Albanian mafia for drug and prostitution. The organization has been active since at least the 1970s and remains influential.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Bruzzaniti
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Bruzzaniti is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Mafia-type criminal organization based in Calabria, Italy. It originates from Africo and is also active in the Milan area. The clan is allied with the Morabito, Palamara, and Crisafulli 'ndrine. Its history spans from the 1970s to the present, with recent events noted in the 2020s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Mandalari
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Mandalari is a 'ndrina (family) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Guardavalle, Calabria, Italy. It is active in construction, real estate, international drug trafficking, money laundering, waste management, and public contracts. The group has alliances with several other 'ndrine and is present in Milan and its hinterland, as well as in New York, Nevada, and Arizona. It also has ties to Freemasonry.

title: Venezuelan-Colombian Criminal Alliance Network
summary: This community is dominated by the Cartel of the Suns, a network of corrupt Venezuelan military officials, which forms alliances with Colombian guerrilla groups (FARC dissidents, ELN, FARC) and Venezuelan gangs (Tren de Aragua). These organizations cooperate primarily in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, and extortion across the Colombia-Venezuela border. The Cartel of the Suns provides state protection and logistics, while guerrilla groups supply cocaine and armed support. Mexican cartel La Unión Tepito connects to this network via Tren de Aragua, extending the reach into Mexico City. Smaller groups like Cartel de La Guajira and Los Mapaches facilitate smuggling and money laundering, creating a cohesive cross-border criminal enterprise.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cartel of the Suns
top_orgs_detail.description: The Cartel de los Soles is a term used to describe criminal networks of drug trafficking and corruption within the Bolivarian National Armed Forces of Venezuela, allegedly emerging in the early 1990s. It involves high-ranking military and political officials, including Nicolás Maduro and Diosdado Cabello, accused by the United States of leading the network. The organization is not a traditional cartel but a series of disconnected cells within the Venezuelan military. The Venezuelan government denies the allegations, and some experts question its existence.

top_orgs_detail.name: FARC dissidents
top_orgs_detail.description: Groups of former FARC members who refused to demobilize after the 2016 Colombian peace process or resumed insurgency. Numbering 2,000-2,500 armed combatants, they are heavily involved in cocaine production and sales. They operate in rural Colombia and Venezuela, controlling areas as a parallel state. Led by former mid-level commanders, they are a major security challenge for Colombia.

top_orgs_detail.name: National Liberation Army (Colombia)
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian far-left guerrilla organization founded in 1964 by Fabio Vásquez Castaño and other rebels trained in Cuba. Advocates a composite ideology of Marxism-Leninism, Camilism, and liberation theology. Engaged in the Colombian conflict, with an estimated 5,000 fighters in Colombia and Venezuela as of 2026. Designated as a terrorist group by several countries and the EU.

top_orgs_detail.name: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia
top_orgs_detail.description: Far-left Marxist-Leninist guerrilla group involved in the Colombian conflict, founded in 1964 from peasant self-defense groups. Engaged in kidnappings, extortion, and drug trafficking to fund operations. Demobilized in 2017 after a peace agreement, though some dissident factions remain active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tren de Aragua
top_orgs_detail.description: Transnational criminal organization originating from Aragua state, Venezuela, based in Tocorón prison. Founded around 2009-2010, it engages in human trafficking, drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. Led by Héctor 'Niño' Guerrero, it is considered the most powerful criminal group in Venezuela, with operations expanding to several Latin American countries and the United States. Designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. in 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: La Unión Tepito
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal cartel based in Tepito, Mexico City, founded in 2009 under the tutelage of Edgar Valdez Villarreal (La Barbie). Its first leader was Francisco Javier Hernández Gómez (Pancho Cayagua), executed in 2017. The cartel engages in drug trafficking, kidnapping, extortion, and homicide, operating primarily in the Mexico City metropolitan area. It is currently active and has alliances with the CJNG.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cartel de La Guajira
top_orgs_detail.description: Criminal organization originating from Maicao, operating in the La Guajira peninsula across the Colombia-Venezuela border. Specializes in smuggling, drug trafficking, arms trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. Has ties to the Cártel de los Soles and former Venezuelan military officials.

title: Corleonesi-led Mafia-Camorra Alliance
summary: This community is dominated by the Corleonesi, the powerful Sicilian Mafia faction that led the Second Mafia War and forged alliances with numerous Sicilian and Camorra clans. The Corleonesi allied with the Nuvoletta, Gionta, and Lubrano-Ligato Camorra clans, as well as Sicilian families like the Grecos, Motisis, and Madonias, creating a cross-regional criminal network. Cooperation involved drug trafficking, extortion, and mutual support in violent conflicts, with the Corleonesi often acting as the central hub. The Nuvoletta clan served as a key bridge between Sicilian and Neapolitan organizations, while the D'Alessandro and Gallo-Cavalieri clans extended the network's reach into Camorra territory. This alliance reflects a strategic consolidation of power across southern Italy's major organized crime groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Corleonesi
top_orgs_detail.description: An alliance of Mafia families within the Sicilian Mafia, formed in the 1970s in Corleone, Sicily. Led by Luciano Leggio, Salvatore Riina, Bernardo Provenzano, and others, it took over the Sicilian Mafia Commission and waged the Second Mafia War (1978–1983). The coalition dominated the Mafia until 2006, imposing a quasi-dictatorship over Cosa Nostra.

top_orgs_detail.name: Nuvoletta clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Nuvoletta clan was a powerful Neapolitan Camorra clan based in Marano di Napoli, Italy, founded in the 1960s by Lorenzo and Angelo Nuvoletta. It was affiliated with Sicilian Mafia families and considered one of the most powerful Camorra clans between the 1970s and 1990s. The clan engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other criminal activities, and was known for its entrepreneurial, corporate-style structure. Following the deaths of its leaders and police crackdowns, the clan was succeeded by the Polverino and Orlando clans.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan D'Alessandro
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in the province of Naples, Italy, based in Castellammare di Stabia and originating from the Scanzano district. Active since the mid-1970s, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and murder. Collaborates with 'Ndrangheta clans and has expanded into Emilia-Romagna. Known for infiltrating legitimate businesses and organizing illegal dog fights.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Gionta
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan based in Torre Annunziata, Italy, founded by Valentino Gionta in the early 1980s. Engaged in cigarette smuggling, heroin trafficking, and control of the fish market. Known for its violent rise and conflicts with other Camorra groups. Currently active despite arrests of key members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan dei Barcellonesi
top_orgs_detail.description: Mafia clan originating from Barcellona Pozzo di Gotto, Sicily. Active since at least the 1970s, involved in extortion and other criminal activities. The clan's structure has been fluid, with multiple factions and shifting alliances. Its mafia status was not recognized in the 1986 Messina maxi-trial.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Lubrano-Ligato
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in Pignataro Maggiore, Caserta province, founded in 1965 by Vincenzo Lubrano. Historically allied with the Nuvoletta clan, Cosa Nostra, and the Banda della Magliana. The Ligato faction remains active after the death of boss Raffaele Ligato in 2022.

top_orgs_detail.name: Galatolo Mafia clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Galatolo Mafia clan was a criminal family from the Acquasanta neighborhood of Palermo, active from the 1950s until 2014. They controlled the Palermo docks and were involved in a violent conflict over the fruit and vegetable wholesale market in the 1950s. The clan was devastated by internal betrayal and later re-emerged, aligning with the Corleonesi in the 1980s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Greco Mafia clan
top_orgs_detail.description: Historically one of the most influential Mafia clans in Sicily, based in Ciaculli and Croceverde Giardini near Palermo. Founded in the late 19th century, the family played a key role in the Sicilian Cosa Nostra, with members serving as secretaries of the Sicilian Mafia Commission. They were involved in heroin trafficking, cigarette smuggling, and money laundering. Despite a decline in the 2000s, they remain active with strong influence.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Gallo-Cavalieri
top_orgs_detail.description: The Gallo-Cavalieri clan is a powerful Camorra organization based in Torre Annunziata, Italy, founded in the mid-1970s by Enrico Gallo and Pasquale Gallo. It was historically allied with the Gionta clan and part of the Nuova Famiglia, but later engaged in a bloody feud with them. The clan is considered one of the richest, most powerful, and most violent Camorra groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Motisi Mafia clan
top_orgs_detail.description: Historical Sicilian Mafia clan from the Pagliarelli area in Palermo, founded in 1936. One of the oldest active Mafia clans in Palermo, it has been involved in the Sicilian Mafia Commission and allied with the Corleonesi. Members have been convicted for murders of anti-mafia judges and rival clan members. Current boss Giovanni Motisi has been a fugitive since 1993.

title: San Luca 'Ndrangheta Clans Alliance
summary: This community is a dense network of 'Ndrangheta clans primarily based in San Luca, Calabria, Italy, including the Giorgi, Strangio, Romeo, Pelle, and La Maggiore 'ndrine. These clans are bound by shifting alliances and family ties, often cooperating in international drug trafficking, particularly cocaine from South America to Europe. They have been targeted together in major police operations like European 'Ndrangheta Connection and Puerto Liberado, indicating joint criminal ventures. The community also includes peripheral clans like Petullà and Codispoti, which serve as allies and mediators, extending the network's reach to other Italian regions and abroad. Despite historical feuds, such as the Strangio-Pelle rivalry, the community is characterized by pragmatic cooperation for drug trafficking and money laundering.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Giorgi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Giorgi 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization based in San Luca, Calabria, Italy. They are involved in international drug trafficking, with operations in Germany and the Netherlands. The clan has ties with other 'Ndrangheta families and the Salento criminality in Apulia. They have been targeted by multiple police operations in the 2010s and 2020s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Strangio
top_orgs_detail.description: A powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Luca, Calabria, Italy. Historical rivals of the Pelle 'ndrina, they are known for the Duisburg massacre in 2007. Involved in drug trafficking, ship sinkings, and a long-running feud with the Pelle-Vottari clan. The organization remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Romeo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Romeo 'ndrina, also known as Stacchi, is a 'Ndrangheta clan based in San Luca, Italy. It is allied with the Nirta (La Maggiore) and Pelle clans. The clan has been involved in international drug trafficking, with operations spanning Europe and the Americas. Members have been arrested in major operations such as Puerto Liberado and European 'Ndrangheta connection.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Pelle
top_orgs_detail.description: The Pelle 'ndrina, also known as Pelle-Vottari, is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Luca, Italy. It is among the most influential 'ndrine, with members holding key positions in the organization's consultative and top bodies, particularly in the Ionian mandate and the province. The clan is active in international cocaine trafficking, with contacts with Colombian cartels, as well as in contract rigging and extortion. It was documented in the 2010 Reale operation as having influence in regional election campaigns.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina La Maggiore
top_orgs_detail.description: A powerful 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Luca, Calabria, Italy. Founded by the Nirta brothers in the late 19th century, it is considered one of the most influential 'ndrine historically. Active in drug trafficking, particularly heroin and cocaine, with operations in northern Italy and Europe. The clan has been implicated in high-profile events such as the kidnapping of Aldo Moro.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Petullà
top_orgs_detail.description: The Petullà are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cinquefrondi, Italy. Active since the 1980s, they are involved in international drug trafficking, with members in northern Italy, France, Germany, Peru, and Australia. They have been engaged in a feud with the Foriglio clan and have been targeted in multiple police operations.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Codispoti
top_orgs_detail.description: The Codispoti are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Luca, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Nirta-Scalzone, Romeo, Libri, and De Stefano families. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking from South America to Italy and other European countries, and has acted as mediators in the Reggio Calabria mafia war. They are considered a reference point for the 'Ndrangheta in Rome.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Cua
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Cua is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Natile di Careri, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in international drug trafficking and the appropriation of public contracts. The clan has been active since at least the 1970s and has been targeted in major operations such as Minotauro and European 'Ndrangheta Connection. Members have been arrested for cocaine trafficking from South America to Europe.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Iozzo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Iozzo-Chiefari are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Chiaravalle Centrale and Torre di Ruggiero, Calabria, Italy. They are part of the Locale of Serra San Bruno. The group is involved in drug trafficking (marijuana and cocaine), illegal weapons possession, and illicit activities in construction, earthmoving, wholesale timber, and subcontracting. They have been targeted in multiple police operations, including 'Ortrhus' in 2019 and 'Anteo' in 2021.

title: Secondigliano Alliance and Camorra Clans
summary: This community is centered on the Secondigliano Alliance, a powerful coalition of Camorra clans from Naples, Italy. The alliance includes major groups like the Contini, Di Lauro, Lo Russo, and Rinaldi clans, which cooperate in drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering. The Scissionisti di Secondigliano, a splinter group from the Di Lauro clan, also maintains ties with many smaller clans. The network is characterized by shifting alliances, feuds, and collaborations, with a strong international presence in Spain and the Netherlands. Despite internal conflicts and police crackdowns, these clans remain dominant in the Neapolitan underworld.

top_orgs_detail.name: Secondigliano Alliance
top_orgs_detail.description: The Secondigliano Alliance is a strategic alliance of Camorra clans based in Naples, Italy, founded in the late 1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, Edoardo Contini, and Francesco Mallardo. It controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in many areas of Naples and is considered one of the two most powerful Camorra organizations. The alliance has branches in the Netherlands and Spain, involved in international drug trafficking and money laundering. Despite police crackdowns, it remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Scissionisti di Secondigliano
top_orgs_detail.description: The Scissionisti di Secondigliano, also known as the Amato-Pagano clan, is a Camorra clan based in the Secondigliano district of Naples, Italy. Founded in 2004 by Raffaele Amato and Cesare Pagano, it split from the Di Lauro clan and became a major force in international drug trafficking, with a strong presence in Spain. The group engaged in a violent feud with the Di Lauro clan in 2004-2005, resulting in over 100 murders. Currently, it remains one of the most important Camorra organizations.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lo Russo clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lo Russo clan was a Neapolitan Camorra clan based in Miano, Naples, founded in the late 1970s by the Lo Russo brothers. It controlled numerous neighborhoods in northern Naples for over three decades, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and prostitution. The clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia and later the Secondigliano Alliance. Following arrests of its leaders, the organization is considered overthrown.

top_orgs_detail.name: De Luca Bossa clan
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in the eastern suburbs of Naples, specifically Ponticelli and Cercola. Founded by Antonio De Luca Bossa, known as 'o sicco, after splitting from the Sarno clan. Engaged in a bloody feud with the Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno families, culminating in a car bomb attack in 1998. The clan was dismantled after the killing of its acting boss Giuseppe Mignano in 2002.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rinaldi clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Rinaldi clan is a Camorra clan based in San Giovanni a Teduccio, Naples, founded in 1989 from a split of the Mazzarella clan. It is heavily involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, racketeering, and counterfeiting. Since 2019, it has formed a powerful alliance with the De Luca Bossa and Aprea-Cuccaro clans, becoming the dominant Camorra group in eastern Naples. The clan is allied with the Secondigliano Alliance and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Di Lauro clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Di Lauro is a Camorra organization based in the northern neighborhoods of Naples, Italy, specifically Secondigliano and Scampia. Founded by Paolo Di Lauro in 1982, it became one of the most powerful drug trafficking groups in Europe, at its peak earning over €500,000 per day from drug sales. The clan controlled the open-air drug market in Scampia's Vele district and was involved in a violent internal feud starting in 2004. Despite arrests of key members, the clan's influence persisted through alliances and family succession.

top_orgs_detail.name: Aprea-Cuccaro clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is a Camorra criminal organization operating in Barra, Naples, founded in the early 1990s by Giovanni Aprea and Angelo Cuccaro. It is involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, racketeering, and money laundering. The clan has allied with the Secondigliano Alliance and the De Luca Bossa clan, and has been engaged in feuds with the Mazzarella and Sarno clans. It remains active as of the 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Contini clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Contini clan is a powerful Neapolitan Camorra clan based in Naples, Italy, founded in the early 1980s by Edoardo Contini. It operates primarily in the Arenaccia district and Naples Central Station area, with international activities in Spain, the Netherlands, Scotland, and Eastern Europe. The clan is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance and is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering. It is considered one of the most powerful Camorra groups, known for its lack of internal divisions and informants.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Pariante
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in the western suburbs of Naples, specifically in Bacoli and Monte di Procida. Founded by Rosario Pariante, who was originally the right-hand man of Paolo Di Lauro. The clan split from the Di Lauro clan in 2004, triggering the First Scampia feud. Currently led by Genny Pariante, son of Rosario.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Di Biasi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Di Biasi, also known as 'i Faiano', was a Camorra organization operating in the Quartieri Spagnoli of Naples, Italy. It was historically allied with the Giuliano clan but faced rivalry from the Russo clan. The group was involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities. In 2008, a major police operation led to the arrest of several members, weakening the clan.

title: Colombian Narco-Paramilitary Network
summary: This community is dominated by Colombian narcoparamilitary groups, centered on the powerful Clan del Golfo, which maintains alliances with numerous other organizations including Los Rastrojos, Oficina de Envigado, and the Popular Liberation Army. The ties reflect a complex web of cooperation in drug trafficking, territorial control, and paramilitary activities, often involving franchise arrangements, shared drug routes, and mutual defense pacts. Mexican cartels like Cártel del Noreste and Cártel de Guasave are also connected through alliances, indicating cross-border drug trafficking links. The community includes both left-wing guerrillas and right-wing paramilitaries, united by pragmatic business interests rather than ideology.
top_orgs: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan del Golfo
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary and terrorist organization that emerged after the demobilization of the AUC between 2003 and 2006. It is considered the largest, most dangerous, and best-structured criminal group in Colombia, with presence in 392 municipalities. Its activities include drug trafficking, massacres, extortion, kidnapping, and illegal mining. The group's top leader, Dairo Antonio Úsuga David (alias Otoniel), was captured in October 2021.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Rastrojos
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organized armed group formed by dissidents of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC) after its demobilization in 2006. It became independent after the assassination of its main founder in Venezuela in 2008 and was one of the most important drug trafficking organizations in Colombia. The group finances itself through cocaine, marijuana, and heroin trafficking, as well as illegal gold mining. It operated mainly in Valle del Cauca and Cali, with influence in other parts of Colombia and western Venezuela. By 2017, the group had fragmented into small factions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Popular Liberation Army
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian left-wing guerrilla group founded in 1967 based on Marxist-Leninist ideology. Originally the armed wing of the Communist Party of Colombia (Marxist-Leninist), it was the third-largest guerrilla group in Colombia before most members demobilized in 1991. A dissident faction remains active in the Catatumbo region and the Colombia-Venezuela border, with around 400 members, and is classified as an organized armed group by the Colombian government.

top_orgs_detail.name: Oficina de Envigado
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian drug cartel and criminal organization originally founded as an enforcement wing and debt collection service for Pablo Escobar's Medellín Cartel. Founded by Diego Murillo Bejarano (Don Berna) in the early 1990s. It operates mainly in Medellín and Envigado, controlling extortion, gambling, and money laundering. By 2019, its activities shifted from direct drug trafficking to providing services to lower-level traffickers and mafia groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cártel del Noreste
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal and terrorist organization formed in 2015 as a split from Los Zetas after the capture of Omar Treviño Morales. Based in Nuevo Laredo, Tamaulipas, it controls that city and operates in several Mexican states. Its activities include drug trafficking, kidnapping, extortion, human trafficking, and money laundering. In February 2025, it was designated a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organization and one of the emerging criminal gangs serving drug trafficking (Bacrim), part of the Colombian internal armed conflict. Founded by Pedro Oliviero Guerrero Castillo alias 'Cuchillo' after demobilizing from the Bloque Héroes del Guaviare, with 200 men. It was the heir of the Bloque Centauros of the AUC and allied with drug trafficker Daniel Barrera. After the death of Cuchillo and capture of Barrera, 500 members surrendered in 2011, and the group split into two factions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Caparrapos
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian narco-paramilitary group that was part of the internal armed conflict. Formed in 1996 as a personal guard for alias Macaco, later became part of the AUC and then the Clan del Golfo. Engaged in massacres, murders, extortion, forced displacement, drug trafficking, and illegal mining. The group was dismantled in 2021 with the death of its leader Robinson Gil Tapias.

top_orgs_detail.name: United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian far-right paramilitary and drug trafficking group active from 1997 to 2006. Formed through a merger of local right-wing militias to combat left-wing guerrillas. Heavily financed by drug trade and local elites, with alleged ties to Colombian military. Responsible for numerous massacres and human rights abuses. Demobilized by 2006, but successor groups remain.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cártel de Guasave
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and money laundering. It emerged in 2008 as an armed wing of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and became independent in February 2017 under Fausto Isidro Meza Flores. Based in Guasave, Sinaloa, it is allied with La Mayiza against Los Chapitos. The group is still active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Cachiros
top_orgs_detail.description: Honduran drug trafficking organization founded in the early 1990s by cattle ranchers in Colón and Olancho departments. It became a major cocaine transporter between Colombian and Mexican cartels, amassing a net worth of nearly one billion dollars. The group had extensive business and political ties, including bribing high-level Honduran officials. Its leaders surrendered to US authorities in 2015, leading to asset seizures.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bloque Meta
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian drug trafficking and neo-paramilitary organization active in the Colombian armed conflict. It emerged from the ERPAC after the AUC demobilized in 2006, and later merged into Los Puntilleros. The group was considered one of Colombia's most brutal drug trafficking organizations, with around 260 members. It was completely dismantled after the capture of its last leader in September 2017.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Paisas
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian criminal and narcoparamilitary organization based in Medellín, formed in 2008 from remnants of paramilitary groups demobilized during peace talks (2004-2006). It was founded by former members of the Oficina de Envigado after the extradition of Don Berna. The group engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. It was dissolved by Colombian security forces in 2014, with remaining members joining the Clan Úsuga.

top_orgs_detail.name: North Coast Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: The North Coast Cartel was a Colombian drug cartel operating along the Caribbean coast from the 1980s to 2010/2011. Based in Barranquilla, it controlled drug trafficking routes and local production. Its leader Alberto Orlandez Gamboa was arrested in 1998 and extradited to the US. The cartel was involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, and violence.

all_orgs: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia

title: Colombian-Mexican Drug Trafficking Alliance
summary: This community is centered on the Medellín and Cali Cartels, which cooperated with Mexican cartels like the Guadalajara and Sonora Cartels to control cocaine trafficking routes from Colombia to the United States. They also formed paramilitary groups such as Muerte a Secuestradores and Los Pepes to combat kidnappings and target Pablo Escobar. Puerto Rican organizations like the Martinez Familia Sangeros and Ñetas served as distribution networks in the Caribbean. The ties are primarily based on drug supply, territorial franchising, and mutual support against common enemies.
top_orgs: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá

top_orgs_detail.name: Medellín Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: The Medellín Cartel was a loose coalition of Colombian drug trafficking organizations based in Medellín, Colombia, active from the 1970s to 1993. It played a central role in the international cocaine trade, led by Pablo Escobar and others. The network was known for its violence, including assassinations and terrorism, and was designated a terrorist group by the United States. It was dismantled after Escobar's death in 1993.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cali Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian drug trafficking organization based in Cali, founded in the 1970s by the Rodríguez Orejuela brothers, José Santacruz Londoño, and Hélmer Herrera Buitrago. It was a major cocaine cartel that rivaled the Medellín Cartel and operated globally. Known for its sophisticated money laundering and intelligence networks. The cartel was dismantled in the late 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Martinez Familia Sangeros
top_orgs_detail.description: A criminal organization based in Puerto Rico, founded in the 1980s by Quitoni Martinez. It consists of six crime families, with the Estrella Family being the primary one. The group is involved in drug trafficking, loan sharking, extortion, and other crimes, with connections to Colombian cartels and international operations. It remains active with an estimated 1200-1500 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Guadalajara Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: The Guadalajara Cartel was the first major Mexican drug trafficking organization, formed around 1978 by Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, Rafael Caro Quintero, and Ernesto Fonseca Carrillo. Based in Guadalajara, Jalisco, it controlled most drug trafficking routes in Mexico during the late 1970s and 1980s, smuggling opium, marijuana, and cocaine into the United States. The cartel was dismantled in 1989 following the arrests of its leaders.

top_orgs_detail.name: Muerte a Secuestradores
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian paramilitary group and private army formed in 1981 by drug cartels, politicians, and landowners to fight kidnapping and guerrilla groups. Supported by the Medellín Cartel, it assassinated political opponents and community organizers. Active through the 1990s, it waged counterinsurgency against FARC-EP and M-19.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Pepes
top_orgs_detail.description: Paramilitary group formed in 1992 by enemies of Pablo Escobar, based in Antioquia, Colombia. Financed by the Cali Cartel and led by the Castaño brothers, they waged a small-scale war against the Medellín Cartel until Escobar's death in 1993. The group had ties to Colombian National Police and later evolved into the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC).

top_orgs_detail.name: Sonora Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican drug cartel founded in 1987 by Miguel Caro Quintero and Rafael Caro Quintero, operating out of northern Mexico and smuggling drugs into the United States. It was considered one of the oldest and best-established cartels by the DEA, with direct links to Colombian cartels. The cartel was acquired by the Sinaloa Cartel by 2007 and reported defunct by 2010.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cartel de la Costa
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian drug trafficking organization active in northern Colombia from the 1980s to 1998. It controlled drug trade in the Caribbean region and managed routes to the Caribbean and Central America. Initially based in Santa Marta and later Barranquilla, it was led by José Rafael Abello Silva and later Alberto Orlández Gamboa. The cartel dissolved in 1998 due to arrests, deaths, and the incursion of the AUC's Northern Bloc.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Charlines
top_orgs_detail.description: A Galician drug trafficking clan founded in 1975 by Manuel Charlín Gama and his family. Based in Galicia, Spain, they engaged in smuggling, hashish and cocaine trafficking, and money laundering. The clan was dismantled in 1990 by judge Baltasar Garzón but remained active, with members imprisoned and later released. They are considered one of the most important drug clans in Galicia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Colima Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican drug trafficking and methamphetamine producing cartel founded in 1988 by José de Jesús Amezcua Contreras and his brothers. Based in Guadalajara, Jalisco, it controlled the methamphetamine trade using precursor chemicals from Asia. The cartel was absorbed into the Sinaloa Cartel and became defunct by 2005.

top_orgs_detail.name: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian paramilitary group formed in northwestern Colombia, operating mainly in Antioquia and Córdoba departments. Founded by the Castaño brothers to retaliate against FARC-EP for their father's assassination. Initially protected farmers' interests and drug crops, later became a core component of the United Self-Defenders of Colombia (AUC).

top_orgs_detail.name: Organizacion de Narcotraficantes Unidos
top_orgs_detail.description: Puerto Rican criminal organization based in Bayamón, founded in 1995 by José 'Coquito' López Rosario. It unified drug dealers under one umbrella, becoming the most powerful drug trafficking organization in Puerto Rico by 2009. Involved in distributing heroin, cocaine, marijuana, crack, Xanax, and Percocet, and transporting shipments from Latin America to the United States. Declined after mass arrests in 2009-2010.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cartel del Dorado
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian criminal organization active from 1970 to 2010, based in Bogotá. It supplied narcotics to the Medellín Cartel and later became one of the world's largest under leader Luis Caicedo. Engaged in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, human trafficking, and arms trafficking. The organization was dismantled after Caicedo's arrest in 2010.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ñetas
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ñetas are a prison gang founded in 1979 at Oso Blanco prison in Río Piedras, Puerto Rico, by Carlos Torres Iriarte. Originally formed for mutual protection and to improve prison conditions, they later engaged in drug trafficking, robbery, and extortion. The gang operates in Puerto Rico, the United States, Spain, and Ecuador, with an estimated 12,000 members. Despite the death of their founder, they remain active and are known for their white color identification.

top_orgs_detail.name: The Extraditables
top_orgs_detail.description: Narcoterrorist organization created by Colombian drug lords in the early to mid 1980s to oppose extradition to the United States. Its members were largely drawn from the Medellín Cartel, with leaders including Pablo Escobar and Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha. The group engaged in violent acts such as murder, bombing, and kidnapping to pressure the Colombian government. It was active from 1984 until 1993.

all_orgs: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá

title: Chicago Folk Nation Gang Alliance
summary: This community is centered on the Folk Nation alliance, a Chicago-based coalition of street gangs founded in 1978. The network includes major gangs like the Spanish Gangster Disciples, Simon City Royals, Latin Eagles, and Maniac Latin Disciples, all affiliated with Folk Nation. Cooperation ties reveal a dense web of alliances, drug trafficking partnerships, and mutual protection agreements, with many gangs having merged or split over time. The community's activities are primarily drug trafficking, racketeering, and violent crime, with a strong presence in the Midwest and expansion to other states. Despite some national connections, the core identity remains rooted in Chicago's gang landscape.
top_orgs: Almighty Latin Kings and Queens Nation

top_orgs_detail.name: Spanish Gangster Disciples
top_orgs_detail.description: The Spanish Gangster Disciples is a Latino street gang originating in South Chicago, founded by Rudy Rios in the early 1980s as an offshoot of the Gangster Disciples. It is part of the Folk Nation alliance and is involved in drug trafficking, assault, robbery, and murder. The gang uses symbols such as the Star of David and colors baby blue and black. It remains active in Chicago.

top_orgs_detail.name: Folk Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: An alliance of street gangs originating in Chicago, founded in 1978 by Larry Hoover within Stateville Correctional Center. The alliance has spread throughout the United States, particularly the Midwest. Members use symbols like the Star of David and the number 6. It is involved in drug trafficking, burglary, extortion, and homicide.

top_orgs_detail.name: Simon City Royals
top_orgs_detail.description: The Simon City Royals are a street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1952 as Simon City, a greaser gang, and later merged with the Ashland Royals in 1969 to form the Simon City Royals. They are heavily involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and loan sharking, and have expanded to other states, notably Mississippi. The gang is predominantly white and is one of the oldest and largest white gangs in the US.

top_orgs_detail.name: Latin Eagles
top_orgs_detail.description: The Latin Eagles are a Puerto Rican street gang founded in Chicago, Illinois in 1964. Originally formed as a club to protect against white greaser gangs, it evolved into a criminal organization. The gang is aligned with the Folk Nation and has been involved in territorial disputes with rival gangs such as the Latin Kings and Gangster Disciples. It is still active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gangster Disciples
top_orgs_detail.description: The Gangster Disciples are an African-American street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1968 by David Barksdale and Larry Hoover. Originally formed as the Black Gangster Disciple Nation, the group split in 1989 into the Gangster Disciple Nation and the Black Disciple Nation. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, assault, firearms violations, fraud, homicide, and money laundering, with an estimated 30,000 members in Chicago and operations across the United States and internationally.

top_orgs_detail.name: Maniac Latin Disciples
top_orgs_detail.description: Predominantly Hispanic street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in 1966 as a baseball club that evolved into a full-fledged gang. They are part of the Folks Alliance and are involved in drug trafficking, assault, auto theft, robbery, homicide, money laundering, and weapons trafficking. The gang has an estimated 7,500-10,000 members and operates in multiple states across the U.S.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Disciples
top_orgs_detail.description: African American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in 1966 by David Barksdale. Originally formed as the Devil's Disciples, it later merged with the Gangster Disciples to form the Black Gangster Disciples Nation before splitting again. The gang is involved in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, murder, and racketeering, with an estimated 15,000 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Spanish Cobras
top_orgs_detail.description: Primarily Latino street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in the 1970s by former members of the Maniac Latin Disciples. Active in multiple Midwestern states and parts of Florida, with a strong presence on Chicago's north and west sides. Allied with Folk Nation gangs and rivals of People Nation gangs including the Latin Kings.

top_orgs_detail.name: Almighty Insane Popes Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: Chicago street gang formed in the late 1950s on the north side, originally from a Greek greaser gang. The name originally stood for 'Protecting Our People Eliminating Spics' but later changed to 'scum'. The gang split into north and south factions, with the north side allied with Folk Nation and the south side with People Nation. Active in Chicago with rivalries against Latin Kings and other gangs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Supreme Team
top_orgs_detail.description: Organized crime syndicate based in South Jamaica, Queens, New York City, founded in 1981 by Kenneth 'Supreme' McGriff and his nephew Gerald 'Prince' Miller. Primarily involved in crack cocaine distribution, the gang generated over $200,000 daily at its peak in 1987. Leaders were convicted in federal court, with McGriff receiving life imprisonment in 2007.

top_orgs_detail.name: TAP Boyz
top_orgs_detail.description: Chicago-based Arab American street gang formed in 1992 to protect the Arab-American community from anti-Arab sentiment after the Gulf War. They operated primarily in Chicago and Bridgeview, Illinois, and disbanded in 1999 after losing members to rival gangs. Their colors were white and red, and their symbol was a four-pointed star.

top_orgs_detail.name: La Raza Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: La Raza Nation is a street gang founded in Chicago in 1973, primarily composed of Hispanic individuals of Mexican or Central American origin. The gang is affiliated with the Folk Nation and has been involved in conflicts with rival gangs such as the Almighty Saints, Latin Kings, and Black P. Stones. Its activities have expanded beyond Chicago to states including Wisconsin, Ohio, Florida, and Georgia. The gang remains active as of the 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Almighty Latin Kings and Queens Nation

all_orgs: Almighty Latin Kings and Queens Nation

title: Platì 'Ndrangheta Clans and Allies
summary: This community is centered on the 'Ndrangheta clans originating from Platì, Calabria, particularly the powerful Barbaro and Papalia 'ndrine, which act as hubs for a network of allied families like the Molluso, Sergi, Trimboli, and Marando. These clans cooperate extensively in international drug trafficking, especially cocaine and heroin, with ties to South American cartels and other criminal groups. They maintain a strong presence in northern Italy (Lombardy and Piedmont) and have connections in Australia. The community is characterized by intermarriage, shared operations, and mutual support in feuds, forming a cohesive criminal bloc.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Barbaro
top_orgs_detail.description: The Barbaro 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Platì, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the late 19th century by Francesco Barbaro, it is one of the most powerful 'Ndrangheta clans, with branches operating in Lombardy, Piedmont, and Australia. The clan is involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, arms trafficking, and other criminal activities. It has been engaged in feuds with rival clans such as the Mammoliti 'ndrina.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Papalia
top_orgs_detail.description: The Papalia are a powerful 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta originating from Platì, Calabria, Italy. They are heavily rooted in northern Italy, especially in the Milanese hinterland, with their stronghold in Buccinasco. Their main activities include drug trafficking (cocaine and heroin), extortion, and construction contract management. They have been involved in feuds and high-profile kidnappings, and many members have been arrested in major operations like Nord-Sud and Duomo Connection.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sergi 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Sergi is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Calabrian mafia organization, based in Platì, Italy. It is involved in drug trafficking, particularly cocaine and heroin, operating in northern Italy (Milan, Busto Arsizio, Gallarate) and Rome, with a strong presence in Australia since the 1970s. The clan has been implicated in kidnappings, murders, and political corruption, and has faced numerous arrests and operations against it.

top_orgs_detail.name: Trimboli 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: The Trimboli are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Platì, Italy. They have been active in Volpiano, Turin since the 1970s, allied with the Marando and Agresta clans, and in Australia since the 1960s. Involved in international drug trafficking, they have faced multiple arrests and operations against them, including Operation Igres and Revenge 2. The clan remains active, with members still being arrested.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Marando
top_orgs_detail.description: The Marando 'ndrina is a Calabrian 'Ndrangheta clan based in Platì, Italy. Active in northern Italy, particularly in Milan, Volpiano, Turin, and Liguria, it is involved in cocaine and heroin trafficking. The clan has been engaged in feuds with rival groups such as the Stefanelli family. Key figures include Francesco Marando, who was killed in 1996, and Pasquale Marando, who carried out revenge killings.

top_orgs_detail.name: Molluso 'ndrina
top_orgs_detail.description: The Molluso are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Platì, Italy, with a strong presence in Buccinasco, Lombardy. They have been active in Lombardy since the 1980s and are considered satellite affiliates of the powerful Barbaro-Papalia clans. The historical boss is Giosofatto Molluso. Members have been involved in cocaine trafficking with Turkey and Colombia, and more recently in illegal waste trafficking.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Agresta
top_orgs_detail.description: The Agresta are a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta from Platì, Calabria, active since at least the 1920s. They are also active in Piedmont, particularly in Volpiano, since the 1970s in collaboration with the Marando and Trimboli clans. Involved in drug trafficking, illegal gambling, and money laundering, they have been targeted in several police operations including Minotauro and Cerbero.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Cataldo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Cataldo 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Locri, Calabria, Italy, founded in the 1960s. It is involved in drug trafficking and has been engaged in a long blood feud with the Cordì 'ndrina. The clan has allied with the Marafioti family and the De Stefano 'ndrina, and has been involved in cocaine trafficking from Colombia. It remains active as of the present.

title: Syrian Rebel Coalition Network
summary: This community comprises a dense network of Sunni Islamist rebel groups that fought against the Ba'athist regime during the Syrian Civil War. The central organizations are Al-Nusra Front and Ahrar al-Sham, which formed shifting alliances with numerous smaller factions to coordinate military operations and share resources. Cooperation ties include joint attacks, shared training camps, and membership in umbrella coalitions like the Army of Conquest. Despite ideological differences and occasional rivalries, these groups were united by their common goal of overthrowing Bashar al-Assad and establishing an Islamic state. The network reflects the fragmented yet interconnected nature of the Syrian insurgency, where pragmatic alliances were formed to maximize combat effectiveness.

top_orgs_detail.name: Al-Nusra Front
top_orgs_detail.description: Sunni Islamist militant group that fought against the Ba'athist regime in the Syrian Civil War, aiming to overthrow Bashar al-Assad and establish an Islamic state. Formed in 2012, it was designated a foreign terrorist organization by the US in 2012 and was the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016. In 2016 it broke ties with Al-Qaeda and renamed itself Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham, then merged into Hayat Tahrir al-Sham in 2017.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ahrar al-Sham
top_orgs_detail.description: Ahrar al-Sham was a coalition of Sunni Islamist rebel groups that fought against the Ba'athist regime in the Syrian Civil War. Founded in 2011, it became one of the largest rebel factions, with up to 20,000 fighters. The group aimed to establish an Islamic state under Sharia law. It was dissolved in January 2025 and incorporated into Syria's Ministry of Defense.

title: European Drug Trafficking Super-Cartel
summary: This community is dominated by the Kinahan Organised Crime Group, which acts as a central hub connecting Irish, British, Dutch, Moroccan, and Spanish criminal networks. The groups cooperate primarily in large-scale cocaine and synthetic drug trafficking, with ties extending to Colombian and Mexican cartels. The Moroccan mafia and Dutch penose serve as key logistics and distribution partners, while the Galician Mafia and French Milieu provide access to European ports. The community is characterized by strategic alliances, shared hideouts, and joint operations, including assassination pacts among the Noffel clan, Taghi organisation, and Irish mob. Despite occasional feuds, these organizations form a cohesive super-cartel dominating the European drug market.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kinahan Organised Crime Group
top_orgs_detail.description: Major Irish transnational organised crime syndicate founded by Christy Kinahan in the 1990s. Based in Ireland with operations in the UK, Spain, and UAE. Engaged in drug trafficking, money laundering, murder, and arms trafficking. Estimated wealth up to €1 billion. Involved in a feud with the Hutch Gang since 2015.

top_orgs_detail.name: Moroccan mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: A collection of criminal organizations primarily composed of people of Moroccan descent, specializing in trafficking large quantities of cocaine and synthetic drugs through Spain, Portugal, the Netherlands, and Belgium. Active since the 1990s, these networks have privileged relationships with Colombian and Mexican cartels and import drugs via European ports. The group gained notoriety during the Mocro-War gang conflict starting in 2012.

top_orgs_detail.name: British firms
top_orgs_detail.description: British firms are organised crime groups originating in the United Kingdom, predominantly composed of White British individuals but also including other ethnicities. They are involved in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, racketeering, and extortion. The groups have expanded operations to Spain, the Netherlands, and Thailand. They are considered the dominant organized crime presence in the UK despite the rise of foreign gangs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Organised crime in the Netherlands
top_orgs_detail.description: Organised crime in the Netherlands, often referred to as penose, encompasses criminal organizations primarily of Dutch descent active since the 18th century. Based in the Netherlands, these groups are heavily involved in drug trafficking, particularly ecstasy, cocaine, and cannabis, making the Netherlands the world's largest ecstasy exporter. They cooperate with Colombian cartels, British firms, and Moroccan drug barons, and have expanded operations to Spain and Thailand. The term penose originates from Bargoens slang and is used to describe the Dutch underworld.

top_orgs_detail.name: Galician Mafia
top_orgs_detail.description: Drug smuggling groups based in the Spanish region of Galicia, originating from tobacco smuggling by local fishermen. They traffic cocaine, hashish, heroin, and illegal tobacco into Spain and Portugal, with connections to Colombian, Moroccan, and British criminal networks. Known for violent clan feuds and allegations of political corruption.

top_orgs_detail.name: Irish mob
top_orgs_detail.description: The Irish mob is a collection of organized crime groups composed of largely ethnic Irish members operating primarily in the United States since the early 19th century. Originating in Irish-American street gangs, it has appeared in most major U.S. and Canadian cities, especially in the Northeast and Midwest. Notable historical gangs include the Dead Rabbits, Whyos, and the Westies. The group has been involved in various criminal activities such as murder, drug trafficking, and racketeering.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lyons Crime Family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lyons Crime Family is an organised crime group based in Glasgow, Scotland, founded in the 1990s by Eddie Lyons Snr. It is involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and violence.
