The group has been engaged in a deadly feud with the Daniel Crime Family since 2001 and has connections with the Kinahan Organized Crime Group. Key members have been arrested in Dubai in 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: Organized crime in France
top_orgs_detail.description: Organized crime in France, known as grand banditisme or milieu, is based in major cities like Marseille, Grenoble, Paris, and Lyon. It has evolved from prostitution and hijacking in the early 1900s to drug trafficking, extortion, counterfeiting, and white-collar crime today. The Corsican mafia, including the Unione Corse and Gang de la Brise de Mer, is a prominent component. As of 2024, these groups remain powerful, controlling various businesses in southern France.

top_orgs_detail.name: Grendon crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: Irish organised crime syndicate based in Ireland, emerging in the late 20th century. Involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, firearms smuggling, extortion, and contract violence. Considered by Gardaí as the most significant Irish-based drug trafficking network. Concentrated within Ireland, with a hierarchical family-led structure.

title: Wagner Group and African Paramilitary Allies
summary: This community is centered on the Wagner Group, a Russian private military company, and its extensive network of allied paramilitary and state forces across Africa and the Middle East. Wagner Group provides security, training, and combat support in exchange for mining contracts and political influence, cooperating with national armies like FAMa (Mali), FACA (Central African Republic), and FADM (Mozambique), as well as with the Rapid Support Forces in Sudan and the Libyan National Army. The community also includes Russian state entities like the Russian Armed Forces and the Internet Research Agency, reflecting Wagner's integration with Russian state interests. These ties are primarily military alliances and business partnerships, often involving joint operations against insurgents or support for authoritarian regimes.
top_orgs: Forças Armadas de Defesa de Moçambique

top_orgs_detail.name: Wagner Group
top_orgs_detail.description: Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, then by his son Pavel. Emerged during the war in Donbas in 2014, later played a major role in the Russian invasion of Ukraine, notably in the Battle of Bakhmut. Operates in Syria, Libya, Central African Republic, Mali, and other African countries, providing security in exchange for mining contracts. Accused of war crimes and led a brief armed rebellion against Russia in June 2023.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rapid Support Forces
top_orgs_detail.description: Sudanese paramilitary force originating from the Janjaweed militias used in the Darfur conflict. Formally established in August 2013 under Muhammad Dagalo (Hemedti). It fought alongside the Sudanese Armed Forces until 2023, then turned against them in a civil war. The RSF has been accused of war crimes, genocide, and human rights abuses, and has established a parallel government in 2025.

top_orgs_detail.name: Forças Armadas de Defesa de Moçambique

all_orgs: Forças Armadas de Defesa de Moçambique

title: Neo-Nazi Accelerationist Network
summary: This community consists of a transnational neo-Nazi and white supremacist network united by accelerationist ideology, aiming to hasten societal collapse and establish white ethnostates. Central nodes like Atomwaffen Division, The Base, and Feuerkrieg Division coordinate paramilitary training, propaganda, and violent actions across the US, Europe, and Russia. Cooperation ties reveal alliances with Russian far-right groups (Russian Imperial Movement, Rusich Group) and occultist organizations (Order of Nine Angles), as well as shared members and joint operations. The network's structure is decentralized but interconnected through mutual support, training exchanges, and ideological alignment, making it a cohesive threat despite geographic dispersion.

top_orgs_detail.name: Atomwaffen Division
top_orgs_detail.description: International far-right extremist and neo-Nazi network formed in 2015 and based in the Southern United States. It expanded across the US and other countries, described as one of the most violent neo-Nazi movements of the 21st century. Designated as a terrorist group by multiple governments, its members were responsible for murders, bombings, and planned attacks. The group dissolved in November 2024.

top_orgs_detail.name: The Base
top_orgs_detail.description: The Base is an international neo-Nazi organization and paramilitary training network formed in 2018 by Rinaldo Nazzaro. It advocates accelerationist and white nationalist ideology, aiming to overthrow governments and establish white ethnostates. The group operates in multiple countries and has been designated as a terrorist organization by several nations and the European Union.

top_orgs_detail.name: American Front
top_orgs_detail.description: White supremacist organization founded in San Francisco, California by Bob Heick in 1984. Initially modeled after the British National Front, it evolved into a political group with skinhead and non-skinhead members. Active in multiple US states by 1989, it published the tabloid Aryan Warrior and held White Workers Day marches.

top_orgs_detail.name: Combat 18
top_orgs_detail.description: Neo-Nazi terrorist organization founded in 1992 in the United Kingdom. Originally formed as a stewarding group for the British National Party, it later split and became an openly violent group. It has spread to other countries including Canada and Germany, and has been linked to murders of immigrants, non-whites, and dissidents. Banned in Canada and Germany, but still active.

top_orgs_detail.name: White Aryan Resistance
top_orgs_detail.description: White supremacist and neo-Nazi organization in the United States, founded in 1983 by Tom Metzger. Originally named White American Political Association, then White American Resistance, before adopting the name White Aryan Resistance in 1984. Based in Warsaw, Indiana, it published a newspaper and produced a cable TV show. The group is described as openly white-racist and revolutionary.

title: Manchester Gang Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered on the Gooch Close Gang, a dominant organized crime group in Manchester, UK, which has forged extensive alliances with numerous local gangs such as the Cheetham Hill Gang, Rusholme Crew, and Old Trafford Crips. The cooperation primarily involves drug trafficking, weapon smuggling, and mutual protection against rival gangs like the Doddington Gang. The network also includes international links to the Jamaican Shower Posse, indicating cross-border drug and arms smuggling. Additionally, the Noonan Crime Family and Quality Street Gang connect through truces and joint criminal enterprises, including armed robberies in Holland with the Red Army hooligan firm. Overall, this community represents a web of Manchester-based gangs united by shared criminal interests and territorial defense.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gooch Close Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Organised crime group based in Moss Side, South Manchester, England, founded in the late 1980s. Primarily involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, robbery, and murder. Known for violent disputes with rival gangs such as the Doddington Gang. Still active as of the article's last update.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cheetham Hill Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Organised crime group based in Cheetham, Manchester, England, established by the early 1980s. Engaged in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, armed robbery, and other serious crimes. Known for turf wars with Moss Side and Salford gangs, and for controlling Manchester's club scene. The gang was at its peak between the late 1980s and early 2000s and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Quality Street Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The Quality Street Gang operated in Manchester, England, in the 1960s-1980s. Considered by some police to be major crime instigators, others view them as a social friendship group. No member was ever arrested or convicted. They are linked to football hooliganism and armed robberies in Holland.

top_orgs_detail.name: Shower Posse
top_orgs_detail.description: Jamaican gang founded around 1981 by Lester Lloyd Coke and Vivian Blake, based in Tivoli Gardens, Kingston. Involved in drug and arms smuggling, with branches in North America and the UK. Known for political ties to the Jamaica Labour Party and alleged CIA connections. Still active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Red Army
top_orgs_detail.description: A hooligan firm associated with English football club Manchester United, active since the 1970s. Known for violence and criminal activities including drug smuggling, armed robberies, and theft. The firm has subdivisions such as the Inter City Jibbers (ICJ) which engages in acquisitive crime. Its activities have declined since the late 1980s.

title: European Left-Wing Militant Networks
summary: This community comprises European left-wing militant and separatist groups active from the 1970s to the 2000s, including ETA, the Provisional IRA, and the Red Brigades. They are linked by shared ideologies of armed struggle against state authority and capitalism, and by practical cooperation in weapons, training, and logistics. ETA serves as a central hub, connecting Basque separatist groups with Irish, Italian, and other European militants. The network also includes support organizations like NORAID and state sponsors such as Libya and Czechoslovakia. These ties reflect a transnational web of solidarity and resource sharing among revolutionary movements.
top_orgs: Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária

top_orgs_detail.name: ETA
top_orgs_detail.description: ETA was an armed Basque nationalist and separatist organization active in Spain and France from 1959 to 2018. Founded during Francoist Spain by Basque nationalist students, it evolved into a clandestine paramilitary group conducting assassinations, bombings, and kidnappings. The group killed over 800 people and was classified as a terrorist organization by multiple countries. It announced a definitive end to armed activity in 2011 and formally dissolved in 2018.

top_orgs_detail.name: Provisional Irish Republican Army
top_orgs_detail.description: Irish republican paramilitary force that sought to end British rule in Northern Ireland and create an independent socialist republic. It emerged in December 1969 from a split in the IRA and was the most active republican group during the Troubles. It waged a guerrilla and bombing campaign against British forces, killing over 1,700 people. The IRA declared a ceasefire in 1997, decommissioned weapons in 2005, and its political wing Sinn Féin participated in the Good Friday Agreement.

top_orgs_detail.name: Red Brigades
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian far-left Marxist-Leninist militant group formed in 1970, responsible for numerous violent attacks during Italy's Years of Lead, including the kidnapping and murder of former prime minister Aldo Moro in 1978. The group sought to create a revolutionary state through armed struggle and remove Italy from NATO. It was broken up in the 1980s but had a resurgence in the late 1990s to 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária

all_orgs: Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária

title: Chinese and Southeast Asian Criminal Networks
summary: This community is dominated by Chinese triad societies and Asian-American street gangs, with ties to Southeast Asian militant groups. Central organizations like 14K, Sun Yee On, and Wo Hop To cooperate in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, and money laundering, often through alliances with smaller gangs such as Wah Ching and Tiny Rascal Gang. The inclusion of Abu Sayyaf and its allies (Maute Group, MNLF) indicates cross-regional collaboration in illicit activities. Many ties are based on shared ethnic backgrounds and territorial control in Chinese diaspora communities, particularly in the United States and Hong Kong.

top_orgs_detail.name: 14K
top_orgs_detail.description: The 14K is a triad group based in Hong Kong but active internationally, founded in 1945 by Kuomintang Lieutenant-General Kot Siu-wong in Guangzhou, China. It is the second largest triad in the world with around 20,000 members, primarily involved in drug trafficking, illegal gambling, and other criminal activities. The group relocated to Hong Kong in 1949 and remains active, with a loose structure and factional violence.

top_orgs_detail.name: Abu Sayyaf
top_orgs_detail.description: Jihadist militant and pirate group based in the Philippines, founded in 1991 by Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani. It follows Wahhabi Sunni Islam and has been responsible for bombings, kidnappings, and assassinations. The group aligned with the Islamic State in 2014. It was declared dismantled in 2024 but resurfaced in 2026.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tiny Rascal Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Primarily Cambodian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, founded in 1981 by Cambodian youth for protection. Engages in drug trafficking, assault, arms trafficking, murder, and theft. Has an estimated 5,000-10,000 members and associates as of 2009, with operations in 16 US states and Canada.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ghost Shadows
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the early 1970s to the mid 1990s. Formed in 1971 by immigrants from Taiwan and Hong Kong, affiliated with the On Leong Tong. Engaged in extortion, kidnapping, murder, racketeering, drug trafficking, and illegal gambling. Became defunct due to federal RICO crackdowns in the 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sun Yee On
top_orgs_detail.description: Sun Yee On is a major triad organization based in Hong Kong and China, founded in 1919 by Heung Chin. It has over 25,000 members worldwide and is active in multiple countries including the UK, US, France, and Belgium. The group is involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other illegal activities. It remains active as of the present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Wo Hop To
top_orgs_detail.description: Wo Hop To is a triad group based in Wan Chai, Hong Kong, founded in 1908 in Sai Ying Pun as a secret political organization opposing the Qing dynasty. It specializes in protection rackets and is one of the Four Major Gangs of Hong Kong, alongside Wo Shing Wo, 14K, and Sun Yee On. The group expanded into the United States in the early 1990s, establishing a presence in San Francisco.

top_orgs_detail.name: Wah Ching
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese American criminal organization and street gang founded in San Francisco in 1964. Involved in narcotics, racketeering, gambling, and other crimes. Active primarily in the San Francisco Bay Area and Greater Los Angeles. Known for the 1977 Golden Dragon Massacre and conflicts with rival gangs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Shui Fong
top_orgs_detail.description: Shui Fong is a major Triad group based in Hong Kong, founded in the 1930s from a workers' union of a soft drink company. It operates in Hong Kong, Macau, mainland China, and Chinese communities abroad, engaging in extortion, loan sharking, narcotics, and control of nightclubs and mahjong dens. It is one of Hong Kong's most active triads and part of Macau's 'Four Major Gangs'. The group has been involved in violent conflicts with rivals like 14K.

top_orgs_detail.name: Wo Shing Wo
top_orgs_detail.description: Wo Shing Wo is the oldest of the Wo Group triad societies and the triad with the longest history in Hong Kong, established in Sham Shui Po in 1930. It is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, gambling, and prostitution, and has extended its influence from Tsuen Wan to other parts of Hong Kong, Macau, and globally. The group continues to use traditional initiation ceremonies for new members.

top_orgs_detail.name: On Leong Chinese Merchants Association
top_orgs_detail.description: A tong society based in Chinatown, Manhattan, established in November 1893. It fought violent wars with the Hip Sing Tong for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses. In recent years, linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang. Currently has over 30,000 registered members, mostly with commercial or industrial backgrounds.

top_orgs_detail.name: White Tigers
top_orgs_detail.description: The White Tigers were a Chinese American street gang based in New York City, primarily active in Flushing, Queens, and northern Chinatown from the early 1980s to the mid 1990s. Formed in 1980 by immigrants from China and American-born Chinese, they engaged in extortion, kidnapping, illegal gambling, racketeering, drug trafficking, human trafficking, arson, and murder. The gang was involved in turf wars with rivals like the Green Dragons and had an estimated membership of 10 to 50 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Snakehead
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese gangs based in Fujian province that smuggle people to wealthier countries. They use forged documents, bribes, and fake business delegations to bypass immigration controls. Notable members include Cheng Chui Ping and Guo Liang Chi. Active since the 1990s, they operate globally in Chinese communities.

title: Calabrian-Neapolitan Crime Alliance
summary: This community is a cross-regional coalition linking 'Ndrangheta 'ndrine from Calabria with Camorra clans from Naples, centered around the powerful 'Ndrina Gallico. The Gallico family leads a faction in a long-standing feud, allied with the Morgante, Sgrò, and Sciglitano 'ndrine, and historically with the Dinaro and Imerti-Condello-Fontana groups. On the Camorra side, the Licciardi clan, a key member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano, cooperates with the Gallico and Filippone clans in drug trafficking, and with the Abbinante and Sautto-Ciccarelli clans in violent operations. The ties are primarily based on drug trafficking, shared feuds, and coordinated criminal activities, bridging the traditionally separate organized crime groups of southern Italy.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Gallico
top_orgs_detail.description: The Gallico are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Palmi, Calabria, Italy. They are involved in extortion, drug trafficking, and infiltration of public contracts. They are allied with the Morgante, Sgrò, and Sciglitano families and operate also in Rome. The group has been engaged in a long feud with the Parrello-Condello and Bruzzise clans.

top_orgs_detail.name: Licciardi clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Licciardi is a powerful Camorra syndicate based in the Secondigliano district of Naples, Italy. It was formed in the mid-1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, who transformed the area into a strategic hub for drug storage and trafficking. After his death, leadership passed to his siblings, notably Maria Licciardi. The clan is a member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano cartel and remains active in drug trafficking and counterfeiting, with operations extending to the Netherlands.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Abbinante
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Abbinante is a Camorra organization operating in the northern area of Naples, specifically in Marano di Napoli. Founded by Raffaele Abbinante, it was originally affiliated with the Nuvoletta clan and later became a trusted group of Paolo Di Lauro. The clan is involved in drug trafficking and cigarette smuggling, and has been engaged in violent feuds with rival clans. Key members include Raffaele, Guido, and Antonio Abbinante.

title: London Gang Alliances and Rivalries
summary: This community is a dense network of London-based street gangs and organized crime groups, spanning from the 1960s to the present. It includes historic firms like the Krays' The Firm and the Richardson Gang, alongside modern gangs such as Tottenham Mandem and Ghetto Boys. Cooperation ties reveal a web of alliances, protection arrangements, and federations, often linking older and newer groups. The community is characterized by shifting loyalties, splinter groups, and joint ventures like the Shower Syndicate, reflecting the fragmented yet interconnected nature of London's underworld.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tottenham Mandem
top_orgs_detail.description: Organised street gang based in Tottenham, North London, founded in the 1970s on the Broadwater Farm estate. Active in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, armed robbery, kidnapping, and contract killing. One of the largest gangs in North London during the 1990s, controlling much of the local drug markets. Declined in the early 2000s, splintering into groups like NPK and Star Gang.

top_orgs_detail.name: The Firm
top_orgs_detail.description: English organized crime group based in Bethnal Green, London, led by the Kray twins from the late 1950s until their arrest in 1968. Involved in murder, armed robbery, arson, protection rackets, gambling, and assaults. Gained celebrity status in the 1960s before being convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ghetto Boys
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ghetto Boys is a street gang based in South London, primarily in New Cross, Lewisham, and Deptford. Formed in the 1970s on the Woodpecker Estate, the gang is mostly of Black British origin and has been involved in drug trafficking, murder, assault, and robbery. It had a violent conflict with the Peckham Boys during the 2000s but declined by the mid-late 2000s, splintering into groups like Shower and 814. The gang is still active as of the 2000s-present.

top_orgs_detail.name: Richardson Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: English crime gang based in South London in the 1960s, founded by Charles Richardson. Known for extreme violence and torture methods such as pulling teeth and nailing victims to floors. Involved in racketeering, narcotics, extortion, and other crimes. Rivaled the Kray Firm.

title: 1960s-70s US Leftist Revolutionary Network
summary: This community comprises a network of leftist revolutionary organizations active in the United States during the 1960s and 1970s. They were united by a shared opposition to the U.S. government, capitalism, and racial oppression, often advocating for socialism, Black liberation, Puerto Rican independence, and anti-war causes. Cooperation ties include alliances, ideological influences, and joint actions such as building occupations, robberies, and prison escapes. Key hubs like the Young Lords, Weather Underground, and Black Panther Party connected diverse groups ranging from street gangs to militant cells, reflecting a broad coalition of radical activism.

top_orgs_detail.name: Young Lords
top_orgs_detail.description: The Young Lords were a far-left political organization that evolved from a Chicago street gang founded in 1959. They conducted direct action campaigns and community service programs in Chicago and New York City, advocating for Puerto Rican independence and socialism. The group split into the Young Lords Party in 1970 and dissolved in 1972, with some members joining the Puerto Rican Socialist Party.

top_orgs_detail.name: Weather Underground
top_orgs_detail.description: American Marxist militant organization active from 1969 to 1977, originally a faction of Students for a Democratic Society. It sought to overthrow the U.S. government through a bombing campaign targeting government buildings and banks, often issuing evacuation warnings. The group was classified as a domestic terrorist group by the FBI and disintegrated after the Vietnam War peace accord.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Spades
top_orgs_detail.description: The Black Spades is a mostly African-American street gang that originated in the Bronx, New York, in the late 1960s. It gained popularity in the 1970s and later spread to other parts of New York City and states like Massachusetts and Connecticut. In 2019, the gang rebranded as TBS New Direction, a community service group focusing on anti-violence and food distribution. The gang was influenced by the Five-Percent Nation, Malcolm X, and the Black Panthers.

top_orgs_detail.name: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love
top_orgs_detail.description: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love was an organization of drug users and distributors operating from the mid-1960s through the late 1970s in Orange County, California. Founded by John Griggs, it produced and distributed LSD and hashish with the goal of starting a 'psychedelic revolution' in the United States. The group was known for manufacturing 'Orange Sunshine' LSD and assisting Timothy Leary's escape from prison. It was largely dismantled after a major drug raid in 1972, though some members were arrested as late as 2009.

title: Myanmar Ethnic Armed Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered on the United Wa State Army (UWSA), the largest ethnic armed group in Myanmar, which acts as a key hub connecting various insurgent organizations. The Three Brotherhood Alliance, a coalition of the Arakan Army, Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army, and Ta'ang National Liberation Army, forms a core subgroup, with many other groups listed as allies. Cooperation is primarily military, involving joint operations, weapons supply, and strategic alliances against the Myanmar military junta. The network includes both well-known groups like the Kachin Independence Army and smaller entities such as the Bamar People's Liberation Army, all united in opposition to the central government.

top_orgs_detail.name: United Wa State Army
top_orgs_detail.description: The United Wa State Army is the military wing of the United Wa State Party, the de facto ruling party of Wa State in Myanmar. Formed in 1989 after the collapse of the Communist Party of Burma, it is a well-equipped ethnic minority army of 20,000–30,000 soldiers led by Bao Youxiang. It controls territory independent of Myanmar's central government but officially recognizes Myanmar's sovereignty. The UWSA is the largest non-state armed group in Myanmar and has led the Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee since 2017.

top_orgs_detail.name: Three Brotherhood Alliance
top_orgs_detail.description: An alliance of three ethnic armed groups in Myanmar: the Arakan Army, Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army, and Ta'ang National Liberation Army, formed in June 2019. It rose to prominence in 2023 for resisting the Burmese junta after the 2021 coup, launching Operation 1027 in October 2023. The alliance operates primarily in Rakhine State and northern Shan State.

title: Sicilian Mafia Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered on the Clan dei Cursoti and Clan Cappello, two powerful Catania-based Mafia groups that have formed a dense web of alliances with numerous smaller Sicilian clans. The cooperation ties reveal a complex network of support during internal conflicts, such as the 1991 faida, where the Ercolano, Ferrera, and Malpassoti clans backed the Catania faction of Cursoti. The Cappello clan extends its influence through alliances with the Cintorino, Pillera-Puntina, Urso-Bottaro-Attanasio, Lo Piccolo, and Piacenti-Ceusi clans, while the Cursoti maintain ties with the Piacenti and Sciuto-Tigna clans in Milan. Overall, this community represents a coalition of Sicilian Mafia families, primarily from the Catania region, united by strategic partnerships and mutual support in criminal activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan dei Cursoti
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian criminal organization originating from Catania, active in the 1970s-1980s with bases in Milan and Turin. It split into two factions, the Milanese and Catania branches, and was involved in conflict with the Santapaola family. The clan is currently led by the Di Stefano brothers.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Cappello
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Cappello is a Mafia organization and family originating from the province of Catania, Sicily, named after boss Salvatore Cappello. It is considered, together with the Santapaola family, one of the most powerful Cosa Nostra clans in eastern Sicily. The clan emerged from the Ferlito-Pillera group in the 1980s and has been involved in internal conflicts and alliances with other local clans.

title: Bronx Gang Peace Coalition
summary: This community is centered around the Ghetto Brothers, a New York City gang that organized a peace meeting in the 1970s to reduce violence among rival street gangs. The meeting was attended by numerous gangs including the Savage Skulls, Savage Sisters, and many others, most of which are from the Bronx or nearby areas. The cooperation ties are primarily based on truce negotiations and peace meetings, reflecting a temporary alliance to end conflicts. The Ghetto Brothers acted as a central mediator, leveraging their influence to bring together diverse gangs for dialogue.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ghetto Brothers
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ghetto Brothers were a gang and music group founded in New York City's South Bronx in the late 1960s. They aimed to uplift young Latino and Black men in their community and were involved in politics, particularly the Puerto Rican independence movement. The gang spread to much of the Northeastern United States and later merged with the Savage Nomads to form Los Solidos in the 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Savage Skulls
top_orgs_detail.description: The Savage Skulls are a mostly Puerto Rican and African American street gang founded in the Hunts Point area of the Bronx in the late 1960s. They gained popularity in the 1970s and declared war on drug dealers in their territory. The gang was involved in conflicts with rival gangs and was documented in a 1979 film. They are known for appropriating Nazi symbolism.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clan Network
summary: This community consists of multiple 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, primarily operating in the provinces of Catanzaro and Vibo Valentia. The clans are interconnected through alliances, joint criminal operations, and shared territorial interests, particularly in drug trafficking, extortion, and public contract corruption. Key hubs like the 'Ndrina Iannazzo and 'Ndrina Giampà form the core, with ties to other clans such as the Fruci, Anello, and Bonavota. Many of these clans have been targeted together in major anti-mafia operations like Effetto Domino and Prima, indicating sustained cooperation. The network's cohesion is reinforced by mutual support in feuds and shared control over local economic sectors.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Iannazzo
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Lamezia Terme, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Giampà and Cannizzaro-Daponte clans. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud with the Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra clan since 2000, and has been implicated in public contract corruption and extortion. Its members have been targeted in multiple anti-mafia operations, including Effetto Domino, Andromeda, Nettuno, and Quinta Bolgia.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Fruci
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Fruci is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a criminal organization based in Calabria, Italy. It operates primarily in the province of Crotone and is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and other illegal activities. The clan has been targeted in several police operations, including 'Prima', 'Effetto Domino', and 'Imponimento'. Key members include Giuseppe Fruci and Vincenzo Fruci.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Giampà
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Giampà is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in the Nicastro district of Lamezia Terme, Italy. It emerged in the late 1980s under boss Francesco Giampà, known as 'Il professore', who won a bloody feud against a rival faction. The clan is involved in drug and arms trafficking and was a key player in the Lamezia Terme feud from 2002 to 2011. Francesco Giampà is currently serving a life sentence.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Anello
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Anello is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Filadelfia, Calabria, Italy. Its activities include robberies, extortion, and drug trafficking. The clan has been involved in several operations by Italian authorities, including Operation Conquista, Effetto Domino, and Via col vento. It has interests in various towns in the province of Vibo Valentia and has been in conflict with other 'ndrine.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Bonavota
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bonavota are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Sant'Onofrio and Stefanaconi in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria, Italy. They have members in Piedmont (Turin, Carmagnola, Moncalieri) and Rome. Their activities include extortion, usury, drug trafficking, and money laundering. They have been involved in a bloody feud with the Petrolo-Bartolotta clans in the 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Lo Bianco
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lo Bianco are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Vibo Valentia, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Barba clan within the local 'Ndrangheta structure. Their illegal activities include public works contracts, extortion, usury, and control of the healthcare sector. The clan has been targeted by operations such as Nuova Alba (2007) and The Goodfellas (2010).

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Cracolici
top_orgs_detail.description: The Cracolici are a 'ndrina (Calabrian mafia clan) based in Maierato, Maida, and Filogaso in the province of Vibo Valentia, with branches in Novara and Milan. They have been involved in conflicts with the Bonavota clan for control of Sant'Onofrio. In 2024, operation Artemis revealed a splinter group based in Maida that exploited a power vacuum to expand into drug trafficking and forestry sectors.

title: Cape Town Gang Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered on two dominant Cape Town gangs, The Americans and Hard Livings, which are allied and cooperate in drug trafficking, extortion, and other criminal activities. They are supported by a network of smaller neighborhood gangs such as the Clever Kids, Junky Funky Kids, Naughty Boys, and others, which act as affiliates or satellite groups. The Americans also have ties to the 26 and 27 prison number gang, indicating prison-based connections. The community represents a hierarchical alliance structure typical of Cape Town's gang landscape, where larger organizations control territory and resources through a web of smaller allied gangs.

top_orgs_detail.name: The Americans
top_orgs_detail.description: Large street gang and organized crime group based in Cape Flats, Cape Town, South Africa. Founded in 1983 by Neville Harold (Jackie Lonte) in Belgravia Estate, Athlone. Engages in drug trafficking, extortion, abalone poaching, protection rackets, and prostitution. Aligned with 26 and 27 prison number gangs, and rivals the British camp. Lonte was assassinated in 1998.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hard Livings
top_orgs_detail.description: The Hard Livings gang is a large street gang and organized crime group based in Manenberg, Cape Town, South Africa. Founded in 1971 by Rashied Staggie and his twin brother Rashaad, it started as a criminal street gang involved in drug distribution. The gang evolved into a structured criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, prostitution, contract killing, extortion, illegal diamond smuggling, and other activities. It has international connections, including cooperation with the Sicilian mafia.

title: Parrello-Condello Faction of 'Ndrangheta
summary: This community is a coalition of 'Ndrangheta 'ndrine centered around the powerful Parrello clan from Palmi, Italy. The Parrello family leads a faction in a long-standing feud with the Gallico family, and the other 13 'ndrine are allied with them as part of the Parrello-Condello faction. Their cooperation primarily involves joint criminal activities such as drug trafficking, extortion, and infiltration of public contracts, as well as mutual support in the feud. The Parrello clan's high degree centrality indicates it is the key node connecting these smaller 'ndrine, which have limited direct ties among themselves.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Parrello
top_orgs_detail.description: The Parrello are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Palmi, Reggio Calabria, Italy. They have been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and infiltration of public contracts. The clan has been engaged in a feud with the Gallico family since 1986. Recent operations have led to arrests for international cocaine trafficking and corruption.

title: Black Jackets and Black Cobra Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered around the Black Jackets, a Turkish-German gang, and the Black Cobra, a Danish immigrant street gang, which are allied with each other and with numerous smaller gangs. The Black Jackets serve as a hub, forming alliances with groups like 210 International, Bandogs, Black Army, Black Dogs, Dogpack 416, Holmblaaa, Kommando 210, and Veles gang. The Black Cobra is allied with Black Army, Black Ghost, Black Spider, and M16. The cooperation appears to be based on mutual support and shared criminal activities, likely including drug trafficking and other organized crime, with many of the smaller gangs lacking detailed descriptions but clearly integrated into this network.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Jackets
top_orgs_detail.description: Primarily ethnically Turkish criminal gang active in Germany, the Netherlands, and Denmark. Formed in 1985 in Heidenheim an der Brenz by immigrants from Turkey, Italy, and former Yugoslavia. Engages in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, prostitution, burglary, and vandalism. Claims to be a brotherhood rather than an outlaw motorcycle club or criminal organization.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Cobra
top_orgs_detail.description: Black Cobra is a large immigrant street gang in Denmark, founded in 2000 in Roskilde. It operates in major Danish cities and parts of Sweden, with approximately 100 members. The gang engages in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder, and controls a youth gang called the Black Scorpions. Members identify by wearing black and white shirts with a cobra emblem.

title: Transnational Drug Trafficking Alliance
summary: This community is a loose coalition of drug trafficking organizations spanning the Americas, Europe, and the Caribbean, centered on Colombian and Mexican cartels as primary suppliers. They cooperate through direct drug shipments, money laundering, contract killings, and enforcement alliances. European groups like the Clerkenwell syndicate and Arifs provide distribution networks in the UK, while Marielitos and No Limit Soldiers serve as enforcers and intermediaries. Chinese organized crime groups facilitate money laundering for Italian and Mexican organizations. The network is characterized by pragmatic, transactional ties rather than deep loyalty, with many groups connected through multiple overlapping partnerships.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clerkenwell crime syndicate
top_orgs_detail.description: English criminal organization based in Clerkenwell, London, founded in the 1980s by Terry Adams and his brothers Tommy and Patrick. Involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and other serious crimes. Allegedly one of the most powerful syndicates in the UK with wealth up to £200 million. Influence has decreased since 2000.

top_orgs_detail.name: Marielitos
top_orgs_detail.description: Marielitos are Cuban immigrants who arrived in the United States during the 1980 Mariel boatlift, with a subset involved in organized crime. They are based in the United States, primarily Miami, and are known for drug and arms trafficking, gambling, and contract killing. The group has been active since the early 1980s and maintains alliances with other criminal organizations.

top_orgs_detail.name: No Limit Soldiers
top_orgs_detail.description: Organized crime group and street gang from Curaçao, originating in the impoverished neighborhood of Koraal Specht in Willemstad. Involved in high-intensity drug trafficking into the Netherlands, contract killings, and other criminal activities. Allegedly connected to the assassination of politician Helmin Wiels. Allies include Colombian cartels and Dutch Penose.

top_orgs_detail.name: Texis Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: The Texis Cartel was a Salvadoran criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, transporting drugs from Colombian cartels through northern El Salvador to North America. Founded in the 1990s by businessman José Adán Salazar Umaña, politician Juan Umaña Samayoa, and businessman Roberto Herrera, it bribed law enforcement and judicial officials. The cartel was effectively dismantled by Salvadoran authorities in 2018 after several leaders were arrested.

top_orgs_detail.name: Arifs
top_orgs_detail.description: The Arifs are a Turkish Cypriot criminal organization based in south-east London, involved in armed robbery, drug trafficking, and racketeering since the late 1960s. They rose to prominence after the Kray brothers' downfall and were considered Britain's leading crime family in the late 1980s. The gang was led by brothers Deniz, Mehmet, and Doğan Arif, but many members were convicted in the 1990s. They maintain ties with relatives in Turkey for drug shipments.

title: United Nations Gang Alliance Network
summary: This community is centered on the United Nations gang, a major Canadian criminal organization based in British Columbia. The gang has formed a broad coalition of smaller Asian-Canadian gangs, including the Big Circle Gang, Dhak-Duhre coalition, Crazy Dragon Killaz, and others. These alliances are primarily for drug trafficking, with the United Nations providing cocaine and oversight to groups like the Fresh Off the Boat Killers. The network operates mainly in British Columbia and Alberta, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and violence. The United Nations gang acts as a central hub, coordinating activities and maintaining control over its allied groups.
top_orgs: Big circle gang of British Columbia
Mo dumplings gang of British Columbia

top_orgs_detail.name: United Nations (gang)
top_orgs_detail.description: The United Nations (UN) is a criminal gang formed in Abbotsford, British Columbia, Canada in 1997 by Clayton Roueche and a group of high-school friends. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, extortion, money laundering, murder, and other criminal activities. It operates primarily in Canada and the United States, with alliances with various Asian organized crime groups and the Sinaloa Cartel. The gang remains active as of the present.

Mo dumplings gang of British Columbia
