title: Camorra Alliances in Campania
summary: This community consists of Camorra clans primarily based in the Campania region of Italy, particularly in the provinces of Avellino, Naples, and surrounding areas. The organizations are interconnected through a web of alliances, pacts, and shared criminal activities, including drug trafficking, extortion, and political corruption. Key players like Clan Cava, Clan Russo di Nola, and the Moccia clan form a central alliance, cooperating against common enemies and expanding their territorial influence. Historical feuds, such as that between Clan Cava and Clan Graziano, have shifted over time, with former rivals becoming allies or reaching non-aggression pacts. The community's cohesion is driven by mutual benefit, strategic marriages, and joint operations, reflecting the fluid and pragmatic nature of Camorra relationships.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Cava
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Cava is a Camorra syndicate based in Quindici, in the province of Avellino, Italy. It has been involved in a bloody feud with the rival Graziano clan for over thirty years. The clan's most influential bosses were the Cava brothers, including Emiliano, Lorenzo, Biagio, and Antonio. Despite state crackdowns and internal weakening, the clan remains active and is considered the most influential in the province of Avellino, with branches in Naples, Salerno, and other regions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Russo di Nola
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in Nola, Italy, founded by brothers Pasquale and Salvatore Russo in the 1980s. Originally lieutenants of Carmine Alfieri, they replaced the Alfieri clan. Despite arrests of top bosses, the clan remains active under a new generation and has expanded alliances, including with the Mazzarella clan of Naples.

top_orgs_detail.name: Moccia clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Moccia is the oldest and most powerful Camorra organization operating in the lower Liburia area, particularly in Afragola, Casoria, Arzano, Caivano and neighboring municipalities north and northeast of Naples. Founded by Gennaro Moccia in the 1960s, it was later led by his wife Anna Mazza, the first woman in Italy to face criminal proceedings for mafia crimes. The clan has a confederative structure with local groups and maintains a hegemonic presence in the territory, confirmed by multiple judicial investigations.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Fabbrocino
top_orgs_detail.description: Mafia-type criminal organization originating from the Vesuvian area of Italy, between Ottaviano, San Gennaro Vesuviano and San Giuseppe Vesuviano. Founded by Mario Fabbrocino in the late 1970s, it controls a vast territory and engages in diversified illegal activities including money laundering, extortion, and vote-buying. The clan has been involved in violent conflicts with rival organizations such as the Nuova Camorra Organizzata.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Graziano
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Graziano is a Camorra organization originating from Quindici, Italy. It has been involved in a violent conflict with the rival Cava clan since the 1980s, initially over post-earthquake reconstruction funds. The clan was part of the larger 'Clan dei Quindiciari' and later joined the Nuova Camorra Organizzata. It has been active since the 1960s and has managed to elect mayors in Quindici.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Sacco-Bocchetti
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Sacco-Bocchetti is a Camorra organization active in the northeastern suburbs of Naples, specifically in the San Pietro a Patierno and Secondigliano districts. It is named after Gennaro Sacco and Gaetano Bocchetti, the latter a founder of the Alleanza di Secondigliano. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking and extortion, with several members arrested or killed in recent years.

title: Piru Gang Alliance Sets
summary: This community consists primarily of Piru sets, which are part of the larger Bloods gang alliance, based in Compton, California. The organizations are tightly interconnected through alliances, with Fruit Town Piru acting as a central hub, cooperating with nearly all other sets. Cooperation involves mutual support in gang rivalries and criminal activities such as drug trafficking, robbery, and arms trafficking. The community also includes a few non-Piru gangs like Compton Varrio Largo 36 and Paybacc Crips, which are allied with Fruit Town Piru. Overall, this network represents a coalition of Bloods-affiliated gangs that collaborate for territorial control and shared criminal enterprises.

top_orgs_detail.name: Fruit Town Piru
top_orgs_detail.description: A set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods alliance, based in Compton, California. Formed in the 1970s, it is named after fruit-named streets in its neighborhood. Known for its rivalry with the Mob Piru Bloods during the early 2000s, and has been involved in murders and feuds. The gang has affiliations with rappers such as Soulja Boy and Chris Brown.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cedar Block Piru
top_orgs_detail.description: The Cedar Block Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods gang alliance, formed around 1980 in west Compton, California. It gained notability for its connection to rapper The Game, who has referenced the gang in his songs. The gang was involved in the 1992 murder of Allen King and his three children. Its territory includes multiple cities across the United States.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tree Top Piru
top_orgs_detail.description: The Tree Top Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods gang alliance. It was founded in Compton, California in the 1970s and has since spread to other parts of the United States. The gang is known for its connection to rappers such as DJ Quik and YG, and has been involved in violent crimes including murder and racketeering.

top_orgs_detail.name: Elm Street Piru
top_orgs_detail.description: The Elm Street Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods alliance. It originated in Compton, California, and has since spread to other parts of the United States, including Brooklyn, Atlanta, and Virginia. The gang is primarily African-American and has been involved in criminal activities such as recruiting high school students, conspiracy to murder a witness, and a fatal shooting during protests.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mob Piru
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mob Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance and a criminal organization, part of the larger Bloods alliance. Based in East Compton, California, it is primarily African American and involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and arms trafficking. Suge Knight, co-founder of Death Row Records, is an affiliate. The set split from Lueders Park Piru at some point.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lueders Park Piru
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lueders Park Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods alliance, based in Compton, California. Originating as the Lueders Park Hustlers, it was instrumental in forming the Bloods in 1972. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and weapons sales, with approximately 140 members in 2005. It has rivalries with several Crip sets and was allied with Mob Piru before 2009.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clan Alliance
summary: This community consists of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, primarily centered around the powerful 'Ndrina Gallace and 'Ndrina Ruga. These clans cooperate in drug trafficking, extortion, and other criminal activities, often forming alliances to dominate local territories and engage in feuds. Key ties include the Gallace-Ruga alliance and the Ruga-Metastasio-Leuzzi consortium, which have been involved in violent conflicts like the 'faida dei boschi'. The community's cooperation is reinforced by shared operations and mutual support against common enemies.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Gallace
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Gallace is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan originating from Guardavalle, Italy. Over the past 30 years, it has expanded its operations across Italy, including Tuscany, Lazio, Lombardy, and Rome. The clan is heavily involved in international drug trafficking, fraud, usury, and extortion. It has strong alliances with other 'Ndrangheta clans such as Farao-Marincola and Leuzzi.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Ruga
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Ruga is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Monasterace, Italy. It was dominant in the Stilaro Valley until the mid-1990s, involved in construction contracts, agricultural control, and kidnappings in the 1980s. The clan has been found in Baden-Württemberg, Germany, and allied with the Metastasio, Leuzzi, and Gallace-Novella clans. Its leadership shifted to the Metastasio brothers after the Ruga were imprisoned.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Loielo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Loielo are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Ariola, a frazione of Gerocarne, in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria. Their criminal activities include extortion and drug trafficking. They rose to power in the 1970s and were allied with the Maiolo family until a decade-long feud in the 1990s, which they won. In the 2000s, they lost dominance to the Emanuele family. The group was largely dismantled by the 2012 'Light in the Woods' operation.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Gallelli
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Gallelli is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization based in Badolato, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in extortion, illegal land guardianship, and fraudulently obtaining European Union funds. The clan has been active since at least the 1970s and has faced multiple police operations, including Itaca-Freeboat in 2013 and Pietranera in 2017, leading to arrests and convictions.

title: Naples Camorra Alliance Network
summary: This community is a coalition of Camorra clans from Naples, Italy, centered around the powerful Mazzarella clan. The clans cooperate primarily for territorial control, drug trafficking, and mutual defense against rival groups like the Alleanza di Secondigliano and the Paranza dei Bambini. Key alliances include the Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno cartel and the Mazzarella-Del Prete-Buonerba alliance. The Mazzarella clan serves as a central hub, with smaller clans like De Bernardo and Papi operating under its authority. The network's cooperation is driven by shared interests in dominating Naples' criminal underworld.

top_orgs_detail.name: Mazzarella clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Mazzarella clan is a powerful Camorra clan based in Naples, Italy, founded in the 1960s by the Mazzarella brothers. Initially involved in cigarette smuggling, it grew into a major criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. The clan has been involved in violent conflicts with rival Camorra groups and has international operations in Spain, France, and Peru.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Sarno
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Sarno was a Camorra criminal organization based in Naples, Italy, led by the Sarno brothers: Ciro, Vincenzo, Luciano, Giuseppe, and Pasquale. It formed in the early 1980s after the dissolution of the Nuova Famiglia groups, engaging in conflicts with the Alleanza di Secondigliano and later joining the Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno cartel. The clan was involved in territorial disputes and camorra wars, particularly against the De Luca Bossa clan.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Misso
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra clan operating in the Rione Sanità neighborhood of Naples, Italy. Founded in the 1980s by Giuseppe Misso, known as 'o nasone. Specializes in thefts, robberies of banks, post offices, and armored vans, as well as usury, counterfeiting, and gold trafficking. Engaged in violent feuds with rival clans, including a split in 2005 led by Salvatore Torino.

title: Japanese Yakuza Syndicate Alliances
summary: This community is dominated by the Yamaguchi-gumi, Japan's largest yakuza syndicate, which acts as a central hub connecting various other groups through alliances and affiliations. Many organizations, such as the Toa-kai, Matsuba-kai, and Inagawa-kai, are members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation, which facilitates cooperation among Tokyo-based gangs. The Yamaguchi-gumi has formal alliances with several groups, including the Aizukotetsu-kai, Kokusui-kai, and Soai-kai, often solidified through sakazuki ceremonies. Additionally, the Sumiyoshi-kai and Inagawa-kai maintain a loose alliance with the Yamaguchi-gumi, reflecting a broader network of inter-syndicate cooperation. These ties enable joint operations, territorial agreements, and conflict resolution within Japan's organized crime landscape.

top_orgs_detail.name: Yamaguchi-gumi
top_orgs_detail.description: Japan's largest yakuza organization, founded in 1915 by Harukichi Yamaguchi in Kobe. It engages in extortion, gambling, drug trafficking, and other criminal activities, with operations across Japan and overseas. Membership has declined significantly from its peak, with 3,100 active members as of 2025. The group is known for providing disaster relief after the 1995 Kobe earthquake and the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami.

top_orgs_detail.name: Toa-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Toa-kai is a yakuza syndicate based in Tokyo, Japan, founded in 1948 by Hisayuki Machii as the Tosei-kai. Originally a far-right anti-communist group, it became one of Tokyo's most powerful gangs during post-war economic growth. The syndicate is a member of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation and aligned with the Yamaguchi-gumi. As of the infobox, it has an estimated 20 members and is led by Yasushi Hayano.

top_orgs_detail.name: Matsuba-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Matsuba-kai is a yakuza organization based in Tokyo, Japan, founded in March 1953 from remnants of the Sekine-gumi and Fujita-gumi. It is a designated yakuza group with an estimated 280 active members as of 2024. The group has been involved in various criminal activities including heroin smuggling and violent feuds with rival gangs. It is a member of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

top_orgs_detail.name: Inagawa-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Inagawa-kai is the third largest yakuza group in Japan, based in the Kantō region. Founded in 1949 by Kakuji Inagawa in Atami, Shizuoka, it originated from traditional gamblers and engages in illegal gambling, drug trafficking, extortion, and prostitution. It was one of the first yakuza organizations to operate overseas and has provided disaster relief. As of 2025, it is led by Kazuya Uchibori.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sumiyoshi-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Sumiyoshi-kai is the second-largest yakuza group in Japan, based in Tokyo, with an estimated 2,100 members. It operates primarily in upscale districts such as Kabukichō and Ginza, engaging in protection rackets. Founded in 1958 by Jusaku Abe, it is a confederation of smaller gangs with a looser chain of command than its rival, the Yamaguchi-gumi. The group has undergone several name changes and reorganizations, and remains active as of 2020.

top_orgs_detail.name: Soai-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Soai-kai is a yakuza organization based in Chiba, Japan, formed in 1945 by Toramatsu Takahashi. Originally named the Gyosui-kai, it was renamed Takatora-gumi and later Soai-kai in 1955 when it became independent. It is a designated yakuza group with an estimated 80 active members and is one of the three dominant yakuza groups in Chiba Prefecture.

top_orgs_detail.name: Goto-gumi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Goto-gumi is a Japanese yakuza organization based in Fujinomiya, Shizuoka, founded in 1985 by Tadamasa Goto. It operates as a secondary affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi, Japan's largest yakuza syndicate. The group was involved in various criminal activities, and its founder was a prominent crime boss in Tokyo. The organization's status after Goto's expulsion in 2008 and death in 2026 is unclear.

top_orgs_detail.name: Honda-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Honda-kai were a Japanese yakuza gang active in Kobe in the mid-20th century. They were a bakuto gang primarily involved in illegal gambling. After World War II, they allied with the Yamaguchi-gumi but were soon absorbed and ceased to be independent. An American Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate may have reused the name in the mid-2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Fukuhaku-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Fukuhaku-kai is a yakuza organization based in Fukuoka, Japan, founded in 1985 by Akira Umedu. It is a designated yakuza group under the Organized Crime Countermeasures Law with an estimated membership of 60. The group operates in the Nakasu red-light district and has been involved in conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and other local syndicates.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kokusui-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Kokusui-kai is a Tokyo-based yakuza organization founded in 1958 by Masaji Morita. It has an estimated 1000 members and is known for controlling Tokyo's fashionable Ginza district. Despite its relatively low membership, it is considered wealthy and successful. It was originally a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai but became an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi in 2005.

top_orgs_detail.name: Aizukotetsu-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: A yakuza organization based in Kyoto, Japan, founded in 1868 by Senkichi Kousaka. Its name derives from the Aizu region and 'Kotetsu', a type of Japanese sword. In 1992 it was among the first syndicates designated under Japan's anti-boryokudan laws, and it challenged the legislation in court. At its peak in 1993 it had 1,600 members, but membership has since declined to about 40.

title: New Zealand Gang Alliance Network
summary: This community comprises a dense network of New Zealand-based outlaw motorcycle clubs and street gangs, including the Head Hunters, Black Power, Killer Beez, and Tribesmen. They are bound by mutual alliances and cooperation in drug trafficking, particularly methamphetamine manufacturing and distribution, as well as shared criminal activities like shootings and money laundering. The ties often stem from infobox ally listings and documented joint operations, such as the 2003 methamphetamine ring involving Head Hunters and King Cobras. Despite internal rivalries with groups like the Mongrel Mob, these organizations maintain strategic partnerships to strengthen their criminal enterprises across the North Island.

top_orgs_detail.name: Head Hunters Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: New Zealand outlaw motorcycle club formed in 1967 as a street gang in East Auckland, evolving into an MC in 1985. Based in Ellerslie with chapters across the North Island, it is involved in drug trafficking, theft, murder, and money laundering. The club has over 1,000 criminal convictions and is considered one of the most dangerous organized crime groups in New Zealand.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Power (New Zealand gang)
top_orgs_detail.description: Black Power is a prominent New Zealand gang formed in 1970 by Reitu Harris and Māori youth in Wellington. Members are predominantly Māori and Pasifika. The gang is involved in organized crime, including drug manufacturing and dealing, and has a presence in Australia and Bali. It was founded in response to the rival Mongrel Mob and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Killer Beez
top_orgs_detail.description: Large street gang based in South Auckland, New Zealand, founded in 2003 by Josh Masters as a spin-off of the Tribesmen gang. Engaged in drug manufacturing, drug sales, aggravated robbery, assassination, fraud, and money laundering. Known for their distinctive patch and motto 'AO' (All Out).

top_orgs_detail.name: New Zealand Nomads
top_orgs_detail.description: The New Zealand Nomads are a large organised criminal gang formed in Horowhenua, New Zealand in 1977 by Dennis Hines. Originally part of the Black Power gang, they split to form their own gang. They are involved in drug dealing, theft, murder, and extortion, and have a long-standing rivalry with the Mongrel Mob. The gang remains active with members in prison as of 2013.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tribesmen Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Tribesmen Motorcycle Club is a prominent outlaw motorcycle club based in the North Island of New Zealand, founded in 1980 in Murupara. They are known for their yellow colors and middle finger salute, and have chapters nationwide. The club has been involved in drug dealing, assault, and methamphetamine manufacturing. As of 2020, they are considered the dominant gang in Christchurch.

top_orgs_detail.name: King Cobras
top_orgs_detail.description: The King Cobras are a prominent Pacific gang in New Zealand, founded by Samoans in Ponsonby, Auckland in the 1950s. They are the oldest gang in New Zealand, with an estimated 400 members. The gang is involved in drug dealing, assault, and murder, and has a history of violence and firearm possession.

title: Serbian-Russian Criminal Alliance
summary: This community is centered around the Serb mafia in Scandinavia, which acts as a hub connecting various Serbian and Russian criminal organizations. The Serb Volunteer Guard, a paramilitary unit, provides a link to state-backed entities like the Serbian State Security Directorate and the Yugoslav People's Army, indicating a blend of organized crime and former military ties. Russian groups such as Tambovskaya Bratva, Obshchak, and the Brothers' Circle are allied through shared criminal networks, while Swedish gangs like Brödraskapet and Fucked For Life cooperate with the Serb mafia. The community's cooperation spans drug trafficking, arms smuggling, and paramilitary activities, reflecting a transnational alliance rooted in post-Yugoslav and post-Soviet criminal underworlds.

top_orgs_detail.name: Serb mafia in Scandinavia
top_orgs_detail.description: Organized crime group operating in Sweden, Norway, and Denmark, founded in the 1970s by Yugoslav guest-workers. Based in Stockholm and Copenhagen, it engages in cigarette smuggling, drug trafficking, robbery, and extortion. The gang gained media attention in the 1990s under leaders like Dragan Joksović and is allegedly led by Milan Ševo. It remains active with an estimated 250 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Serb Volunteer Guard
top_orgs_detail.description: Elite Serbian volunteer paramilitary unit founded and led by Željko Ražnatović (Arkan) on 11 October 1990. Fought in the Croatian and Bosnian Wars, known for discipline and effectiveness but also responsible for war crimes and massacres. Operated with backing from Serbian State Security Directorate and was disbanded in April 1996.

top_orgs_detail.name: Obshchak
top_orgs_detail.description: The Obshchak is a traditional umbrella organization of criminal groups in Estonia, functioning as a trade union that settles conflicts and establishes boundaries among groups like the Russian mafia and Estonian organized crime. Dominated by Nikolai Tarankov from 2003 until his murder in 2016, it funds legal fees and packages for imprisoned members. The organization has about ten member groups and has been involved in drug trafficking, tax fraud, and white-collar crime. Estonian police have disrupted its operations through arrests since the 2010s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tambovskaya Bratva
top_orgs_detail.description: Russian criminal organization based in Saint Petersburg, founded in 1988 by Vladimir Kumarin and others from Tambov Oblast. Engaged in protection rackets, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. Known for conflicts with rival gangs like the Malyshevskaya OGG. Despite denials, Kumarin is alleged to be the leader. The group remains active with around 600 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Fucked For Life
top_orgs_detail.description: Swedish criminal network founded by Daniel Maiorana, originally named Tumba Lords. Based in Tumba, Stockholm, the gang engages in car and motorcycle thefts, armed robberies, and drug trafficking. Membership estimated at around 40 in 2004. The group has been active since the late 1990s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Brothers' Circle
top_orgs_detail.description: The Brothers' Circle is a term used to refer to Russian organized crime, commonly the Russian mafia. It is a loose translation of the Russian term 'Bratva', meaning brotherhood. The group is composed of leaders and senior members of several criminal organizations based in Europe, sharing a common ideology based on the thief in law tradition. Its existence is debated, with some experts viewing it as a label for disparate gangs rather than a single organization.

top_orgs_detail.name: Brödraskapet
top_orgs_detail.description: Swedish prison gang founded on May 27, 1995, by inmates at Kumla maximum security prison. It is considered a criminal organization by Swedish police under EU criteria. The gang engages in drug and arms trafficking, robbery, murder, and extortion, with an estimated 60-70 members nationwide.

title: Khmer Rouge and Communist Allies
summary: This community centers on the Khmer Rouge and its network of communist and nationalist allies during the Cambodian Civil War and Cambodian-Vietnamese War. The Khmer Rouge, a genocidal regime, received support from China, the Chinese Communist Party, the Communist Party of Thailand, the People's Army of Vietnam, the Viet Cong, and the Pathet Lao. It also formed tactical alliances with non-communist groups like FUNCINPEC, KPNLF, and GRUNK to oppose Vietnamese forces. The ties are primarily military and logistical cooperation, with China and the Chinese Communist Party providing funding and arms. The community reflects the Cold War dynamics in Southeast Asia, where various factions united against common enemies despite ideological differences.

top_orgs_detail.name: Khmer Rouge
top_orgs_detail.description: The Khmer Rouge was a communist-led insurgent and later ruling party in Cambodia, founded in 1951 and led by Pol Pot. It ruled Cambodia from 1975 to 1979, implementing radical social engineering that resulted in the Cambodian genocide, with 1.5 to 2 million deaths. The group was defeated in 1979 but continued as an insurgency until 1999.

title: Stasi's Global Revolutionary Network
summary: This community is centered around the East German Stasi, which provided military training and advisory support to a diverse array of left-wing revolutionary and liberation movements during the Cold War. The Stasi's cooperation extended to African groups like ZANLA, FRELIMO, SWAPO, MPLA, and Umkhonto we Sizwe, as well as militant organizations in Latin America and the Middle East, such as the Manuel Rodríguez Patriotic Front, the Abu Nidal Organization, and the Black September Organization. Additionally, the Stasi trained European terrorist groups like the Rote Armee Fraktion. This network reflects the Stasi's role as a key supporter of anti-imperialist and communist movements worldwide.

title: North and West African Jihadist Network
summary: This community comprises jihadist militant groups operating primarily in North and West Africa, with Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (AQIM) and Boko Haram as central hubs. The organizations cooperate through alliances, joint operations, and coordination of activities such as kidnappings and attacks. Many are affiliates or allies of al-Qaeda or the Islamic State, sharing ideological goals and operational support. The network spans Algeria, Nigeria, Libya, Tunisia, and the Sahel, reflecting a transnational jihadist insurgency.
top_orgs: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa

top_orgs_detail.name: Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb
top_orgs_detail.description: Islamist militant organization active in North Africa and the Sahel, originating from the Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat (GSPC) which rebranded in 2007. It is an affiliate of al-Qaeda, primarily composed of Algerian and Saharan members, and funds itself largely through kidnapping for ransom. The group has been designated a terrorist organization by numerous countries and international bodies. In 2017, its Sahara branch merged with other groups to form Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

top_orgs_detail.name: Boko Haram
top_orgs_detail.description: Jihadist militant group based in northeastern Nigeria, founded in 2002 by Mohammed Yusuf. It seeks to establish an Islamic state and has been responsible for tens of thousands of deaths, mass abductions, and displacement of millions. The group split in 2016, with one faction becoming the Islamic State's West Africa Province. It remains active despite the death of long-time leader Abubakar Shekau in 2021.

top_orgs_detail.name: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa

all_orgs: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa

title: White Power Skinhead Network
summary: This community is a transnational network of neo-Nazi and white supremacist skinhead groups, centered around the Hammerskins, which acts as a hub connecting various factions. Cooperation primarily involves organizing white power music events, such as the White Boy Summer Fest co-hosted by Hammerskins, Blood & Honour, and Veren Laki, as well as sharing membership and affiliate status. The groups are ideologically aligned in promoting white separatism and racial hatred, with ties spanning the United States, Europe, and Australia. Despite some groups being defunct or leaderless, the network persists through music production and joint activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hammerskins
top_orgs_detail.description: Neo-Nazi group formed in 1988 in Dallas, Texas, focused on producing and promoting white power rock music. It is one of the most prominent American white power skinhead groups, with chapters in the US, Canada, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. The group is self-described as 'leaderless' and has been involved in violent attacks and hate crimes, though not centrally organized. Its website is defunct and online forums inactive as of 2020.

top_orgs_detail.name: Volksfront
top_orgs_detail.description: American white separatist organization founded in 1994 by Randal Lee Krager and Richard Arden while incarcerated in Oregon. It aimed to create an all-white homeland in the Pacific Northwest and had chapters in the US and several European countries. The group was considered neo-Nazi and a racist skinhead group, with about 50 members in the US. It dissolved in August 2012.

top_orgs_detail.name: Keystone United
top_orgs_detail.description: Neo-Nazi group based in Pennsylvania, founded in 2001 as the Keystone State Skinheads. It is one of the largest single-state racist skinhead crews in the United States, with chapters in multiple Pennsylvania cities. The group organizes white power concerts and family-oriented events, and changed its name to Keystone United in 2008.

title: Turkish Ultranationalist Criminal Network
summary: This community is centered around the Grey Wolves, a Turkish far-right ultranationalist movement with deep ties to organized crime and political violence. The Grey Wolves connect to various groups through ideological alignment, financial support, and operational cooperation, including the German-based Osmanen Germania gang, which served as security for Grey Wolves events. Other ties involve Syrian Turkmen brigades and Islamist factions, suggesting a network that blends ultranationalism with criminal activities and paramilitary operations. The community spans Turkey, Germany, and conflict zones like Syria, indicating a transnational reach.

top_orgs_detail.name: Grey Wolves
top_orgs_detail.description: The Grey Wolves are a Turkish far-right ultranationalist and neo-fascist political movement, officially the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP). Founded by Colonel Alparslan Türkeş in the late 1960s, they were heavily involved in political violence in Turkey in the 1970s, including the Maraş massacre. They have been designated as a terrorist organization by Kazakhstan and banned in Azerbaijan and France. The group is active in Turkey, Northern Cyprus, Western Europe, and post-Soviet states, and is suspected of ties to the Turkish deep state and organized crime.

top_orgs_detail.name: Osmanen Germania
top_orgs_detail.description: Turkish-nationalistic and extreme right criminal gang in Germany, formed between late 2014 and April 2015. It was involved in organized crime including assault, blackmail, prostitution, kidnapping, narcotics, and gun crime. The gang had connections to the Turkish AKP party and the neo-fascist Grey Wolves, and was banned by the German government in 2018.

title: Asian-American Gangs and Tongs Alliance
summary: This community is a network of Asian-American criminal organizations, primarily based in the United States, that have formed alliances for mutual protection and against common rivals. Central to the community is the Black Dragons, a Chinese-American gang that allies with several other groups, including the Asian Boyz, Vietnamese Boyz, Hop Sing Tong, and United Bamboo Triad. The community also includes historical tongs like the Suey Sing Association and Hop Sing Tong, which have cooperated against other tongs. These ties are driven by shared ethnic backgrounds, territorial interests, and opposition to rival groups such as the Wah Ching and Bing Kong Tong. The cooperation ranges from street-level gang alliances to formal tong partnerships, reflecting a blend of traditional organized crime and modern gang activity.

top_orgs_detail.name: Black Dragons
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese-American criminal organization and street gang formed in 1980 in Lincoln Heights, Los Angeles, by Chinese immigrants. Operated in Los Angeles and San Gabriel Valley, involved in organized crime (extortion, drug trafficking, counterfeiting) and street-level crime (murder, robbery, car theft). Peaked with over 100 core members, dismantled in 2002-2003 after FBI's Operation Back Door, but reportedly revived in 2020.

top_orgs_detail.name: Suey Sing Association
top_orgs_detail.description: The Suey Sing Association is a historical Chinese American association established in 1867. Formerly known as the Suey Sing Tong, it changed to its current name in 1920. It has branches in the United States and Canada, and in 1994 became the first Chinese community organization to fly the flag of the People's Republic of China. It currently has over 1000 members.

top_orgs_detail.name: Asian Boyz
top_orgs_detail.description: The Asian Boyz are a street gang based in Southern California, founded in the late 1980s by Cambodian refugees to protect themselves from other gangs. They have about 2,000 to 5,000 active members, predominantly Southeast Asian, and are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and homicide. The gang is active in 28 cities across 14 U.S. states and has ties to the Crips.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hop Sing Tong
top_orgs_detail.description: The Hop Sing Tong is a Chinese American Tong established in 1875. It has multiple branches across the United States, including in San Francisco, Los Angeles, Portland, Seattle, and Denver. Some branches are now defunct.

top_orgs_detail.name: Joe Boys
top_orgs_detail.description: The Joe Boys were a Chinese American youth gang founded in the 1960s in San Francisco's Chinatown by Joe Fong, a former Wah Ching member. They were involved in drug trafficking, extortion, robbery, and murder. The gang was active from 1969 to 1977 and had ties to the Suey Sing Tong while rivaling the Wah Ching and Hop Sing Tong.

title: Palestinian Militant and Leftist Network
summary: This community centers on the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP), a Marxist-Leninist faction that opposes Israel and advocates a one-state solution. The PFLP is allied with the Popular Resistance Committees, the Palestinian People's Party, and the Japanese Red Army, and it received financial support from the Danish far-left Blekingegade Gang. The Doghmush clan, a Gaza-based criminal group, is affiliated with both Fatah and the Popular Resistance Committees, linking organized crime to militant networks. These ties reflect a blend of ideological solidarity, operational cooperation, and criminal financing, primarily aimed at supporting Palestinian armed struggle.
top_orgs: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine

top_orgs_detail.name: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
top_orgs_detail.description: Palestinian Marxist-Leninist organization founded in 1967 by George Habash. It is the second-largest faction in the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) and opposes recognition of Israel, advocating a one-state solution. The group pioneered aircraft hijackings in the late 1960s-70s and its military wing is the Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades. Designated a terrorist organization by the US, Japan, Canada, and the EU.

top_orgs_detail.name: Doghmush clan
top_orgs_detail.description: A Palestinian clan from the Gaza Strip involved in extortion, smuggling, arms dealing, and killings. Known for its long-running rivalry with Hamas and affiliation with various militant groups including Fatah, Hamas, and Al-Qaeda. The clan was behind the 2007 kidnapping of British journalist Alan Johnston. Many members were killed in Israeli airstrikes during the 2023 Gaza war.

top_orgs_detail.name: Blekingegade Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Danish far-left criminal group active from 1972 to 1989, which committed robberies to finance the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP). The group was known for its extreme secrecy, meticulous planning, and use of disguises. One police officer was killed during their final robbery in 1988. The gang was disbanded after the discovery of a weapons cache on Blekingegade in 1989.

all_orgs: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Alliance Network
summary: This community consists of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, centered around the D'Agostino and De Stefano families. The D'Agostino 'ndrina acts as a key connector, linking the Serraino-Condello-Imerti and Commisso-Cordì-Aquino groups, and cooperating with the Gullace-Raso-Albanese clan in the Canolo locale. The De Stefano clan is allied with both D'Agostino and the Valle clan, which is also tied to the Lampada family. The Santapaola family is closely linked to De Stefano, indicating a broader network of cooperation in drug trafficking and other illicit activities. These ties reflect historical alliances and territorial control within the 'Ndrangheta structure.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina D'Agostino
top_orgs_detail.description: The D'Agostino 'ndrina is a powerful Calabrian Mafia clan based in Rosarno, Laureana di Borrello, Sant'Ilario dello Ionio, and Canolo, Italy. It operates in drug trafficking and has been historically important within the 'Ndrangheta, serving as a link between Reggio and Locri factions. The clan has been involved in feuds and is currently largely incarcerated but still influential.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Valle
top_orgs_detail.description: The Valle clan, also known as the Valle-Lampada clan, is a 'Ndrangheta family originally from Reggio Calabria, Italy, that established itself in Vigevano and Cisliano in the 1980s. Allied with the Lampada and De Stefano families, they are active in real estate, gambling, and restaurant businesses. The clan faced arrests in the 1990s and had assets confiscated, including a restaurant property later assigned to the municipality of Cisliano for social reuse.

title: Ndrangheta Pino-Sena Alliance Network
summary: This community consists of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, centered around the 'Ndrina Pino. The Pino clan formed alliances with the Basile-Calvano, Cirillo, and Muto clans during a feud against the Perna-Pranno-Vitelli group in the late 1970s-1980s. The Muto clan also cooperated with Chirillo in international drug trafficking. Later, the remnants of the Perna-Cicero-Pranno faction united with the Pino-Sena group. These ties reflect shifting alliances and ongoing cooperation in drug trafficking and other criminal activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Pino
top_orgs_detail.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. It was formed after the death of Luigi Palermo in 1977 and split into two factions, leading to a long feud with the Perna clan. The clan was involved in drug trafficking, arms possession, and international criminal activities, including a bid for the construction of Bucharest airport in Romania. In 1995, boss Franco Pino became a pentito (collaborator), leading to arrests. As of the 2000s, the clan has merged with the Perna group.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Muto
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Muto is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Cetraro. It is involved in illegal activities in the tourism sector, the fish market, drug trafficking, and has connections in the Cilento area of Campania. The group was allied with the Pino-Sena in a feud against the Perna-Pranno-Vitelli in the late 1970s-1980s, resulting in 27 deaths. It has also been implicated in the sinking of radioactive waste ships.

title: New Zealand A-Team Alliance
summary: This community consists of eight outlaw motorcycle clubs, primarily based in New Zealand, united under the A-Team alliance. The central hub is the Lone Legion Brotherhood, which has direct cooperation ties with all other members, including the Lost Breed Motorcycle Club, Epitaph Riders MC, Forty-Fives MC, Outcasts MC, Satan's Slaves MC, Sinn Fein MC, and Southern Vikings MC. The alliance likely facilitates mutual support, territorial control, and collective action against rival gangs. Notably, the Lone Legion Brotherhood and Lost Breed MC have since patched over to larger international clubs, indicating the A-Team's role as a stepping stone or local coalition within the broader outlaw motorcycle club landscape.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lone Legion Brotherhood
top_orgs_detail.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Blenheim, New Zealand, established in the 1970s. It controlled Blenheim as its exclusive territory until around 2007. Involved in a fatal shooting in 2007 that led to a murder conviction. The club was a member of the A-Team alliance and reportedly patched over to the Outlaws MC in 2019.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lost Breed Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Nelson, New Zealand, founded in 1976. The club claimed opposition to methamphetamine and domestic violence, though police viewed these claims skeptically. It was shut down in December 2015 when members patched over to the Hells Angels.

title: Ecuadorian Narco-Alliances with Albanian Link
summary: This community is dominated by Ecuadorian criminal organizations that splintered from Los Choneros, including Los Tiguerones, Los Lobos, Los Chone Killers, and Los Pipos, which have formed shifting alliances such as the Nueva Generación coalition. They cooperate in drug trafficking, contract killings, and terrorist attacks, often in response to state crackdowns. The Albanian network Kompania Bello connects them to European cocaine distribution, while FARC 48th Front and Los del norte provide additional Latin American ties. Their cooperation is driven by shared origins, territorial disputes, and the need for transnational logistics.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Tiguerones
top_orgs_detail.description: Ecuadorian narcoterrorist and criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. It split from Los Choneros in 2021 after the assassination of their leader. Based in Esmeraldas, with operations in Guayaquil and Manta. Led by William Jofre Alcívar Bautista, a former prison guard. Gained notoriety for the armed takeover of TC Televisión in January 2024.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Lobos
top_orgs_detail.description: Ecuadorian criminal and terrorist organization specializing in drug trafficking and contract killing. Formed in 2020 as a splinter group of Los Choneros after the assassination of its leader. Operates primarily in Latacunga, Cuenca, Machala, and Pastaza province, with over 8,000 members. Involved in major prison riots and the assassination of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Chone Killers
top_orgs_detail.description: Ecuadorian criminal and narcoterrorist organization dedicated to drug trafficking, contract killing, and other crimes. Originally an armed wing of Los Choneros, it split off in 2020 after the murder of leader Jorge Luis Zambrano. Based in Guayas province with operations centered in Durán. The group has been involved in prison massacres and terrorist attacks, and maintains a territorial dispute with the Latin Kings since 2023.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kompania Bello
top_orgs_detail.description: Transnational Albanian-speaking cocaine trafficking network that organized shipments from South America to Europe and coordinated wholesale distribution across EU member states. It negotiated directly with South American cartels and used encrypted communications and informal value transfer systems. The network was dismantled in September 2020 following a five-year multi-country investigation codenamed Operation Los Blancos, resulting in over 100 arrests and seizures of nearly 4 tonnes of cocaine.

title: Brazilian Criminal Alliances Network
summary: This community is centered on Comando Vermelho, one of Brazil's largest criminal organizations, which forms alliances with multiple regional gangs and international cartels. Bala na Cara, Primeiro Grupo Catarinense, and Família do Norte are Brazilian groups that cooperate with Comando Vermelho in drug trafficking and other illicit activities. The Santa Cruz Cartel from Bolivia provides international connections, while Milícia (Brazil) represents a hybrid of paramilitary and criminal elements. These ties facilitate the flow of drugs, weapons, and money across Brazil and into neighboring countries, creating a powerful syndicate that dominates local and regional crime.

top_orgs_detail.name: Comando Vermelho
top_orgs_detail.description: One of the two largest criminal organizations in Brazil, alongside the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC). Founded in 1979 at the Cândido Mendes prison on Ilha Grande, Rio de Janeiro. Engages in drug trafficking, organized crime, and operates in multiple Brazilian states and neighboring countries. Known for its 'caixa comum' (common fund) financed by member contributions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bala na Cara
top_orgs_detail.description: Bala na Cara is a Brazilian criminal organization based in Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul, founded in the early 2000s. It is one of the two largest and most violent criminal groups in the state, involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and murders. The group is known for extreme violence, often executing victims with multiple shots to the face. It has allied with Comando Vermelho and expanded operations to other states and countries.

top_orgs_detail.name: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense
top_orgs_detail.description: The largest criminal organization in the Brazilian state of Santa Catarina, founded on March 3, 2003, in the Florianópolis penitentiary. It commands rebellions, robberies, kidnappings, murders, and drug trafficking, operating mainly in Santa Catarina with connections in at least three other Brazilian states and borders with Paraguay, Bolivia, and Peru. The group has 14 leaders divided into two 'ministries' and is modeled after the Comando Vermelho and Primeiro Comando da Capital.

top_orgs_detail.name: Família do Norte
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal organization founded in 2007 in Manaus, Amazonas, led by Zé Roberto da Compensa, João Pinto Carioca, and Gelson Carnaúba. It was one of the largest criminal groups in Amazonas, involved in drug trafficking, assassinations, robberies, extortion, and prison rebellions. The group engaged in a violent conflict with the PCC from 2015 onward, culminating in the 2016 prison massacres. It became defunct around 2021 after losing territory to the Comando Vermelho.

top_orgs_detail.name: Santa Cruz Cartel
top_orgs_detail.description: Bolivian drug cartel headquartered in Santa Cruz de la Sierra, one of the largest in the country. Active since the 1980s, it has been involved in narco-terrorist incidents including assassination attempts on DEA agents and bombing attempts on politicians. The cartel is allied with Colombian, Brazilian, and Mexican drug trafficking organizations and engages in turf wars with smaller gangs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Milícia (Brazil)
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal organizations operating in low-income urban communities, initially in Rio de Janeiro, formed from the 2000s. They extort residents and control illegal services like gas, cable TV, and gambling, often composed of off-duty or active police, firefighters, and other security personnel. They have expanded rapidly, now controlling more territory than drug trafficking factions in Rio.

title: Sinaloa Cartel Armed Wings
summary: This community consists of armed wings and enforcer groups affiliated with the Sinaloa Cartel, primarily operating in northern Mexico. Organizations like Gente Nueva, Artistas Asesinos, Los Ninis, and Los Ántrax serve as hit squads and security forces for cartel leaders, while Cártel Independiente de Sonora recently split from the Sinaloa Cartel due to internal disputes. Cooperation ties are based on alliances within the cartel's network, with groups supporting each other in turf wars against rivals such as the Juárez Cartel. The community reflects the fragmented and violent nature of the Sinaloa Cartel's internal structure, with shifting loyalties and ongoing conflicts.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gente Nueva
top_orgs_detail.description: Gente Nueva is the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel, founded in 1995 in Sonora, Mexico. It consists of well-trained gunmen who battle rival cartels, particularly the Juárez Cartel and its enforcer La Línea, for control of smuggling routes in Ciudad Juárez. The group is involved in drug trafficking, kidnapping, and murder, and has been active in the Sinaloa Cartel's turf war since 2008.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cártel Independiente de Sonora
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal federation dedicated to drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other crimes. Originally formed as Gente Nueva, an elite armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel created to combat the Juárez Cartel in northwestern Mexico. In 2023, it split from the Sinaloa Cartel due to internal disputes with Los Chapitos and became independent. Based in Sonora, it operates in Chihuahua, Sonora, and Sinaloa.

top_orgs_detail.name: Artistas Asesinos
top_orgs_detail.description: Mexican criminal organization founded in 2008 by Jorge Ernesto Sáenz and Éder Ángel Martínez, operating as an armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Ciudad Juárez and the El Paso metropolitan area. Known for their artistic skills in drawing and graffiti, they engage in homicide, drug dealing, extortion, and robbery. Currently one of the main violent groups in Chihuahua, controlling part of the Cereso 3 prison and in conflict with rival groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Ninis
top_orgs_detail.description: Los Ninis are the main armed wing of the Los Chapitos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel, based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, Mexico. Composed of young hitmen aged 20-35, they serve as security forces, torturers, and enforcers for Los Chapitos leaders. They are also tasked with monitoring drug labs, camps, and trafficking routes. The group is led by Néstor Isidro Pérez Salas (alias "El Nini") and is responsible for an internal war within the Sinaloa Cartel to impose Los Chapitos' dominance.

top_orgs_detail.name: Los Ántrax
top_orgs_detail.description: Los Ántrax is a large enforcer unit and hit squad for the Sinaloa Cartel, based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, Mexico. Led by figures such as José Rodrigo Aréchiga Gamboa and Jesús Victoriano, the group is responsible for numerous homicides and provides armed security for drug lord Ismael El Mayo Zambada. It is considered the Sinaloa Cartel's largest and deadliest enforcer unit.

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Clan Alliances
summary: This community consists of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy, primarily from the provinces of Catanzaro, Vibo Valentia, and Reggio Calabria. The central node is the 'Ndrina Sia, which is allied with the Vallelunga, Sia-Procopio-Lentini, and Novella clans, forming a coalition that has been involved in violent feuds, such as the 2008-2010 conflict. The Vallelunga clan, historically dominant in the mountainous area, is allied with the Turrà clan and has ties to Sia-Procopio-Lentini. The Costa and Curciarello clans have a shifting alliance, with Curciarello initially allied with Costa, then switching to the rival Commisso clan during a feud, before returning to Costa. Overall, this community reflects the complex network of alliances and rivalries typical of 'Ndrangheta clans in Calabria, often centered on territorial control and drug trafficking.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Sia
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Sia is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Soverato, Calabria, Italy, with influence in neighboring towns. It is allied with other 'ndrine and was involved in a bloody feud from 2008 to 2010 that resulted in about twenty deaths. The clan has been targeted by multiple police operations, including Showdown, Showdown 2, and Showdown 3, leading to arrests and asset seizures. Its boss Vittorio Sia was killed in 2010.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Vallelunga
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Vallelunga, also known as the Viperari, is a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Serra San Bruno, Calabria, Italy. Active since the 1960s, it is considered the dominant clan in the mountainous area spanning the provinces of Catanzaro, Vibo Valentia, and Reggio Calabria. Historically allied with the Turrà clan, it has been involved in violent feuds for territorial control, notably the Faida dei boschi in the late 1980s, which solidified its power.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Costa
top_orgs_detail.description: The Costa are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Siderno, Italy. Initially allied with the Commisso clan, they later became rivals. They were involved in drug trafficking in Turin, Gorizia, and Modena. A war with the Commisso clan in the late 1980s and early 1990s led to the deaths of several Costa members. The clan is now largely defunct, with surviving members imprisoned or cooperating with authorities.

title: Haitian Gang Coalition Viv Ansanm
summary: This community consists of major Haitian criminal organizations united under the Viv Ansanm coalition, formed in 2024. Led by the Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies, the coalition includes G-Pèp, Gran Grif, and other gangs like 400 Mawozo, Zoe Nation, and Zoe Mafia Family. These groups cooperate to control territory, engage in kidnappings, drug trafficking, and violence, and have destabilized Haiti, leading to political crises. The alliance amplifies their collective power against state authorities.
top_orgs: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies

top_orgs_detail.name: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies
top_orgs_detail.description: A federation of 12 armed groups in Haiti led by former police officer Jimmy 'Barbecue' Chérizier, founded in June 2020. It controls over 80% of Port-au-Prince and is involved in assassinations, drug trafficking, and other criminal activities. In March 2024, it orchestrated a jailbreak that led to the resignation of Prime Minister Ariel Henry.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gran Grif
top_orgs_detail.description: Gran Grif is the largest and most powerful criminal organization in the Artibonite department of Haiti, based in Savien. Founded around 2016 by former congressman Prophane Victor, it engages in massacres, kidnappings, sexual violence, and forced recruitment of children. The group was responsible for 80% of reported civilian deaths in Artibonite in 2022 and has been designated a terrorist organization by the Dominican Republic and the United States.

top_orgs_detail.name: 400 Mawozo
top_orgs_detail.description: The 400 Mawozo is the largest gang in Haiti, based in Ganthier and Port-au-Prince suburbs. Founded around 2020, it consists of deportees, former smugglers, and police officers. The gang gained international attention in 2021 for kidnapping U.S. missionaries. Its leader, Wilson Joseph, was extradited to the United States, and the gang aligned with G-Pèp in 2022.

all_orgs: Revolutionary Forces of the G9 Family and Allies

title: Biker Gangs and Organized Crime
summary: This community brings together Canadian outlaw biker gangs like the Rockers Motor Club with American organized crime groups such as the Cohen crime family, National Crime Syndicate, and Minneapolis-based syndicates. The Rockers Motor Club, an enforcement arm for the Hells Angels, is allied with the Evil Ones MC and has ties to the National Crime Syndicate and Minneapolis Combination. The Cohen crime family, led by Mickey Cohen, worked under the National Crime Syndicate and also funded the Irgun paramilitary group. The Minneapolis Combination and Minneapolis Syndicate cooperated by dividing territories. These connections suggest a network of mutual support and cooperation across different criminal enterprises, from biker gangs to traditional organized crime and even political violence.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rockers Motor Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Canadian outlaw biker gang and support club for the Hells Angels, founded in 1992 by Maurice 'Mom' Boucher in Montreal. It served as an enforcement arm and recruitment tool for the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War, carrying out drug trafficking, assaults, and murders. The group was active until 2001.

title: Sydney Gangland War Alliances
summary: This community centers on the Alameddine crime network, a major drug-trafficking organization in Western Sydney, and its allies during the Sydney gangland war of the early 2020s. The Alameddines have cooperative ties with the Elmir family, Haouchar crime family, Hauochar Clan, and KVT, who serve as muscle. The Ahmad family is allied with both the Alameddines and the rival Hamzy/Hamze crime family, indicating a bridging role. The community reflects a network of shifting alliances and mentorship, such as Rafat Alameddine learning from Bilal Haouchar.

top_orgs_detail.name: Alameddine crime network
top_orgs_detail.description: Australian organised crime group based in Merrylands, Western Sydney, led by Rafat Alameddine. Allegedly one of Sydney's largest drug-trafficking organisations, earning around $1 million weekly profit at its peak. Involved in a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family during the Sydney gangland war of the early 2020s. Some members have links to Islamic extremism.

title: Facchineri-Led 'Ndrangheta Alliance
summary: This community is a coalition of 'Ndrangheta 'ndrine from Calabria, Italy, centered around the powerful 'Ndrina Facchineri. The Facchineri clan, based in Cittanova, has forged alliances with six smaller 'ndrine—Ladini, Marvaso, Mercuri-Ferraro, Monteleone, Sorbara, and Valente—primarily in the San Giorgio Morgeto area. These ties, often formed during feuds like the first faida, suggest a hierarchical structure where Facchineri provides leadership and protection while the allied groups offer local support and manpower. Their cooperation likely facilitates joint criminal activities such as drug trafficking and extortion, strengthening their collective influence in the region.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Facchineri
top_orgs_detail.description: Historical 'ndrina based in Cittanova, Calabria, operating since the late 19th century. Engages in extortion, commercial activities, drug trafficking, and past heroin trafficking and kidnappings. Involved in feuds with rival clans, notably the Raso-Albanese-Gullace group. Has expanded to other Italian regions including Puglia, Umbria, and Lazio.

title: Brazilian Militia and Allied Groups
summary: This community centers on Brazilian militias, paramilitary groups composed of current and former police officers and politicians, which engage in extortion and protection rackets in Rio de Janeiro. The militias have alliances with various Brazilian extremist groups such as Carecas do ABC, Carecas do Subúrbio, Neo-integralist groups, and Bicheiros, as well as with international supporters like Diehard Duterte Supporters. These ties suggest a network of far-right, anticommunist, and paramilitary organizations that cooperate ideologically and operationally. The community reflects a convergence of criminal and political extremism in Brazil.

top_orgs_detail.name: Brazilian militias
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian militias are illegal paramilitary groups composed mainly of current and former police officers, firefighters, and politicians, operating as mafia-like organizations. Based primarily in Rio de Janeiro, they engage in extortion, protection rackets, and other organized crime activities, often displacing drug gangs to control favelas. They emerged in the late 2000s from death squads rooted in the Brazilian military dictatorship. The groups maintain deep political connections and have been implicated in high-profile assassinations.

top_orgs_detail.name: Carecas do ABC
top_orgs_detail.description: A skinhead gang dissident from Carecas do Subúrbio, formed in the Greater ABC region of Brazil, primarily in Santo André. Currently present in São Paulo and other Brazilian capitals. The group is territorial, moderately politicized, anticommunist, and antiliberal, advocating violence against perceived 'rotten sectors of society'. It admits black and northeastern members, distinguishing it from other skinhead groups.

title: Abdel-Kader Family Network
summary: This community is centered around the Abdel-Kader family, which cooperates with six Jewish crime families and syndicates, including the Abutbul, Alperon, Dumrani, and Shirazi families, as well as the Amir Molnar and Zeev Rosenstein syndicates. The ties are all described as cooperation between the Abdel-Kader family and each Jewish crime organization, suggesting a pattern of cross-ethnic alliances. The Abdel-Kader family acts as a hub, connecting multiple Jewish crime groups, likely for mutual benefit in illicit activities. The community appears to be a coalition of organized crime families from different backgrounds, united through the central Abdel-Kader node.

title: Chinese-American and Asian Criminal Networks
summary: This community is centered on Chinese-American organized crime, with the Hip Sing Association and its affiliated Flying Dragons gang at the core. The Hip Sing Association, a traditional Chinese tong, cooperated with the Kuomintang and the Office of Policy Coordination in drug trafficking. The Flying Dragons served as enforcers for the Hip Sing, while the Vietnamese gang Born to Kill initially worked as enforcers for the Flying Dragons. The Green Gang, a Shanghai-based triad, financially supported the Kuomintang and is listed as an ally of the Dutch Afro-Curaçaoan Hopi Boys, suggesting a transnational link. Overall, these ties reflect historical alliances between Chinese criminal organizations, political entities, and other ethnic gangs, primarily for drug trafficking and enforcement.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hip Sing Association
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese-American criminal organization formed as a labor group in New York City's Chinatown around 1904. Involved in Tong wars with rivals and drug trafficking with the Kuomintang. Established chapters across the US. Some branches have recently transitioned to legitimate fraternal organizations.

top_orgs_detail.name: Flying Dragons
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the 1970s to early 1990s. Formed in 1967 by immigrants from Hong Kong, affiliated with the Hip Sing Tong. Engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and illegal gambling. Heavily involved in heroin trafficking after the Pizza Connection prosecutions. Indicted on federal racketeering charges in 1994, effectively dismantled.

top_orgs_detail.name: Green Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The Green Gang was a Chinese secret society and criminal triad organization prominent in Shanghai during the early to mid 20th century. It originated from the Luojiao Buddhist sect and grain transport boatmen along the Grand Canal. The gang engaged in criminal activities such as opium trafficking and operated through networks of hometown associations. Key leaders included Du Yuesheng and Huang Jinrong.

top_orgs_detail.name: Born to Kill (gang)
top_orgs_detail.description: Born to Kill was a New York City-based street gang composed of first-generation Vietnamese immigrants, active in the 1980s and early 1990s. Founded by Tho Hoang "David" Thai in 1988, the gang engaged in drug trafficking, counterfeiting, contract killing, extortion, and other crimes. Its peak membership in New York ranged from 75 to 100. The gang was largely dismantled by 1992 following arrests and prosecutions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Hopi Boys
top_orgs_detail.description: The Hopi Boys are an Afro-Curaçaoan criminal group based in the south-east region of Amsterdam, Netherlands. They are one of the most influential gangs in the Dutch capital, controlling rackets, drug dealing, and prostitution. The group is predominantly Afro-Curaçaoan and has allies such as the Green gang, while rivaling the Kloekhorststraat Gang.

title: Islamic State and Its Affiliates
summary: This community centers on the Islamic State (ISIS) and its network of allied groups and enablers. The ties include former al-Qaeda in Iraq operatives who merged into ISIS, Iraqi insurgent groups like the Army of the Men of the Naqshbandi Order and the General Military Council for Iraqi Revolutionaries that allied with ISIS, a Russian GTA gang formed to train fighters for ISIS, and the French cement company Lafarge that paid ISIS for facility operations. The cooperation spans ideological alignment, tactical support, and financial transactions, reflecting ISIS's ability to attract diverse partners across regions and sectors.
top_orgs: General Military Council for Iraqi Revolutionaries
Army of the Men of the Naqshbandi Order

top_orgs_detail.name: Islamic State
top_orgs_detail.description: Transnational Salafi jihadist militant organization and unrecognized quasi-state that proclaimed a worldwide caliphate in 2014. Originating from al-Qaeda in Iraq, it controlled large territories in Iraq and Syria from 2013 to 2019, enforcing extremist Islamic law. Known for brutal tactics including genocides, beheadings, and terrorist attacks globally. After losing its Middle Eastern territories, it reverted to insurgency and maintains affiliates in Africa and Asia.

top_orgs_detail.name: GTA gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The GTA gang was a violent gang of murderers and terrorists based near Moscow, Russia, formed in March 2012 by Ibaydullo Subkhanov to train soldiers for ISIL. They robbed and murdered motorists on the M-4 highway using caltrops, and were responsible for 17 murders. The gang was captured in a police raid on 6 November 2014, with Subkhanov killed.

top_orgs_detail.name: General Military Council for Iraqi Revolutionaries
Army of the Men of the Naqshbandi Order
Al-Qaeda in Iraq
top_orgs_detail.description: Salafi jihadist terrorist organization founded on 17 October 2004 by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, operating in Iraq. It was affiliated with al-Qaeda and aimed to expel U.S.-led coalition forces and establish an Islamic state. The group was disbanded on 15 October 2006 when it merged into the Islamic State of Iraq.

all_orgs: Army of the Men of the Naqshbandi Order
General Military Council for Iraqi Revolutionaries

title: Gosha-kai Anti-Yamaguchi Federation
summary: This community consists of five Japanese yakuza groups—Kyodo-kai, Goda-ikka, Kyosei-kai, Shinwa-kai, and Asano-gumi—united as members of the Gosha-kai federation, an alliance formed to oppose the dominant Yamaguchi-gumi. The groups are based in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, with Kyosei-kai playing a leading role in founding the federation. Their cooperation is primarily through their shared membership in Gosha-kai, which coordinates anti-Yamaguchi activities and mutual support. The ties among them are almost exclusively based on this federation membership, highlighting a strategic alliance against a common rival.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kyodo-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Kyodo-kai is a yakuza group based in Hiroshima, Japan, formed in January 1969 by Kokichi Morita after the disbanding of the Takahashi-gumi. It is a designated yakuza group with an estimated 60 active members and is the second largest in the Chugoku region. The group has been involved in an anti-Yamaguchi federation called the Gosha-kai since 1996.

top_orgs_detail.name: Goda-ikka
top_orgs_detail.description: The Goda-ikka is a yakuza group based in Yamaguchi, Japan, founded in 1948 as the Goda-gumi by Koichi Goda. It is the largest designated yakuza group in Yamaguchi Prefecture, with an estimated 30 members. The group has been a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Gosha-kai since 1996.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kyosei-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Kyosei-kai is a yakuza group based in Hiroshima, Japan, formed in May 1964 from seven yakuza clans united by bakuto Tatsuo Yamamura. It is known for its history of fierce conflicts with other yakuza groups, particularly the Yamaguchi-gumi, and is thought to be responsible for Hiroshima's 'town of violence' image. The group has been a leading member of anti-Yamaguchi federations and remains active.

top_orgs_detail.name: Shinwa-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Shinwa-kai is a yakuza group based in Takamatsu, Kagawa, Japan, with an estimated 30 active members. It was renamed from the Takamatsu Shinwa-kai in 1971 and became a designated yakuza group in December 1992. It is the only designated yakuza group based in the Shikoku region and has been a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Gosha-kai since 1996.

top_orgs_detail.name: Asano-gumi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Asano-gumi is a yakuza group based in Okayama, Japan. It was formed in 1945 as the Oyama-ikka and restarted as the Asano-gumi in 1952. The group has been involved in conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and is a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Gosha-kai. It is a designated yakuza group with about 40 members.

title: Hong Kong Triad and Pro-Beijing Alliances
summary: This community is centered on Fujianese organized crime and the Big Circle Gang, two major Chinese criminal syndicates operating in Hong Kong and internationally. They are allied with each other and with the Red Wa, a triad group, and cooperate with Thai crime bosses (Chao pho) for drug trafficking. Additionally, Fujianese organized crime has ties to pro-Beijing groups like the Hong Kong Federation of Trade Unions and the Grand Alliance for Safeguarding Hong Kong, indicating a blend of criminal and political alliances. The community's cooperation spans drug trafficking, human trafficking, and racketeering, with a focus on Hong Kong and Southeast Asia.
top_orgs: Hong Kong Federation of Trade Unions
Grand Alliance for Safeguarding Hong Kong

top_orgs_detail.name: Fujianese organized crime
top_orgs_detail.description: Fujianese organized crime refers to crime syndicates composed of Fujianese people, similar to triad gangs. The term primarily describes Fujianese immigrant gangs in Hong Kong but also includes native crime syndicates in Fujian province. They have been involved in attacks against Hong Kong pro-democracy protesters and collaborate with various criminal organizations and pro-Beijing groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Big Circle Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: Chinese triad formed in the late 1960s in Chinese prison camps by former Red Guards, later relocating to Hong Kong in the 1970s. Known for extreme violence, use of firearms, and sophisticated criminal operations including drug trafficking, human trafficking, and racketeering. Active internationally in Canada, the United States, Australia, and Malaysia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Chao pho
top_orgs_detail.description: Chao pho is a term for organized crime bosses in Thailand, based in provinces with both legitimate and criminal business interests. They are involved in activities such as prostitution, drug trafficking, and illegal gambling, and often cooperate with the United Wa State Army. They maintain close ties with bureaucrats, police, military, and local politicians, and play a role in elections.

Grand Alliance for Safeguarding Hong Kong

title: Calabrian 'Ndrangheta Alliances in Northern Italy
summary: This community consists of 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, primarily based in Petilia Policastro and Marcedusa, that have expanded into northern Italy, especially Milan. They cooperate in drug trafficking, money laundering, and territorial control, forming strategic alliances such as the Coco-Trovato group and the Comberiati-Carvelli axis. Key ties include the Trovato family's alliances with Carvelli, Comberiati, and Caligiuri, as well as the Carvelli-Comberiati partnership in Milan's Quarto Oggiaro neighborhood. The community is involved in violent feuds and high-profile operations, reflecting the transnational reach of the 'Ndrangheta.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Trovato
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Trovato is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Marcedusa, Calabria, Italy. Active in northern Italy since the early 1980s, particularly in Milan, Lecco, Como, and Varese, it engages in drug trafficking, money laundering through construction and nightclubs. The group has alliances with other 'ndrine such as Barbaro, Papalia, and Pelle-Vottari. It has been targeted in multiple operations including Wall Street (1993), Oversize (2001-2006), Annibale (2010), Metastasi (2014), and Cardine-Metal Money (2021).

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Carvelli
top_orgs_detail.description: The Carvelli are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta originating from Petilia Policastro, in the province of Crotone, Calabria. They have expanded operations to northern Italy, particularly Milan (Quarto Oggiaro) and Turin (Alto Canavese), where they established a 24-hour drug market. The group is involved in drug trafficking and has been linked to murders and vendettas. They are allied with the Trovato and Comberiati-Garofalo clans.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Comberiati
top_orgs_detail.description: The Comberiati are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Petilia Policastro, Italy. They are allied with the Coco-Trovato clan and have controlled neighborhoods in Milan, particularly Quarto Oggiaro. The clan was involved in the murder of witness Lea Garofalo in 2009. Key members include boss Vincenzo Comberiati, arrested and sentenced to 41-bis solitary confinement.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Caligiuri
top_orgs_detail.description: The Caligiuri are a 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta originating from Bagnara Calabra, operating in Petronà and Cirò Marina, with connections in Lazio, Lombardy, Germany, and the United States (Brooklyn, New York) with the Colombo and Gambino families. They are allied with the Farao-Marincola and Coco-Trovato clans. In the 1970s, a family feud against the Santoro of Cirò Marina began, leading to multiple murders and arrests for heroin trafficking.

title: Quebec Biker and Gang Alliance
summary: This community is centered on the Popeye Moto Club, a dominant Quebec biker gang that allied with other motorcycle clubs and organized crime groups. The Popeyes cooperated with the Dubois brothers, a powerful French-Canadian crime family, to fight rival gangs, and supplied drugs and weapons to the Gitans Moto Club. They also maintained alliances with the 13th Tribe and Sundowners Motorcycle Clubs. The McSween gang worked as associates of the Dubois brothers, tying them into the network. Overall, this community represents a web of Quebec-based biker gangs and criminal organizations that collaborated in drug trafficking, violence, and territorial control.

top_orgs_detail.name: Popeye Moto Club
top_orgs_detail.description: French-Canadian outlaw motorcycle club and criminal organization based in Quebec, founded in 1965. At its peak, it was the second-largest biker gang in Canada. Known for violence and enforcing a strict policy against other clubs' red-and-white color schemes. In 1977, the club was absorbed by the Hells Angels and became the first Canadian Hells Angels charter.

top_orgs_detail.name: Dubois brothers
top_orgs_detail.description: French-Canadian organized crime group consisting of nine brothers operating in Montreal from the 1950s to mid-1980s. They engaged in extortion, drug trafficking, loan sharking, and murder, rivaling the Cotroni family. The Quebec Crime Commission called them the most important criminal organization in Quebec.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gitans Moto Club
top_orgs_detail.description: French-Canadian outlaw motorcycle gang based in Sherbrooke, Quebec, founded in 1967. Originally a street gang called the Vikings, it evolved into the Dirty Reich MC before becoming the Gitans MC in 1970. The gang controlled drug trafficking in Sherbrooke and was known for violence, robberies, and sexual assaults. It integrated into the Hells Angels in 1984, forming the Sherbrooke charter.

title: US White Supremacist Militia Network
summary: This community consists of far-right, white supremacist and Christian Identity groups active in the United States during the 1980s. The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord (CSA) and The Order are central, with the latter funding other groups through armed robberies. The Order distributed proceeds to the National Alliance and the White Patriot Party, while the CSA maintained ties to Elohim City and the Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior. These organizations shared a goal of overthrowing the U.S. government and establishing a white homeland, cooperating through financial support and ideological alignment.
top_orgs: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior

top_orgs_detail.name: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
top_orgs_detail.description: A far-right survivalist anti-government militia advocating Christian Identity, active in the United States during the 1970s and early 1980s. It developed from a Baptist congregation founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri, and operated a compound in northern Arkansas known as 'the Farm'. The group was dissolved after a 1985 siege and subsequent convictions of its leaders.

top_orgs_detail.name: The Order
top_orgs_detail.description: Neo-Nazi organization active in the United States from September 1983 to December 1984. Founded by Robert Jay Mathews, it aimed to overthrow the U.S. government and establish a white homeland. The group raised funds through armed robbery and was responsible for the murder of radio host Alan Berg. Its leader died in a shootout with FBI agents.
