title: Asia-Pacific Drug Trafficking Network
summary: This community is centered on drug trafficking and money laundering across the Asia-Pacific region, with strong ties between Australian outlaw motorcycle gangs and Asian organized crime syndicates. The Comanchero Motorcycle Club and Sam Gor syndicate cooperate in drug distribution, while the Bamboo Union partners with Sam Gor for trafficking and money laundering. The Bamboo Union also works with the Iron Blood Patriots for overseas heroin trade. Local Australian gangs like DLASTHR and Bra Boys support the Comanchero with drug and weapons distribution and peacekeeping efforts. Together, these groups form a transnational criminal network focused on methamphetamine and heroin markets.

top_orgs_detail.name: Comanchero Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Comanchero Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle gang based in Australia, with chapters in Southeast Asia and New Zealand. Founded in 1968 by William George 'Jock' Ross in Sydney, the club is known for its violent history, including the 1984 Milperra massacre. It is involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities, and remains active with over 1,000 members as of 2019.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sam Gor
top_orgs_detail.description: Sam Gor is an international crime syndicate based in Asia-Pacific, primarily involved in drug trafficking. It is made up of members from five different triads and is headed by Chinese-Canadian Tse Chi Lop, who was arrested in 2021. The syndicate controls an estimated 40-70% of the Asia-Pacific methamphetamine market and earns at least $8 billion per year.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bamboo Union
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bamboo Union is the largest triad in Taiwan, founded in 1957 by waishengren children in Taipei. It has roughly 20,000 members and is involved in drug trafficking, contract killing, and extortion. The gang has historic ties to the Kuomintang and operates internationally in the United States, China, and other countries.

top_orgs_detail.name: DLASTHR
top_orgs_detail.description: DLASTHR is an Assyrian criminal organization active in southwestern Sydney, Australia, originating from the earlier Assyrian Kings gang. Founded in 1997, it is involved in drug trafficking, murder, and extortion. The group has been engaged in turf wars with rival gangs like the True Kings, leading to public shootings and firebombings. NSW police have conducted numerous operations targeting the gang.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bra Boys
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bra Boys are a gang centered on surf culture, founded and based in Maroubra, Sydney, Australia in the 1990s. They have gained notoriety through violence and alleged links to organized crime, as well as some community activism. The gang achieved national and international media attention in 2007 with the release of a documentary titled 'Bra Boys: Blood Is Thicker than Water'.

title: Colombian Paramilitary and State Allies
summary: This community centers on the Bloque Metro de las AUC, a Colombian paramilitary front that fought leftist guerrillas and engaged in drug trafficking. The group cooperated with state security forces—the Colombian Army, National Police, and DAS intelligence agency—as well as the private security group Convivir. It also subcontracted the criminal gang Banda de la Terraza for operations. These ties reveal a network of paramilitary, state, and private actors collaborating in counterinsurgency and illicit activities.
top_orgs: Servicios Especiales De Vigilancia y Seguridad Privada

top_orgs_detail.name: Bloque Metro de las AUC
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian paramilitary front operating primarily in Antioquia department, linked to the ACCU and later the AUC. Founded in 1996 by Carlos Mauricio García Fernández (alias 'Rodrigo Doble Cero'), it operated in 45 municipalities until its dissolution in 2004. The group fought leftist guerrillas and was involved in drug trafficking and counterinsurgency.

title: Italian Mafia Clans in Drug Trafficking
summary: This community consists of six Italian mafia clans, primarily from the Sacra Corona Unita or similar organized crime groups in Southern Italy. The central node is Clan Moretti-Pellegrino-Lanza, which cooperates with most others despite historical conflicts. Cooperation involves drug trafficking, extortion, and racketeering, often through shared funds or alliances. The network is characterized by both conflict and collaboration, typical of mafia dynamics where rivalries are set aside for mutual profit.

title: Neapolitan Camorra Alliance Network
summary: This community consists of the remnants of the Mariano clan, a Camorra group from Naples, allied with several smaller clans—Esposito, Elia, Lepre, Terracciano, and Montescuro—after Marco Mariano's release from prison. The Mariano clan, which fought wars for control of the Quartieri Spagnoli, serves as the central hub, cooperating with these allies to strengthen its position. The ties are primarily strategic alliances formed to consolidate power and territory in the Neapolitan underworld. The community reflects a post-conflict reorganization of Camorra factions.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Mariano
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian Camorra criminal organization originating from the Quartieri Spagnoli in Naples. Founded in the 1980s by brothers Salvatore, Ciro, and Marco Mariano, former associates of the Giuliano brothers. It fought wars with the Faiano clan and the Secondigliano Alliance for control of the Quartieri Spagnoli.

title: MZ/MF Faction and Allies
summary: This community is centered around the MZ/MF faction, a powerful criminal group, which has formed alliances with several smaller organizations including El Chapo Isidro, Gente de Lupe Tapia, Guasave Cartel, Los Cabrera, and Los Rusos. These groups cooperate primarily through formal alignments and military support, as seen in the early May 2025 alliance with El Chapo Isidro and the ongoing fighting alongside Los Rusos. The community appears to be a coalition of Sinaloa-based or affiliated groups united under the MZ/MF faction's leadership, likely for mutual protection and territorial control.

title: Oppido Mamertina 'Ndrangheta Alliance
summary: This community is a coalition of 'Ndrangheta clans based in Oppido Mamertina and the Gioia Tauro plain in Calabria, Italy. The core alliance consists of the Mammoliti, Ferraro, and Raccosta clans, who have cooperated in feuds against rival groups like the Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo clans. The Mammoliti clan is particularly powerful and closely linked to the Rugolo and Mazzafero clans through intermarriage and shared criminal activities. Their cooperation is driven by mutual defense and territorial control, with the Mammoliti clan providing support to the Ferraro-Raccosta faction during conflicts. This network exemplifies the familial and strategic ties that bind 'Ndrangheta clans in the region.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Mammoliti
top_orgs_detail.description: A powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Castellace and Oppido Mamertina in the Gioia Tauro plain, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1930s, it is involved in racketeering, kidnapping, extortion, and land expropriation. The clan is closely linked to the Rugolo clan through intermarriage and is considered one of the more powerful in the area. It has been active in feuds with rival clans such as the Barbaro 'ndrina.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Ferraro
top_orgs_detail.description: The Ferraro are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina, Italy. They are allied with the Raccosta family and part of the local 'locale' of Oppido Mamertina. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud with the Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo clans, resulting in over 30 deaths. In 2016, boss Giuseppe Ferraro was arrested after 18 years as a fugitive.

title: Bari Mafia Alliance Network
summary: This community consists of five criminal clans based in Bari, Italy, that are tightly interconnected through alliances and joint operations. The Parisi-Palermiti clan serves as a central hub, allied with the Capriati, Di Cosola, and Mercante-Diomede clans, while the Palermiti clan formally merged with Parisi in 2017. These groups cooperate primarily in drug trafficking, extortion, and gambling, controlling various neighborhoods of Bari. Their alliances reflect a strategy to consolidate power and reduce violent conflict, though law enforcement operations have weakened several clans. The community's cohesion is driven by shared territorial interests and mutual benefits in illicit markets.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Parisi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Parisi-Palermiti clan is a criminal organization based in Bari, Italy, with strongholds in the Japigia neighborhood. Founded in the 1980s by Savino Parisi and Eugenio Palermiti, it controls drug trafficking, extortion, and other illicit activities. The clan has infiltrated businesses and local politics, and has been involved in violent conflicts with rival groups. Despite the imprisonment of its founder, the organization remains active under the leadership of Eugenio Palermiti.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Capriati
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Capriati is a criminal organization from Bari, Italy, active since the 1980s. It is primarily involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and gambling management, operating mainly in Bari Vecchia and surrounding areas. Historically led by Antonio Capriati, the clan has seen women take significant roles after arrests of male leaders. In 2023, a major operation dismantled its hierarchical structure, arresting 60 people.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Di Cosola
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian criminal organization based in Bari, operating in the Carbonara, Ceglie del Campo, and Loseto neighborhoods. Founded in the 1980s by Antonio Di Cosola, known as 'Strascinacvert', along with Francesco Biancoli, Savino Parisi, and Giuseppe Mercante. The clan was involved in murders, drug trafficking, extortion, and video poker rackets. After the death of Antonio Di Cosola and law enforcement crackdowns, the clan is considered heavily weakened.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Mercante-Diomede
top_orgs_detail.description: Italian criminal organization based in Bari, founded by the Mercante and Diomede families. Active in drug trafficking and cigarette smuggling, operating in the Carrassi, Libertà, San Paolo, and Poggiofranco neighborhoods. Known for the 2000 San Valentino massacre targeting the rival Strisciuglio clan. Key members have been arrested and convicted.

title: Ciminà Feud Alliance Network
summary: This community is a tight-knit alliance of 'Ndrangheta families united by their involvement in the Ciminà feud, a violent conflict from 1966 to the 1980s that caused about 50 deaths. The core members—Varacalli, Franco, Polifroni, and Spagnolo—formed a coalition against common enemies, cooperating in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and extortion across Calabria and Turin. The network's dense ties reflect a historical pact of mutual support, with the Varacalli family acting as a central hub due to its high degree centrality. The alliance persists through ongoing criminal operations, as evidenced by recent arrests of Franco members.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Varacalli
top_orgs_detail.description: The Varacalli are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Ciminà, Italy. They operate in the Locride area and also in Turin, where they are active in drug trafficking. The family was involved in the Ciminà feud from 1966 to the 1980s, which resulted in about 50 deaths and extended to Turin.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Franco
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Franco is a 'ndrina (family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, operating mainly in Pellaro and Lazzaro. It is involved in drug and arms trafficking, contract rigging, and extortion rackets. The group is allied with the Barreca clan and has been active since at least the 1990s, with several members arrested in operations such as Marine and Chalo Nera.

title: Corleonesi Mafia Faction Network
summary: This community is centered on the Clan dei Corleonesi, a dominant faction within Cosa Nostra that rose to power during the Second Mafia War. The ties show that Salvatore Riina, leader of the Corleonesi, distributed extortion money to several Palermo-area families (Famiglia della Noce, Famiglia di San Lorenzo, Santa Maria di Gesù Family), indicating a hierarchical patronage system. The Greco clan of Ciaculli is allied with the Santa Maria di Gesù Family through Stefano Bontade, linking them to the broader Corleonesi sphere. These organizations are bound by shared criminal operations and loyalty to the Corleonesi leadership.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan dei Corleonesi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan dei Corleonesi was a faction within Cosa Nostra formed in the 1970s, led by mafiosi from Corleone such as Luciano Liggio, Salvatore Riina, Bernardo Provenzano, and Leoluca Bagarella. It included members from other provinces and opposed the Palermo faction during the Second Mafia War. Their victory led to a period of attacks against the Italian state in the 1990s.

title: Serbian Mafia and State Security
summary: This community centers on the Zemun Clan, a powerful Serbian mafia group, and its ties to state security and paramilitary organizations. The clan cooperated with the Security Information Agency (BIA) and had connections with UDBA, the former Yugoslav secret service. It also incorporated members of the Boinas rojas (Red Berets) paramilitary unit and initially operated under the Surčin clan. These ties highlight a nexus of organized crime, state security, and paramilitary groups in Serbia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Zemun Clan
top_orgs_detail.description: The Zemun Clan is a Serbian mafia group based in Zemun, a municipality of Belgrade. It rose to prominence between 2000 and 2003, becoming one of the most powerful criminal organizations in Southeast Europe. The clan was involved in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, extortion, and contract killings, and had connections with Serbian state security and paramilitary units. Its leader Dušan Spasojević was linked to the assassination of Prime Minister Zoran Đinđić in 2003, after which a major crackdown weakened the group.

title: Italian Mafia Clusters in Lombardy
summary: This community consists of Italian organized crime groups, primarily 'Ndrangheta and Camorra affiliates, operating in northern Italy. The 'Ndrina Fazzari, a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan from Taurianova, serves as a central hub, maintaining close ties with the Di Silvio and Spada families. Multiple arrests involving the Di Silvio, Spada, and Spinelli clans indicate joint criminal operations, likely including drug trafficking, extortion, and illegal waste disposal. The community's cooperation suggests a network of allied families sharing resources and territory in Lombardy and beyond.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Fazzari
top_orgs_detail.description: The Fazzari are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta originating from Taurianova, with operations in Lombardy, Piedmont, Lazio, Liguria, Australia, and France. They are involved in vote-buying, illegal waste disposal, kidnappings, and contract rigging. The family is closely allied with the Gullace clan and opposed to the Facchineri family. They have been targeted in anti-mafia operations including 'Saggezza'.

title: Indian Subcontinent Mafia Alliance
summary: This community consists of organized crime groups primarily from the Indian subcontinent, including the Indian mafia, Haji Mastan Gang, Karim Lala Gang, and Varadarajan Mudaliar Gang, along with the Canadian mafia. The groups are interconnected through alliances and cooperative criminal activities, such as dock cargo theft. The Indian mafia maintains ties with both the Canadian mafia and the Afghan mafia (Karim Lala Gang), indicating a transnational network. The cooperation appears to be based on mutual benefit and shared operations, likely involving drug trafficking, smuggling, and other illicit enterprises.

title: Camorra Alliance in Battipaglia
summary: This community centers on the Pecoraro-Renna clan, a Camorra group based in Battipaglia, Italy, which controls various criminal activities in the Piana del Sele area. The clan has formed alliances with the De Feo clan after a violent feud, and receives support from the Bisogni, Maiale, and Mogavero families. These ties indicate a network of cooperation among local Camorra organizations, likely for mutual benefit in drug trafficking, extortion, and other illicit enterprises. The community reflects the fragmented yet collaborative nature of Camorra clans in the region.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Pecoraro-Renna
top_orgs_detail.description: The Pecoraro-Renna clan is a Camorra organization based in Battipaglia, Italy, founded by Giovanni Pecoraro after his release from prison. It engaged in a violent feud with the De Feo clan in the 1980s and 1990s, later forming an alliance with them. The clan controls drug trafficking, extortion, usury, prostitution, and tax evasion in the Piana del Sele area.

title: Latin American Leftist Guerrilla Network
summary: This community centers on the 19th of April Movement (M-19), a Colombian urban guerrilla group, which lists four other Latin American leftist organizations as allies. These include Alfaro Vive, Carajo! from Ecuador, the Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN) from Nicaragua, Tupamaros from Uruguay, and the Túpac Amaru Revolutionary Movement (MRTA) from Peru. The ties are ideological, reflecting shared revolutionary nationalism and democratic socialism, and are documented in M-19's infobox. The community represents a network of Cold War-era guerrilla groups that cooperated through mutual recognition and possibly logistical support.

top_orgs_detail.name: 19th of April Movement
top_orgs_detail.description: Colombian urban guerrilla group active from 1974 to 1990, founded after alleged electoral fraud. It advocated revolutionary nationalism and democratic socialism, inspired by Tupamaros and Montoneros. At its peak, it was the second largest guerrilla group in Colombia with 1,500-2,000 members. It demobilized in 1990 and became a political party, later merging into the Independent Democratic Pole.

title: Fukuoka Yakuza Anti-Yamaguchi Alliance
summary: This community consists of four yakuza groups based in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan, all of which are members of the Yonsha-kai federation, an anti-Yamaguchi-gumi alliance. The Dojin-kai, Kudo-kai, Taishu-kai, and Kumamoto-kai are tightly interconnected through formal alliances and shared membership in the federation. These organizations are known for their militant nature and involvement in illicit activities such as methamphetamine trafficking. Their cooperation is driven by a common opposition to the dominant Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate, uniting them in a strategic partnership to counterbalance the larger group's influence.

top_orgs_detail.name: Dojin-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Dojin-kai is a yakuza organization based in Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan, founded in 1971 by Isoji Koga. It is a designated boryokudan group with approximately 310 members, known for its militant nature and involvement in large-scale methamphetamine trafficking, which is traditionally shunned in the yakuza world. The group has been involved in violent conflicts, including the Yama-Michi War against the Yamaguchi-gumi, and is a member of the anti-Yamaguchi Yonsha-kai federation.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kudo-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Kudo-kai is a yakuza group headquartered in Kitakyushu, Fukuoka, Japan, founded before WWII as a bakuto organization by Genji Kudo. It is the largest yakuza group in the Kitakyushu area, known for its extremely militant stance and use of machine guns and hand grenades. The group is a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Yonsha-kai and has been designated as a particularly nefarious group by the National Police Agency. In 2021, its boss Satoru Nomura became the first designated yakuza boss sentenced to death.

top_orgs_detail.name: Taishu-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Taishu-kai is a yakuza organization based in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan, with an estimated 60 active members. It was formed around 1954 by Kuniharu Ota as the Ota Group, later renamed Ota-gumi, and finally Taishu-kai in 1973. It is one of five independent Fukuoka-based designated yakuza syndicates and a member of the anti-Yamaguchi-gumi Yonsha-kai federation.

title: Cape Verdean Gangs and Faial Broker
summary: This community consists of three Cape Verdean street gangs—the Hendry Street Gang, Cape Verdean Outlaws, and Woodward Avenue Gang—along with the Faial family, which acts as a broker facilitating deals among them. The gangs have a mixed relationship, cooperating at times but also rivaling each other. The Faial family serves as a central mediator, enabling cooperation and reducing conflict. This structure suggests a networked criminal ecosystem where a broker family manages interactions between otherwise competitive street gangs.

title: Camorra Clans in Ercolano Area
summary: This community consists of four Camorra clans operating in the Ercolano and Torre del Greco areas of Italy, with Clan Ascione as the central hub. The clans cooperate primarily through territorial agreements for drug dealing and mutual defense against law enforcement. Clan Ascione has ties with Falanga for sharing drug territories, with Luongo for resisting arrests, and with Papale in a violent feud (faida). The community is characterized by local criminal dominance and inter-clan alliances for operational and survival purposes.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Ascione
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Ascione is a Camorra organization based in Ercolano, Italy. It emerged in the 1980s and became dominant after defeating the Esposito clan in a local feud. The clan is involved in extortion and other criminal activities. Its history includes violent conflicts and high-profile murders.

title: Puerto Rican Independence Militant Network
summary: This community consists of the Boricua Popular Army (Los Macheteros) and three allied organizations—FALN, FARP, and Volunteers—all of which collaborated with the Boricua Popular Army in its campaign for Puerto Rican independence from the United States. The ties indicate coordinated militant activities, including bombings and robberies, aimed at advancing the independence cause. The Boricua Popular Army serves as the central node, connecting these groups through shared operational partnerships. Despite limited descriptions of the other organizations, their cooperation with the Boricua Popular Army suggests a unified front within the Puerto Rican independence movement.

top_orgs_detail.name: Boricua Popular Army
top_orgs_detail.description: The Boricua Popular Army, also known as Los Macheteros, is a clandestine militant organization based in Puerto Rico that campaigns for Puerto Rican independence from the United States. Founded in the 1970s, it was led by Filiberto Ojeda Ríos until his death in 2005. The group has claimed responsibility for bombings, attacks on US military personnel, and a 1983 Wells Fargo robbery. Since 2005, it has operated as an activist group.

title: Corsican and Parisian Criminal Alliance
summary: This community brings together Corsican and Parisian criminal organizations, primarily the Gang de la Brise de Mer and the Hornec gang, along with their allies FLNC and Maghrebian gangs. The Gang de la Brise de Mer, a powerful Corsican group, cooperates with the Hornec gang, a Gitanes group controlling rackets in Paris, and is allied with the Corsican nationalist FLNC. The Hornec gang also maintains good connections with Maghrebian gangs, indicating a network that spans Corsica, Paris, and North African communities. Their cooperation likely involves drug trafficking, money laundering, and shared criminal enterprises across France and beyond.
top_orgs: Gang de la Brise de Mer

top_orgs_detail.name: Hornec gang
top_orgs_detail.description: A Gitanes criminal group from the Paris region, led by three brothers from Montreuil. It controls racketeering, drug dealing, and illegal slot machines in Paris, with ties to the Corsican mafia and Maghrebian gangs. The gang rose after succeeding Claude Genova in 1994 but declined after arrests in 2006.

top_orgs_detail.name: Gang de la Brise de Mer
top_orgs_detail.description: Corsican criminal organization founded in the 1970s, based in Northern Corsica. Engages in racketeering, drug trafficking, gambling, money laundering, and other illegal activities across France, Italy, Africa, and Latin America. Known for violent robberies and conflicts with rival gangs. Estimated capital of 120-150 million euros.

all_orgs: Gang de la Brise de Mer

title: Apulian Camorra Offshoots Network
summary: This community comprises criminal groups from Apulia, Italy, that were affiliated with the Nuova camorra pugliese (NCP) during the 1980s. The NCP, founded by Raffaele Cutolo, served as a central hub, with the Clan Cappellari-Caputo and Fratelli Modeo aligning with it as the Camorra expanded into Taranto. The Fratelli Modeo also cooperated with the Gruppo Annacondia, indicating a web of alliances among local clans. These ties reflect a period of consolidation and territorial control under the NCP's influence before its absorption into the Sacra corona unita.

top_orgs_detail.name: Nuova camorra pugliese
top_orgs_detail.description: The Nuova camorra pugliese was a criminal organization based in Apulia, Italy, active from the early 1970s to the mid-1980s. It was founded by Raffaele Cutolo as an offshoot of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata, operating mainly in the provinces of Foggia and Taranto. The organization was eventually absorbed by the Sacra corona unita.

title: Neapolitan Drug Trafficking Clans
summary: This community consists of four Neapolitan clans—Formicola, Giugliano (Rosario), Giugliano (Antonio), and Batti—that are tightly interconnected through drug trafficking partnerships. The ties are exclusively focused on narcotics, with each clan establishing bilateral relationships with the others. The network appears to be a cooperative alliance among smaller Camorra groups, likely operating in the Naples area, to pool resources and expand their drug trade operations.

title: D-Company and its Allies
summary: This community centers on D-Company, a major Indian organized crime syndicate, and its cooperation with other entities. D-Company has ties to the Dubai mafia, likely for logistical support and operations in the Middle East. It also collaborated with Pakistan's Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) in facilitating the 2008 Mumbai attacks, indicating a nexus between crime and terrorism. Additionally, D-Company is allied with the broader Mumbai underworld, as represented by Organised crime in India, suggesting shared criminal activities and networks. These connections highlight D-Company's role as a hub linking regional organized crime with international terrorism.

top_orgs_detail.name: D-Company
top_orgs_detail.description: D-Company is an organized crime syndicate founded and controlled by Dawood Ibrahim, based in India. It was established in the 1970s by Dawood Ibrahim and his brother Shabir Ibrahim Kaskar. The syndicate is involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, and terrorism, and has been designated as a Global Terrorist Organization by the U.S. Department of the Treasury. It remains active, with operations across Asia, the Middle East, and North America.

top_orgs_detail.name: Organised crime in India
top_orgs_detail.description: Organised crime in India encompasses various criminal groups operating primarily in Mumbai and other urban centers, involved in smuggling, extortion, drug trafficking, and contract killings. The Mumbai underworld has been dominated by figures like Haji Mastan, Varadarajan Mudaliar, and Karim Lala since the 1940s. Indian organized crime extends internationally to countries such as Canada, the United States, and the United Kingdom. The groups are loosely structured and not strictly modeled after the Sicilian Mafia.

title: Chicago Street Gang Alliances
summary: This community centers on the Taylor Street Jouster Nation, a Chicago street gang with Italian and Mexican membership active until 2005. The Jousters formed alliances with the Cragin Park Playboys, Freaks, and Ventures through various umbrella groups like PVJ, GFJ, and UFO. These ties reflect a network of cooperation among smaller gangs in Chicago, likely for mutual protection or territorial control. The community's structure suggests a hub-and-spoke model with the Jousters as the central node.

top_orgs_detail.name: Taylor Street Jouster Nation
top_orgs_detail.description: Chicago street gang originally based in Little Italy, founded in the 1950s or 1960s by Pete Bianco. Primarily Italian and Mexican membership, known for medieval knight symbols. Active until 2005, allied with the Chicago Outfit and Gaylords, rivaling several other gangs.

title: German Mhallami Mafia Networks
summary: This community consists of Mhallami crime families operating primarily in Germany, with the Al-Zein crime family as the central hub. The Al-Zein family has documented ties to the Miri clan, the Remmo clan, and La Familia, indicating a network of cooperation among these groups. Their activities include drug and arms trafficking, money laundering, and violent crimes. The ties suggest a loose confederation of clans sharing resources and personnel, likely for mutual benefit in criminal enterprises.

top_orgs_detail.name: Al-Zein crime family
top_orgs_detail.description: The Al-Zein crime family is a Mhallami mafia organization primarily based in Germany, with an estimated 15,000 members. It is involved in drug trafficking, armed robbery, arms trafficking, assault, extortion, fraud, money laundering, kidnapping, and murder. The clan originated from the Syrian-Turkish border region and migrated to Lebanon and later Germany. It is considered the second-largest criminal clan in North Rhine-Westphalia.

top_orgs_detail.name: Miri clan
top_orgs_detail.description: Organized crime cartel in Germany, primarily active in Bremen, Lower Saxony, Berlin, and North Rhine-Westphalia. Founded in the 1980s by members of the Mardelli family-group, refugees from Syria, Turkey, and Lebanon. Engages in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, protection rackets, money laundering, and human trafficking. Estimated 8,000-10,000 members nationwide, with Ibrahim Miri as a longtime leader.

title: Ndrangheta Clan Alliance in Lamezia Terme
summary: This community consists of four 'Ndrangheta clans—'Ndrina Torcasio, Cerra, Gualtieri, and Giorgi-Pizzata—that are tightly allied through mutual cooperation ties. The Torcasio clan serves as the central node, directly allied with each of the other three, while Cerra and Gualtieri also maintain a direct alliance. These clans are based in Lamezia Terme, Calabria, and their cooperation likely stems from shared interests in controlling local criminal activities and mediating feuds, as evidenced by the Andromeda operation's peace agreement. The alliance reflects a strategic consolidation of power within the 'Ndrangheta's regional structure.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Torcasio
top_orgs_detail.description: The 'Ndrina Torcasio is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Lamezia Terme. It is allied with the Gualtieri, Cerra, and Giorgi-Pizzata clans. The clan has been involved in the Lamezia Terme feud since 2000, resulting in dozens of murders. In 2015, the Andromeda operation revealed a peace agreement mediated by Reggio clans.

title: Seminara 'Ndrangheta Clans Alliance
summary: This community consists of four 'Ndrangheta clans based in Seminara, Calabria, Italy, with the Santaiti clan as the central hub. The Santaiti, Gioffrè, Brindisi, and Caia clans are allied through historical feuds and shared dominance in the town. Their cooperation involves joint criminal activities such as drug trafficking, arms smuggling, and infiltration of local government. The Santaiti and Gioffrè clans emerged as dominant after defeating the Pellegrino clan in the 1970s, and they continue to operate together. The ties are formalized as alliances listed in infoboxes, indicating a structured partnership within the 'Ndrangheta.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Santaiti
top_orgs_detail.description: The Santaiti are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Seminara, Calabria, Italy, also active in Liguria. They emerged as a dominant force in the 1970s after winning a feud against the Pellegrino clan. The clan is involved in various criminal activities and has been led by Carmine Demetrio Santaiti. They maintain alliances with other 'ndrine such as Brindisi and Caia.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Gioffrè
top_orgs_detail.description: The Gioffrè are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) based in Seminara, Calabria, Italy, involved in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, human trafficking, prostitution, radioactive waste disposal, and infiltration of local government. They emerged as dominant in Seminara alongside the Santaiti and Romeo clans, winning a feud against the Pellegrino clan in the 1970s. Internal feuds occurred in the 2000s, and several members were arrested in operations such as Artemisia.

title: Pacific Islander Crip Alliances
summary: This community consists of Crips-affiliated street gangs with Pacific Islander heritage, primarily Tongan and Samoan. The Tongan Crip Gang serves as the central node, allied with the Raymond Avenue Crips, Sons of Samoa, and Tongan Foe Lyfe Ganster Crip. These alliances are formalized through infobox listings, indicating mutual support in criminal activities such as drug trafficking, robbery, and extortion. The community spans multiple U.S. states and extends to New Zealand, reflecting a transnational network. Their cooperation strengthens their presence and operational capacity within the broader Crips alliance.

top_orgs_detail.name: Tongan Crip Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The Tongan Crip Gang (TCG) is a Tongan American street gang and a subset of the Crips alliance, founded in Inglewood, California. It is active in California, Utah, Alaska, Washington, and parts of Auckland, New Zealand. The gang engages in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, murder, and fraud. It was involved in a 2007 conspiracy to murder members of MS-13.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sons of Samoa
top_orgs_detail.description: Crips-affiliated street gang based in Long Beach, California, primarily composed of Samoan Americans and other Pacific Islanders. Founded by the 1980s, involved in drug trafficking, robbery, burglary, and extortion. Active in multiple U.S. states including Alaska, California, Missouri, Utah, and Washington.

title: Nuova Famiglia Allied Camorra Clans
summary: This community consists of Camorra clans that allied with the Nuova Famiglia coalition against Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata. Clan Matrone, based in Scafati, is the central hub, cooperating with the Aquino-Annunziata, Loreto, and Ridosso families. Their cooperation was primarily military and political, aimed at territorial control and dominance in the Camorra conflict. The ties are historical alliances formed during the 1980s Camorra wars.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Matrone
top_orgs_detail.description: Camorra organization based in Scafati, Italy, founded in the 1970s. It was a powerful criminal group that controlled the territory and engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, and violence. The clan aligned with the Nuova Famiglia against Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata. After the arrest of Francesco Matrone in 2012, the clan became largely disarticulated, though remnants attempted to reassert dominance.

title: Japanese Yakuza Splinter Groups
summary: This community consists of Japanese yakuza organizations that have splintered from the Yamaguchi-gumi, Japan's largest crime syndicate. The Yamaken-gumi and Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi were formed after a major split in 2015, with leaders Kunio Inoue and Kenichi Yamamoto joining the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. The Kyushu Seido-kai, a splinter from the Dojin-kai, maintained close ties with the Yamaken-gumi. The Toyukai, led by Osamu Teraoka, also aligned with the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. These groups are linked by historical affiliations, leadership connections, and shared involvement in organized crime activities such as drug trafficking and violent conflicts.

top_orgs_detail.name: Yamaken-gumi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Yamaken-gumi is a yakuza gang based in Kobe, Japan, founded in 1961 by Kenichi Yamamoto. It was the largest affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015, when it withdrew to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. In 2020 it became independent, and in 2021 it was welcomed back as the umbrella organization of the Yamaguchi-gumi. Membership dropped from an estimated 3,000-7,000 to around 800 after the split.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi
top_orgs_detail.description: The Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi is a yakuza organization based in Hyogo, Japan, formed in 2015 after breaking away from the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi. It was founded by Kunio Inoue and initially had 2,800 members, but membership declined to 140 by 2023 due to splits and disbandments. The group is designated as a crime syndicate by Japanese authorities and is involved in various criminal activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kyushu Seido-kai
top_orgs_detail.description: The Kyushu Seido-kai was a yakuza organization based in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan, formed in 2006 as a splinter group from the Dojin-kai. It engaged in violent armed conflicts with its former parent syndicate, using automatic firearms and grenades, and was involved in drug trafficking and loansharking. The group was designated as a yakuza group in 2008 and dissolved in June 2013, later reorganizing under new names.

title: Sicilian Mafia Clan Alliances
summary: This community consists of four Sicilian mafia clans—Savasta, Laudani, Pillera, and Ferlito—that are interconnected through a series of alliances. Clan Savasta serves as a central hub, having allied with the Ferlito-Pillera consortium and historically with Clan Pillera, while also seeking an alliance with the Laudani clan. These ties suggest a network of cooperation and mutual support among these criminal organizations, likely for shared illicit activities in Sicily.

title: IS Somalia and Allies Network
summary: This community centers on the Islamic State – Somalia Province (ISS), which cooperates with the Islamic State Yemen Province (IS-YP) as part of the broader IS network, and with the Hobyo-Haradhere Piracy Network for smuggling operations. The ties reflect ISS's reliance on regional IS affiliates for ideological and operational support, and on criminal networks for logistical capabilities such as smuggling. The community illustrates how IS affiliates in the Horn of Africa and Yemen collaborate, while also engaging with local pirate groups to sustain their activities.

top_orgs_detail.name: Islamic State – Somalia Province
top_orgs_detail.description: Islamic State affiliate operating primarily in the mountainous regions of Puntland, northern Somalia. Formed in late 2015 when pro-IS fighters defected from al-Shabaab under Abdul Qadir Mumin. Recognized by IS leadership as Somalia Province in December 2017. Engages in attacks against Puntland security forces and is in conflict with al-Shabaab. Estimated 100–700 fighters, with foreign fighters possibly outnumbering Somali members as of 2024.

title: 1947 Hollister Riot Biker Clubs
summary: This community consists of three early outlaw motorcycle clubs—Market Street Commandos, Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington, and Boozefighters—all of which participated in the infamous 1947 Hollister incident, a key event in biker history that inspired the film The Wild One. Their cooperation ties are based on their joint involvement in this riot, which marked a formative moment for outlaw motorcycle culture in the United States. The clubs share a common origin in the post-war biker scene, with Market Street Commandos later merging into the Hells Angels, while Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington remains active today. Despite their different trajectories, their shared participation in the Hollister riot defines their historical connection.

top_orgs_detail.name: Market Street Commandos
top_orgs_detail.description: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that participated in the 1947 Hollister incident, later immortalized in the film The Wild One. In 1954, the club merged with the Hells Angels to become their San Francisco chapter. The club is known for its role in early outlaw biker history.

top_orgs_detail.name: Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington
top_orgs_detail.description: An outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1945 in Bloomington, California by Norman Briggs. It participated in the 1947 Hollister riot that inspired the film The Wild One. The club is still active, with chapters in California, Nevada, and Utah. Its colors are red and blue, and its motto is PFFP.

title: 'Ndrangheta Clan Alliance Network
summary: This community consists of three 'Ndrangheta clans—Viola, 'Ndrina Zagari, and Maio-Cianci—that are tightly interconnected through alliances and cooperative activities. The Zagari clan, based in Taurianova, Italy, is known for its involvement in a violent feud, extortion, political corruption, and drug trafficking, and is explicitly allied with the Viola clan. Both Viola and Maio-Cianci are linked to the Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola group, as evidenced by joint asset seizures, indicating shared criminal enterprises. The network likely engages in coordinated drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering across Italy, with ties to Northern Italy and Rome.

top_orgs_detail.name: 'Ndrina Zagari
top_orgs_detail.description: The Zagari are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Taurianova, Italy, operating also in Northern Italy and Rome. They were involved in a violent feud with the Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi clans from the 1980s to the 1990s, resulting in 32 murders. The group is allied with the Viola clan and has been implicated in extortion, political corruption, and drug trafficking.

title: Maoist Revolutionary Network
summary: This community unites Shining Path, a Peruvian Maoist guerrilla group, with two international communist organizations: the Revolutionary Internationalist Movement and the International Communist League. The ties are ideological and political, as Shining Path was affiliated with both groups, which serve as coordinating bodies for Maoist revolutionary movements worldwide. Despite Shining Path's decline after its leader's capture, these connections reflect a shared commitment to armed struggle and the overthrow of capitalist states. The cooperation likely involves ideological support, propaganda, and possibly logistical coordination among like-minded militant groups.

top_orgs_detail.name: Shining Path
top_orgs_detail.description: Far-left political party and guerrilla group in Peru, founded in 1969 by Abimael Guzmán. It launched a 'people's war' in 1980 aiming to overthrow the government and establish a New Democracy through guerrilla warfare. Known for extreme brutality, including massacres and violence against peasants, trade unionists, and rivals. Designated as a terrorist organization by Peru, the US, EU, Japan, and Canada. Since Guzmán's capture in 1992, its activity has declined, with remaining factions active in the VRAEM region.

title: Ah Kong's International Drug Network
summary: This community centers on the Singaporean drug syndicate Ah Kong, which dominated the European heroin trade by forging strategic alliances with other criminal groups. Ah Kong's ties with the Malaysian triad Sio Sam Ong facilitated heroin sourcing from the Golden Triangle, while business dealings with the Yugoslavian crime syndicate expanded its distribution network in Europe. The cooperation reflects a transnational drug trafficking operation linking Southeast Asian producers to European markets.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ah Kong
top_orgs_detail.description: Ah Kong was a Singapore-originated organized crime and drug syndicate that controlled the European heroin trade from the 1970s to the 1990s. Based in Amsterdam and Bangkok, it was one of the world's largest drug syndicates, sourcing heroin from the Golden Triangle. The gang was founded by Singaporean Chinese members who fled to the Netherlands after a gang clash. It declined after its boss was assassinated in 1997 and the last official boss died in 2010.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sio Sam Ong
top_orgs_detail.description: A leading Chinese triad in Malaysia, based in Penang and Selangor, with strong presence in northern states like Kedah and Perlis. Established in the 1940s-1950s, it is considered one of the most powerful triads in the country, primarily composed of Hokkien ethnic group. The organization is involved in drug trafficking, kidnapping, murder, extortion, racketeering, and loansharking, and has been linked to multiple murders in Penang.

title: Allied Street Gangs
summary: This community consists of three street gangs—Satanas, Temple Street, and Avenue King Crips—united through alliances centered on Satanas. Satanas acts as a bridge, being allied with both Temple Street and Avenue King Crips, while the latter two have no direct tie. The cooperation likely involves mutual support in territorial disputes or criminal enterprises, forming a small but interconnected network. The lack of descriptions and countries suggests these are local gangs operating in the same region.

title: Foxtrot's Swedish Criminal Alliances
summary: This community centers on the Foxtrot network, a major Swedish drug trafficking organization led by Rawa Majid. Foxtrot cooperates with two smaller, lesser-known groups: Bro-network and Zero Network. The ties are described as alliances, suggesting mutual support in criminal activities, likely involving drug distribution and violence. Foxtrot's high degree centrality indicates it is the key node, coordinating with these allies to expand its operations in Sweden and beyond.

top_orgs_detail.name: Foxtrot
top_orgs_detail.description: Foxtrot is a Swedish criminal network that emerged at the end of the 2010s, led by Rawa Majid (the Kurdish Fox). It is Sweden's main distributor of narcotics and has been linked to shootings, bombings, and assaults. The network has developed direct operational ties with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security, and has been involved in attempted attacks on Israeli and Jewish targets in Europe.

title: Contra Funding Network
summary: This community centers on the Contras, a Nicaraguan rebel group, and two entities that supported them. The Enterprise was a covert network established to provide funding and supplies to the Contras, while Misura was an allied group that aligned closely with the Contras' main military wing, the FDN. These ties reflect a coordinated effort to sustain the Contra insurgency against the Sandinista government through financial and logistical support.

top_orgs_detail.name: Contras
top_orgs_detail.description: The Contras were anti-communist rebel groups that waged a guerrilla war against the Sandinista government in Nicaragua from 1979 to 1990. They were formed by dissidents and former National Guard members, and received military and financial aid from the United States and other countries. The Contras were known for human rights violations and terrorism against civilians. The insurgency ended with a negotiated demobilization in 1990.

title: Turkish Deep State Network
summary: This community centers on the Yüksekova Gang, a criminal organization in Turkey linked to state security forces. The gang cooperated with JİTEM, a clandestine unit of the Turkish Gendarmerie, and had possible ties to Ergenekon, a suspected deep-state organization. Their cooperation involved drug and weapons smuggling, with involvement from military and political figures. The ties were revealed after the Susurluk scandal, exposing state-criminal collusion.

top_orgs_detail.name: Yüksekova Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: An illegal organization based in Yüksekova, Hakkari Province, Turkey, accused of killing 16 people. It had apparent links to the Turkish Gendarmerie's JITEM unit and possible links to the Ergenekon organization. The gang smuggled drugs and weapons, and was headed by high-ranking military personnel and politicians. Its activities were revealed in 1996 after the Susurluk scandal.

title: Filipino Street Gang Alliance
summary: This community consists of three Filipino street gangs: Bahala Na Gang, Sigue Sigue Sputnik, and True Brown Style. Bahala Na Gang acts as a central hub, cooperating with both Sigue Sigue Sputnik and True Brown Style. The ties are based on shared criminal activities, particularly drug trafficking, as evidenced by a joint drug bust involving Bahala Na Gang and Sigue Sigue Sputnik. True Brown Style is listed as an ally of Bahala Na Gang, indicating a broader alliance network. These gangs operate primarily in the Philippines, with Bahala Na Gang also having a presence in the United States.

top_orgs_detail.name: Bahala Na Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The Bahala Na Gang (BNG) is a Filipino street gang founded in the early 1940s in Sampaloc, Manila, Philippines by Divino Talastas. It initially formed to counter the Sige-Sige Gang and later spread to the United States. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, weapons trafficking, and murder. It remains active with territory in the Philippines and the United States.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sigue Sigue Sputnik
top_orgs_detail.description: Filipino street gang based in Tondo, Manila, founded in the 1960s. One of the largest and most well-known gangs in the Philippines, with influence extending through slums and major penitentiaries. Known for elaborate tattoos and a strict code of conduct. Engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and murder.

title: Kosovo Liberation Army Allies
summary: This community centers on the Kosovo Liberation Army (KLA), an ethnic Albanian separatist militia, and its wartime associates. The KLA cooperated with the Abu Bekir Sidik mujahideen unit, though the relationship was fraught with tension, as the KLA may have led the unit into a trap. Additionally, the Albanian Army assisted the KLA by helping to establish a prison camp in Albania. These ties reflect the KLA's reliance on external support during the Kosovo conflict, despite potential mistrust among allies.

top_orgs_detail.name: Kosovo Liberation Army
top_orgs_detail.description: Ethnic Albanian separatist militia that sought Kosovo's secession from Yugoslavia during the 1990s. Founded on 17 November 1994, it engaged in armed conflict with Yugoslav forces and received NATO air support. Officially disbanded on 20 September 1999, with members transitioning to the Kosovo Protection Corps. Some former leaders later faced war crimes indictments.

title: Chinese American Criminal Alliances
summary: This community consists of three Chinese American organizations with historical ties to secret societies and criminal enterprises. Ping On, a Boston-based triad-linked group, was allied with both the Chee Kung Tong, a Chinese secret society, and Hung Mong, another criminal ally. These connections facilitated cooperation in illegal activities such as drug trafficking, extortion, and racketeering during the late 20th century. The alliances were likely based on shared ethnic ties and mutual benefit in criminal operations.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ping On
top_orgs_detail.description: Boston-based Chinese criminal organization founded in the 1970s by Stephen 'Sky Dragon' Tse, who had ties to the 14K Triad. It engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, fraud, prostitution, human trafficking, illegal gambling, money laundering, extortion, murder, illegal immigration, and racketeering. The organization declined after Tse's imprisonment in the 1980s and was linked to the Boston Chinatown massacre. It was active from the 1970s to the early 2000s.

top_orgs_detail.name: Chee Kung Tong
top_orgs_detail.description: The Chee Kung Tong is a Chinese secret society established in 1880, with roots possibly dating to 18th-century Fujian, China. It promotes Chinese values, customs, and democracy, and is known for supporting Sun Yat-sen's revolution against the Qing Dynasty. The organization has an active presence in the United States and Canada, with headquarters in San Francisco.

title: Outlaw Motorcycle Club Alliances
summary: This community centers on the Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club, a prominent outlaw motorcycle gang based in the United States. The club is allied with two other motorcycle clubs, Dahuru MC and Mixbreed MC, forming a network of cooperation among one-percenter biker gangs. These alliances likely facilitate mutual support in criminal activities, particularly drug trafficking, and strengthen their collective presence in the outlaw motorcycle club subculture.

top_orgs_detail.name: Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: The Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club is a mixed-race one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in Gary, Indiana in 1967. It is one of the oldest and most well-known black outlaw motorcycle clubs in the United States, with chapters across the U.S. and international presence. The club is involved in illicit drug sales and is regarded as a criminal organization by law enforcement.

title: Rio de Janeiro Drug Trafficking Alliance
summary: This community consists of two Brazilian criminal organizations, Terceiro Comando and Amigos dos Amigos, both based in Rio de Janeiro and involved in drug trafficking. They were initially allied, with Amigos dos Amigos founded by former Comando Vermelho members who joined forces with Terceiro Comando. Their cooperation was strategic, aimed at strengthening their position against the rival Comando Vermelho. However, the alliance was temporary, as Amigos dos Amigos later split and became independent, eventually controlling the Rocinha favela. Despite their eventual rivalry, the initial tie reflects a shared origin and common enemy.

top_orgs_detail.name: Terceiro Comando
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking in Rio de Janeiro, founded in the early 1980s as a breakaway faction of Comando Vermelho. It operated as a horizontal network without a sole leader, supporting member gangs in favelas. The group was active until 2006 and fought conflicts with Comando Vermelho.

top_orgs_detail.name: Amigos dos Amigos
top_orgs_detail.description: Brazilian criminal organization based in Rio de Janeiro, founded in 1998 by former Comando Vermelho members Celso Luís Rodrigues and Ernaldo Pinto de Medeiros. Initially allied with Terceiro Comando, it later split and gained control of the Rocinha favela. Engaged in drug trafficking and murders, with ongoing rivalry against Comando Vermelho and Terceiro Comando Puro.

title: Midwest Outlaw Motorcycle Clubs
summary: This community consists of two second-tier outlaw motorcycle clubs, the Galloping Goose Motorcycle Club and El Forastero Motorcycle Club, both based in the Midwestern United States. Their cooperation is rooted in shared criminal activities, including drug trafficking, as evidenced by their joint pooling of money to purchase drugs for runs. The clubs have close links and operate in overlapping territories across Iowa, Minnesota, Kansas, and Missouri. Their alliance strengthens their position relative to larger outlaw motorcycle gangs.

top_orgs_detail.name: Galloping Goose Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1942 in Los Angeles, California by Dick Hershberg. Originally an informal racing team, it became chartered in 1946 and expanded across the Midwestern US. Considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement, it has been involved in violent crimes and has support clubs.

top_orgs_detail.name: El Forastero Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: One-percenter motorcycle club founded in 1962 by Tom Fugle and Harlan 'Tiny' Brower in Sioux City, Iowa. Known for criminal activities including motorcycle theft and methamphetamine distribution. Has chapters in Iowa, Minnesota, Kansas, and Missouri, with close links to the Galloping Goose MC. Considered a second-tier outlaw club after the 'Big Four'.

title: Hells Angels Cocaine Network
summary: This community consists of two chapters of the Hells Angels Motorcycle Club: the London chapter and the Montreal chapter. Their cooperation is centered on drug trafficking, specifically the shipment of cocaine from Montreal to London. This tie reflects the international reach of the Hells Angels, leveraging cross-border connections to supply illicit drugs. The community is tightly knit, with both chapters sharing a common organizational identity and criminal enterprise.

title: Balkan Criminal Clan Alliance
summary: This community consists of the Kavač clan and the Šarić clan, two organized crime groups from the Balkans. The Kavač clan maintains close links with the remnants of the Šarić clan, indicating a cooperative relationship likely focused on drug trafficking or other illicit activities. Both groups are based in the same region and share operational ties, suggesting a strategic alliance for mutual benefit. Their cooperation underscores the interconnected nature of Balkan criminal networks.

title: Sureños Street Gang Alliance
summary: This community consists of two Sureños-affiliated street gangs: Barriox13 and Gardena 13. Both are based in Southern California and operate under the umbrella of the Mexican Mafia. Their cooperation is rooted in shared Sureños identity and mutual support in criminal activities such as drug trafficking and territorial control. The alliance strengthens their collective influence within the broader Sureños network.

top_orgs_detail.name: Barriox13
top_orgs_detail.description: Barriox13 is a highly active Sureños street gang based in South Los Angeles, founded in the early 1970s. It has over 5,000 members and is divided into West Side and East Side factions, all loyal to the Mexican Mafia. The gang is involved in narcotics, homicide, drug and human trafficking, and has expanded into Southern California and Mexico.

title: Jewish Sex Trafficking Networks in Argentina
summary: This community consists of two closely linked Jewish criminal organizations active in early 20th-century Argentina: Zwi Migdal and its splinter group Ashkenazum. Both specialized in sex trafficking, primarily of Eastern European Jewish women, and operated extensive networks of brothels in Buenos Aires. Ashkenazum was founded by a former Zwi Migdal member, Simon Rubinstein, and the two groups are listed as allies. Their cooperation likely involved sharing trafficking routes, protection, and resources, reflecting a common origin and similar criminal enterprises.

top_orgs_detail.name: Ashkenazum
top_orgs_detail.description: Organized crime group active in Argentina in the early 20th century, founded by Simon Rubinstein as a splinter from Zwi Migdal. Specialized in sex trafficking of Eastern European Jewish girls and silk smuggling. Operated in Buenos Aires with 700 agents at its peak.

top_orgs_detail.name: Zwi Migdal
top_orgs_detail.description: A Jewish criminal organization founded in Poland in the 1860s, based mainly in Argentina. It trafficked women from Central Europe into sexual slavery and forced prostitution, operating until 1939. At its height, it had 430 members overseeing 2,000 brothels and 4,000 women in Argentina alone.

title: Albanian-American Organized Crime in NYC
summary: This community consists of two Albanian-American criminal organizations based in New York City: the Rudaj Organization and the Albanian Boys. Both groups were active in the 1990s and early 2000s, engaging in extortion, illegal gambling, and violence. The Albanian Boys evolved from a street gang into subcontractors for organized crime, while the Rudaj Organization was a more structured syndicate. Their alliance likely involved cooperation in criminal enterprises such as debt collection and protection rackets, leveraging their shared ethnic ties and territorial proximity in the Bronx and Queens.

top_orgs_detail.name: Rudaj Organization
top_orgs_detail.description: The Rudaj Organization was an Albanian-American gang active in the New York City metro area, founded in 1993 by Alex Rudaj and Nardino Colotti. It engaged in extortion, illegal gambling, murder, and robbery, operating primarily in Queens, The Bronx, and Westchester County. The organization was dismantled after a 2004 federal indictment and its leaders were sentenced to prison.

top_orgs_detail.name: Albanian Boys
top_orgs_detail.description: The Albanian Boys were a gang of Albanians operating in central Bronx, founded in 1991. They evolved from a youth ethnic street gang to subcontractors for local organized crime, engaging in extortion, intimidation, protection money, and debt collection. The gang was active in the 1990s and had about 100 members.

title: Colombian Paramilitary Blocs Alliance
summary: This community consists of two Colombian paramilitary blocs, Bloque Centauros and Bloque Vencedores de Arauca, which cooperated against the rival ACC group. Their tie involves Bloque Vencedores de Arauca providing support to Miguel Arroyave's Bloque Centauros, indicating a strategic alliance in the context of Colombia's armed conflict. Both organizations are part of the broader paramilitary landscape, though their specific origins and operations are not detailed. The cooperation highlights temporary tactical alignments among armed groups in the region.

title: Colombian-Dominican Drug Trafficking Alliance
summary: This community consists of the Colombian crime cartel and Dominicans Don't Play (DDP), a Dominican-American street gang. Their cooperation is centered on cocaine trafficking, with DDP sourcing cocaine from the Colombian cartel for distribution in the United States. This partnership allows the Colombian cartel to access U.S. markets through DDP's established networks, while DDP secures a steady supply of cocaine. The tie is purely transactional and drug-focused, linking a major South American producer with a North American distributor.

top_orgs_detail.name: Dominicans Don't Play
top_orgs_detail.description: Dominicans Don't Play (DDP) is a Dominican-American street gang formed in Manhattan, New York, in 1991. Known for using machetes and knives, they are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang operates primarily in New York City, New Jersey, and other U.S. states, as well as internationally. They have a large share of the cocaine market through association with Colombian cartels.

title: Finnish Biker Gang Alliance
summary: This community consists of two outlaw motorcycle clubs, Cannonball MC and Overkill MC, both based in Finland. Their cooperation is rooted in a shared conflict during the Nordic Biker War, where they aligned against the Undertakers MC. Cannonball MC is a well-established organized crime group with over 200 members, while Overkill MC is a smaller ally. The tie between them is a strategic alliance formed for mutual defense and aggression in the biker war.

top_orgs_detail.name: Cannonball Motorcycle Club
top_orgs_detail.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Finland and Estonia, founded in Helsinki in 1991 by Jari Uotila. Designated as an organized crime group by the National Bureau of Investigation. Operates eleven chapters and a sub-division, Squad 32, with over 200 members. Briefly disbanded in 2017 due to internal dispute but resolved and continued operations.

title: Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala Formation
summary: This community consists of two organizations: Tío Balde and Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala. The tie between them indicates that Tío Balde joined forces with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG) to form the Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala. This suggests a merger or alliance aimed at expanding operations across the Chiapas-Guatemala border. The collaboration likely combines resources and territorial influence to strengthen their presence in the region.

title: South American Drug Trafficking
summary: This community consists of the South American cartels and the Tamarisco clan, linked by a single cooperation tie involving drug trafficking. The Tamarisco clan maintained a representative in South America to facilitate the trafficking of drugs, likely in partnership with the local cartels. This suggests a strategic alliance for the production and transportation of narcotics from South America to other regions. The connection underscores the transnational nature of organized crime in the drug trade.

title: Galveston Gang Rivalry and Cooperation
summary: This community consists of two rival gangs from Galveston, Texas, during Prohibition: the Beach Gang and the Downtown Gang. Despite being rivals, they occasionally cooperated on rum-running operations, indicating a pragmatic relationship for mutual profit. The Downtown Gang, founded by Johnny Jack Nounes, dominated organized crime on the island and had connections with the Chicago Outfit. The Beach Gang is less documented but engaged in similar illegal activities. Their cooperation highlights the fluid nature of alliances in organized crime, where competition and collaboration coexisted.

top_orgs_detail.name: Downtown Gang
top_orgs_detail.description: The Downtown Gang was a criminal organization based in Galveston, Texas, founded by Johnny Jack Nounes around 1920. It dominated organized crime on the island during Prohibition, engaging in bootlegging, gambling, and other illegal activities. The gang was a rival of the Beach Gang and had connections with the Chicago Outfit. It declined and disbanded around 1935.

title: ISIS Caucasus-Azerbaijan Network
summary: This community consists of two Islamic State affiliates in the Caucasus region: the Azerbaijan Province and the Caucasus Province. Their cooperation is rooted in recruitment, with the Azerbaijan Province drawing members from the Caucasus Province. This tie suggests a shared operational pipeline and ideological alignment, enabling the movement of fighters across borders. Despite limited data, the link indicates a coordinated effort to expand IS influence in the South Caucasus.

title: Lucchese Brooklyn Faction Allies
summary: This community consists of the Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction and the Robert's Lounge Crew, both part of the Italian-American Mafia. Their cooperation centers on hijacking operations, as evidenced by the tie between Paul Vario's crew (which merged into the Brooklyn faction) and James Burke's Robert's Lounge Crew. This alliance highlights the interconnected nature of organized crime crews within the Lucchese family's sphere of influence.

top_orgs_detail.name: Lucchese crime family Brooklyn faction
top_orgs_detail.description: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family, based in Brooklyn and Staten Island. It was formed in the early 2000s by merging the Vario Crew and the 19th Hole Crew. The faction is involved in racketeering, murder, and other illegal activities. It remains active as part of the larger Lucchese family.

title: Allied Criminal Clans
summary: This community consists of two criminal organizations, the Amirante clan and the Clan Ferraiuolo-Stolder, which are directly allied through a single cooperation tie. Both groups lack detailed descriptions and country information, but their alliance indicates a partnership likely for mutual benefit in illicit activities. The tie suggests a close operational relationship, possibly involving shared resources or joint criminal enterprises. The small size and direct connection imply a focused, bilateral cooperation.

title: Russian Mafia Leadership Ties
summary: This community consists of two prominent figures in the Russian mafia, Semion Mogilevich and Vyacheslav Ivankov, whose organizations are linked through business connections. Both are high-profile crime bosses with international reach, and their cooperation suggests a strategic alliance within the Russian criminal underworld. The tie indicates shared ventures or mutual support, likely involving money laundering, extortion, or other illicit activities. Despite limited information, the connection underscores the networked nature of organized crime among top echelons.

title: Camorra Clan Alliance in Campania
summary: This community consists of two Camorra clans operating in the Campania region of Italy. The Clan Magliulo, based in Afragola, was a powerful family involved in construction, drug trafficking, and control of shopping centers. The Esposito di Sessa Aurunca group, though less documented, maintained direct contacts with the Magliulo clan. Their cooperation likely involved joint criminal ventures such as drug trafficking or territorial control, reflecting the interconnected nature of Camorra networks in the area.

top_orgs_detail.name: Clan Magliulo
top_orgs_detail.description: The Clan Magliulo was a Camorra criminal organization based in Afragola, Italy, operating also in lower Lazio and Caserta. It was considered one of the most powerful Camorra families. Founded by Mario Magliulo, brother-in-law of Antonio Bardellino of the Casalesi clan, it was involved in construction contracts, control of shopping centers, drug trafficking, and a bloody feud with the Moccia clan. The clan suffered major asset seizures in 1990.
